<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/dict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"os"
10		"sort"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
16	)
17	
18	// suggestLimit caps the number of ranked "did you mean" candidates on a miss.
19	const suggestLimit = 7
20	
21	// DictConfig configures the dictionary engine.
22	type DictConfig struct {
23		Endpoint string // base, e.g. https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries
24		Lang     string // e.g. "en"
25		Fuzzy    bool
26		Wordlist string // path to a newline word list; "" => /usr/share/dict/words
27		Timeout  time.Duration
28	}
29	
30	// DictEngine looks up word definitions via the Free Dictionary API, with an
31	// exact match first and a local fuzzy (nearest-headword) fallback on 404.
32	type DictEngine struct {
33		cfg  DictConfig
34		wl   *wordIndex
35		http *http.Client
36	}
37	
38	// NewDict builds a dictionary engine.
39	func NewDict(cfg DictConfig) *DictEngine {
40		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
41			cfg.Endpoint = "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries"
42		}
43		if cfg.Lang == "" {
44			cfg.Lang = "en"
45		}
46		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
47			cfg.Timeout = 5 * time.Second
48		}
49		path := cfg.Wordlist
50		if path == "" {
51			path = "/usr/share/dict/words"
52		}
53		return &DictEngine{cfg: cfg, wl: &wordIndex{path: path}, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
54	}
55	
56	// Name returns "dictionary".
57	func (e *DictEngine) Name() string { return "dictionary" }
58	
59	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups only.
60	func (e *DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
61	
62	// Detect is not meaningful for the dictionary engine.
63	func (e *DictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) { return "", nil }
64	
65	// Available probes the API with a common word.
66	func (e *DictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
67		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
68		defer cancel()
69		_, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, "test")
70		return err == nil && status == http.StatusOK
71	}
72	
73	// Translate performs a dictionary lookup (exact, then fuzzy on 404).
74	func (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
75		word := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
76		if word == "" {
77			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
78		}
79	
80		entry, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, word)
81		if err == nil && status == http.StatusOK && entry != nil {
82			return single(e.result(entry, req), nil), nil
83		}
84		if err != nil {
85			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err)), nil
86		}
87	
88		// 404: return a ranked "did you mean" list (the frontend chooses one). Gated
89		// to English because the bundled wordlist (/usr/share/dict/words) is English.
90		if status == http.StatusNotFound && e.cfg.Fuzzy && strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(e.cfg.Lang), "en") {
91			if cands, best := e.wl.nearestN(word, 2, suggestLimit); len(cands) > 0 {
92				return single(&TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: e.Name(), Suggestions: cands, SuggestDistance: best}, nil), nil
93			}
94		}
95		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word)), nil
96	}
97	
98	func (e *DictEngine) result(entry *DictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
99		return &TranslateResult{
100			Translation: entry.gloss(),
101			Target:      req.Target,
102			Engine:      e.Name(),
103			Dictionary:  entry,
104		}
105	}
106	
107	// --- Free Dictionary API wire types ---
108	
109	type apiEntry struct {
110		Word       string        `json:"word"`
111		Phonetic   string        `json:"phonetic"`
112		Phonetics  []apiPhonetic `json:"phonetics"`
113		Meanings   []apiMeaning  `json:"meanings"`
114		SourceURLs []string      `json:"sourceUrls"`
115	}
116	type apiPhonetic struct {
117		Text string `json:"text"`
118	}
119	type apiMeaning struct {
120		PartOfSpeech string          `json:"partOfSpeech"`
121		Definitions  []apiDefinition `json:"definitions"`
122		Synonyms     []string        `json:"synonyms"`
123		Antonyms     []string        `json:"antonyms"`
124	}
125	type apiDefinition struct {
126		Definition string `json:"definition"`
127		Example    string `json:"example"`
128	}
129	
130	// fetch requests one word; returns (entry, httpStatus, err). A 404 yields
131	// (nil, 404, nil) so the caller can trigger the fuzzy fallback.
132	func (e *DictEngine) fetch(ctx context.Context, word string) (*DictEntry, int, error) {
133		u := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s", strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.Endpoint, "/"), e.cfg.Lang, urlPathEscape(word))
134		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
139		if err != nil {
140			return nil, 0, err
141		}
142		defer resp.Body.Close()
143		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {
144			_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
145			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
146		}
147		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
148			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
149			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))
150		}
151		var entries []apiEntry
152		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&entries); err != nil {
153			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("decode: %w", err)
154		}
155		if len(entries) == 0 {
156			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
157		}
158		return mergeEntries(entries), http.StatusOK, nil
159	}
160	
161	// mergeEntries folds the API's per-entry results into one DictEntry.
162	func mergeEntries(entries []apiEntry) *DictEntry {
163		out := &DictEntry{Word: entries[0].Word}
164		for _, en := range entries {
165			if out.Phonetic == "" && en.Phonetic != "" {
166				out.Phonetic = en.Phonetic
167			}
168			for _, p := range en.Phonetics {
169				if out.Phonetic == "" && p.Text != "" {
170					out.Phonetic = p.Text
171				}
172			}
173			for _, m := range en.Meanings {
174				meaning := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: m.PartOfSpeech, Synonyms: m.Synonyms, Antonyms: m.Antonyms}
175				for _, d := range m.Definitions {
176					meaning.Definitions = append(meaning.Definitions, Definition{Text: d.Definition, Example: d.Example})
177				}
178				out.Meanings = append(out.Meanings, meaning)
179			}
180			if out.SourceURL == "" && len(en.SourceURLs) > 0 {
181				out.SourceURL = en.SourceURLs[0]
182			}
183		}
184		return out
185	}
186	
187	// gloss returns a short one-line definition for history/plain display.
188	func (d *DictEntry) gloss() string {
189		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
190			for _, def := range m.Definitions {
191				if def.Text != "" {
192					return def.Text
193				}
194			}
195		}
196		return d.Word
197	}
198	
199	func urlPathEscape(s string) string {
200		// dictionary words are simple; escape spaces defensively.
201		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, " ", "%20")
202	}
203	
204	// --- local fuzzy word index ---
205	
206	type wordIndex struct {
207		path  string
208		once  sync.Once
209		words []string
210	}
211	
212	func (wi *wordIndex) load() {
213		wi.once.Do(func() {
214			b, err := os.ReadFile(wi.path)
215			if err != nil {
216				return // no wordlist: fuzzy silently disabled
217			}
218			for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
219				w := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
220				if w != "" {
221					wi.words = append(wi.words, w)
222				}
223			}
224		})
225	}
226	
227	// nearestN returns up to n headwords within maxDist edits of word, closest first
228	// (ties broken alphabetically), excluding word itself. The second return is the
229	// best (smallest) edit distance among the results, or 0 when there are none.
230	func (wi *wordIndex) nearestN(word string, maxDist, n int) ([]string, int) {
231		wi.load()
232		type cand struct {
233			w string
234			d int
235		}
236		var cs []cand
237		for _, w := range wi.words {
238			if w == word || abs(len(w)-len(word)) > maxDist {
239				continue
240			}
241			if d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(word, w); d <= maxDist {
242				cs = append(cs, cand{w, d})
243			}
244		}
245		sort.Slice(cs, func(i, j int) bool {
246			if cs[i].d != cs[j].d {
247				return cs[i].d < cs[j].d
248			}
249			return cs[i].w < cs[j].w
250		})
251		if len(cs) > n {
252			cs = cs[:n]
253		}
254		out := make([]string, len(cs))
255		for i, c := range cs {
256			out[i] = c.w
257		}
258		best := 0
259		if len(cs) > 0 {
260			best = cs[0].d // slice is sorted by distance ascending
261		}
262		return out, best
263	}
264	
265	func abs(n int) int {
266		if n < 0 {
267			return -n
268		}
269		return n
270	}
271
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backlog/api-server.md`

```text
1	# Local HTTP API server (`translate serve`) + OpenAPI/Swagger
2	
3	**Status**: P? — needs a spike
4	**Effort**: L
5	**Related**: `TODO.md` P? · `internal/engine/` · [raycast-extension.md](raycast-extension.md) · [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	2026-07, surfaced while building the Raycast extension. Today every client (the
10	Raycast extension, script commands) **spawns the `translate` binary per call**. A
11	persistent local HTTP server (`translate serve`) with an OpenAPI/Swagger page was
12	proposed as "more usable." This doc records whether it is, and how.
13	
14	A related idea — folding `just raycast-dev` into the binary as `translate raycast
15	dev` — is **rejected** here (option C): it couples the Go CLI to the Node/`ray`
16	toolchain and the extension's on-disk location. Dev-tooling orchestration stays in
17	the `Justfile`. The useful subcommand is `translate serve`, not a `ray develop` wrapper.
18	
19	## Investigation
20	
21	- **Latency win is smaller than it looks.** Per-call spawn cost = config load +
22	  sqlite dict open + engine init. But LLM latency (~3–4 s) dominates, so a warm
23	  server mainly speeds up the **local dict/define** path, *not* LLM translation.
24	- **Streaming is the real win.** An HTTP server controls its own flushing → SSE
25	  (`text/event-stream`); clients consume via `fetch`/EventSource. Cleaner than the
26	  TTY / `--stream` / spawn path. (Caveat: upstream copilot-proxy still buffers claude
27	  `/v1/messages`, so visible streaming stays limited there regardless of transport.)
28	- **Multi-client + docs.** One loopback service → Raycast, Alfred, a browser
29	  extension, editor plugins, `curl`, a web UI. OpenAPI/Swagger documents it for all.
30	- **Sidesteps launchd-PATH** for request-time — the extension hits
31	  `http://127.0.0.1:PORT` instead of resolving/spawning the binary. But the *first*
32	  "start the daemon" still needs a spawn/LaunchAgent (PATH problem just moves).
33	- **Cheap to build.** Reuses `internal/engine` (pure Go, context-aware, TUI-free) as
34	  a thin HTTP adapter. stdlib `net/http` suffices (no framework dep). OpenAPI can be a
35	  hand-written `/openapi.json` + `go:embed`-ded Swagger UI, or generated
36	  (`swaggo/swag`, `danielgtaylor/huma`). Endpoints: `POST /v1/translate`,
37	  `POST /v1/define`, `GET /v1/history[?q=]`, SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`.
38	
39	## Options considered
40	
41	| Option | What | Verdict |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| A. Keep spawn (current) | extension spawns `translate --json` / `--stream` per call | simplest, works, no daemon; per-call startup cost is small vs LLM latency |
44	| B. `translate serve` (HTTP + SSE + OpenAPI) | persistent loopback service; extension opt-in via a "server URL" pref, spawn fallback | best for streaming + multi-client + no-PATH; adds daemon lifecycle + security surface |
45	| C. `translate raycast dev` subcommand | binary shells out to npm/`ray` | rejected — couples Go CLI to Node toolchain + extension location; keep in Justfile |
46	
47	## Current blocker / open questions
48	
49	- **Daemon lifecycle**: how to keep it running — menu-bar app, a `LaunchAgent`, or the
50	  extension auto-starting it (which reintroduces the PATH problem for the first spawn)?
51	- **Security**: loopback-only bind is mandatory (history is personal data); consider a
52	  token for `/v1/history`.
53	- **Concurrent history writes**: server + CLI both appending `history.jsonl` — the
54	  store must be concurrency-safe (append + lock).
55	- **Port**: fixed default (collision risk) vs dynamic (needs discovery). Lean to a
56	  config'd default (e.g. `4155`) + preference.
57	- **Is it worth it vs spawn?** Only if we want a local translation *service* (streaming
58	  UX, multiple clients, web UI). The raw-latency argument is weak (LLM-dominated).
59	
60	## Decision (if any)
61	
62	`2026-07 deferred` — spawn mode works and ships. Revisit if we want a streaming-first
63	UX, multiple clients, or a web UI. If built: start minimal (stdlib `net/http`,
64	loopback, SSE, hand-written OpenAPI + embedded Swagger), extension **opt-in** with a
65	spawn fallback so non-server users are unaffected.
66	
67	## References
68	
69	- stdlib `net/http`; `go:embed` for the Swagger UI assets
70	- OpenAPI generators: https://github.com/swaggo/swag · https://github.com/danielgtaylor/huma
71	- Raycast integration + the streaming caveat: [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
72
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# translate — dev tasks (run `just` to list)
2	
3	# show available recipes
4	default:
5	    @just --list
6	
7	# build the binary into ./translate
8	build:
9	    go build -o translate .
10	
11	# run: `just run "hola mundo" --to en`  ·  `just run` for the TUI
12	run *ARGS:
13	    go run . {{ARGS}}
14	
15	# launch the interactive TUI
16	tui:
17	    go run .
18	
19	# dictionary lookup: `just define ephemeral`
20	define WORD:
21	    go run . define {{WORD}}
22	
23	# guided config wizard
24	init:
25	    go run . init
26	
27	# gofmt, vet, and build
28	check: fmt vet build
29	
30	# format the source tree
31	fmt:
32	    gofmt -w cmd internal main.go
33	
34	# static analysis (scoped to the Go tree; skips raycast/extension/node_modules)
35	vet:
36	    go vet . ./cmd/... ./internal/...
37	
38	# tidy go.mod / go.sum
39	tidy:
40	    go mod tidy
41	
42	# run tests
43	test:
44	    go test ./cmd/... ./internal/...
45	
46	# install into ~/.local/bin (first on PATH; override with DIR=…)
47	install DIR="~/.local/bin": build
48	    mkdir -p {{DIR}}
49	    install -m 0755 translate {{DIR}}/translate
50	
51	# remove build artifacts
52	clean:
53	    rm -f translate
54	
55	# make the Raycast script-commands executable + show how to add them
56	raycast-scripts:
57	    chmod +x raycast/script-commands/*.sh
58	    @echo "Add in Raycast → Settings → Extensions → Script Commands → Add Script Directory:"
59	    @echo "  {{justfile_directory()}}/raycast/script-commands"
60	
61	# run the TS extension in dev (registers it in Raycast; persists after you stop)
62	raycast-dev:
63	    cd raycast/extension && ([ -d node_modules ] || npm install) && npm run dev
64	
65	# type-check / build the extension bundle (does NOT install into Raycast)
66	raycast-build:
67	    cd raycast/extension && ([ -d node_modules ] || npm install) && npm run build
68	
69	# lint the extension with the Raycast eslint config
70	raycast-lint:
71	    cd raycast/extension && ([ -d node_modules ] || npm install) && npm run lint
72

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/config/config.go`

```text
1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
14	)
15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		// Version is the app version (git tag) that last wrote this file, and Schema is
19		// the config schema generation (see SchemaVersion). Both are stamped by Save;
20		// Version is informational, Schema drives the "your config is outdated" reminder.
21		// Declared first so they serialize above the [general] table.
22		Version string `toml:"version,omitempty"`
23		Schema  int    `toml:"schema"`
24	
25		General   General    `toml:"general"`
26		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
27		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
28		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
29		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
30		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
31		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
32		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
33		TTS       TTS        `toml:"tts"`
34		History   History    `toml:"history"`
35	}
36	
37	// SchemaVersion is the current config schema generation. Bump it whenever a new
38	// field or default is added that `translate init` would materialize, so an older
39	// on-disk config (schema < this) can be detected and the user reminded to re-init.
40	// It is NOT the app version (which is the git tag; see cmd/version.go). History:
41	//
42	//	1 — smart-auto default, pair-mode home anchoring, debug, TTS/learn fields.
43	const SchemaVersion = 1
44	
45	// Generator is the app version string (git tag) stamped into config.version on
46	// Save. The cmd layer sets it at startup; it stays "" for library/test callers,
47	// in which case the version key is simply omitted.
48	var Generator string
49	
50	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
51	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
52	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
53	type Overlay struct {
54		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
55		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
56		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
57		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
58		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
59		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
60		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
61		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
62		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
63		Learn         *bool   `toml:"learn,omitempty"`
64		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
65		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
66		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
67		Debug         *bool   `toml:"debug,omitempty"`
68	}
69	
70	// General holds behavior settings.
71	type General struct {
72		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
73		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
74		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
75		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
76		Learn             bool   `toml:"learn"`               // learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)
77		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
78		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
79		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
80		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | smartauto | llm | google | <provider>
81		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
82		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
83		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
84		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
85		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
86		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
87		Debug             bool   `toml:"debug"` // verbose decision logging (also --debug / TRANSLATE_DEBUG)
88	}
89	
90	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
91	type Chain struct {
92		Order []string `toml:"order"`
93	}
94	
95	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
96	type Provider struct {
97		Name      string `toml:"name"`
98		Type      string `toml:"type"` // openai | ollama | openrouter | litellm | generic
99		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
100		Model     string `toml:"model"`
101		ModelFast string `toml:"model_fast,omitempty"`
102		ModelMax  string `toml:"model_max,omitempty"`
103		APIKeyEnv string `toml:"api_key_env,omitempty"` // "" => no Authorization header
104	}
105	
106	// Google configures the free translate_a/single endpoint.
107	type Google struct {
108		Enabled   bool     `toml:"enabled"`
109		Endpoint  string   `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
110		ExtraDT   []string `toml:"extra_dt,omitempty"`
111		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
112		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
113	}
114	
115	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.
116	type Dict struct {
117		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`
118		Source   string `toml:"source"` // local (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT) | api (dictionaryapi.dev)
119		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
120		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
121		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`
122		Wordlist string `toml:"wordlist,omitempty"` // "" => /usr/share/dict/words if present
123	
124		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
125		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
126		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
127		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
128		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
129		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
130	}
131	
132	// TTS configures free, cross-platform text-to-speech (pronunciation playback).
133	// Backends are tried in Order: "native" (macOS say / Linux espeak / Windows SAPI,
134	// offline) then "google" (translate_tts MP3 + a discovered player).
135	type TTS struct {
136		Enabled       bool              `toml:"enabled"`
137		AutoSpeak     bool              `toml:"auto_speak"`        // CLI: speak every result without --speak
138		Order         []string          `toml:"order,omitempty"`   // e.g. ["native","google"]
139		Foreign       string            `toml:"foreign,omitempty"` // the 副/away language to speak; "" => derive
140		PreferForeign bool              `toml:"prefer_foreign"`    // auto-side selection prefers the foreign side
141		Rate          int               `toml:"rate,omitempty"`    // native words/min; 0 => backend default
142		Voices        map[string]string `toml:"voices,omitempty"`  // lang -> native voice override
143		GoogleTTSURL  string            `toml:"google_tts_url,omitempty"`
144		UserAgent     string            `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
145		TimeoutMs     int               `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
146		CacheDir      string            `toml:"cache_dir,omitempty"`
147		Player        string            `toml:"player,omitempty"` // forced audio player binary
148	}
149	
150	// History configures translation history storage.
151	type History struct {
152		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
153		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
154		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
155	}
156	
157	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
158	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
159	type SmartDict struct {
160		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
161		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
162		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
163		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
164		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
165		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
166	}
167	
168	// Recommended copilot-proxy model ids per tier. Claude models are served via the
169	// Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages); the LLM engine routes them there
170	// automatically (they 400 on /chat/completions).
171	const (
172		ModelDefault = "claude-sonnet-5"  // balanced
173		ModelFast    = "claude-haiku-4-5" // snappy — the out-of-box default tier
174		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
175	)
176	
177	// Default returns a config with sensible defaults, written on first run.
178	func Default() *Config {
179		return &Config{
180			General: General{
181				DefaultTarget:     "en",
182				DefaultSource:     "auto",
183				Pair:              false,
184				PairWith:          "en", // used when pair mode is enabled
185				RememberLastPair:  true,
186				LiveTranslate:     false, // off by default to avoid spamming LLM/API while typing
187				DebounceMs:        700,   // when live is on, wait longer before firing
188				Engine:            "auto",
189				Tier:              "fast", // haiku by default — snappy for short, quick translations
190				Preset:            "contextual",
191				AlternativesCount: 3,
192				Stream:            true,
193				Color:             "auto",
194			},
195			Chain: Chain{Order: []string{"copilot", "ollama", "google", "dict"}},
196			Providers: []Provider{
197				{
198					Name:      "copilot",
199					Type:      "openai",
200					BaseURL:   "http://localhost:4141/v1",
201					Model:     ModelDefault,
202					ModelFast: ModelFast,
203					ModelMax:  ModelMax,
204				},
205				{
206					Name:    "ollama",
207					Type:    "ollama",
208					BaseURL: "http://localhost:11434/v1",
209					Model:   "llama3.2:3b",
210				},
211				{
212					Name:      "openrouter",
213					Type:      "openrouter",
214					BaseURL:   "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
215					Model:     "anthropic/claude-sonnet-5",
216					APIKeyEnv: "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
217				},
218			},
219			Google: Google{
220				Enabled:   true,
221				Endpoint:  "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single",
222				ExtraDT:   []string{"bd", "at"},
223				UserAgent: "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli",
224				TimeoutMs: 4000,
225			},
226			Dict: Dict{
227				Enabled:      true,
228				Source:       "local", // CC-CEDICT (zh→en) + ECDICT (en→zh); run `translate dict update`
229				Endpoint:     "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries",
230				Lang:         "en",
231				Fuzzy:        true,
232				CedictURL:    "https://www.mdbg.net/chinese/export/cedict/cedict_1_0_ts_utf-8_mdbg.txt.gz",
233				EcdictURL:    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv",
234				AutoDownload: false, // explicit `dict update` is the blessed path
235				APIFallback:  true,  // English lookups still work before ECDICT is installed
236			},
237			SmartDict: SmartDict{
238				CloseDistance: 1,            // distance-1 typos stay "did you mean"; farther → LLM
239				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences
240				DefineDefault: true,         // `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
241			},
242			TTS: TTS{
243				Enabled:       true,
244				AutoSpeak:     false, // opt-in per call via --speak / TUI ^s
245				Order:         []string{"native", "google"},
246				Foreign:       "", // "" => derive the non-Chinese side to speak
247				PreferForeign: true,
248				GoogleTTSURL:  "https://translate.google.com/translate_tts",
249				UserAgent:     "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli",
250				TimeoutMs:     5000,
251			},
252			History: History{
253				Enabled: true,
254				Backend: "jsonl",
255			},
256		}
257	}
258	
259	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
260	func Path() string {
261		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
262			return p
263		}
264		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
265	}
266	
267	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
268	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
269	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
270		p := Path()
271		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
272		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
273			cfg = Default()
274			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
275				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
276			}
277			return cfg, true, nil
278		}
279		if err != nil {
280			return nil, false, err
281		}
282		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
283		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
284			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
285		}
286		return cfg, false, nil
287	}
288	
289	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename). It stamps the current
290	// schema generation and (when known) the writing app version, so a later build
291	// can detect an outdated config.
292	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
293		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
294			return err
295		}
296		cfg.Schema = SchemaVersion
297		if Generator != "" {
298			cfg.Version = Generator
299		}
300		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
301		if err != nil {
302			return err
303		}
304		p := Path()
305		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
306		if err != nil {
307			return err
308		}
309		tmpName := tmp.Name()
310		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
311		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
312			tmp.Close()
313			return err
314		}
315		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
316			return err
317		}
318		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
319	}
320	
321	// Outdated reports whether the loaded config predates the current schema, i.e.
322	// it was written by an older build (or before schema tracking existed, in which
323	// case the key is absent and reads as 0). The caller uses it to nudge a re-init.
324	func (c *Config) Outdated() bool { return c.Schema < SchemaVersion }
325	
326	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
327	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
328		for i := range c.Providers {
329			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
330				return &c.Providers[i]
331			}
332		}
333		return nil
334	}
335	
336	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
337	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
338	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
339		switch tier {
340		case "fast":
341			if p.ModelFast != "" {
342				return p.ModelFast
343			}
344		case "max":
345			if p.ModelMax != "" {
346				return p.ModelMax
347			}
348		}
349		return p.Model
350	}
351
```

 `./internal/store/store.go`

```text
1	// Package store persists translation history. v1 uses an append-only JSONL file
2	// with in-memory fuzzy search behind the Store interface; a sqlite+FTS5 backend
3	// can drop in later without changing callers.
4	package store
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// Record is one history entry (also the JSONL line shape).
12	type Record struct {
13		ID           string    `json:"id"`
14		TS           time.Time `json:"ts"`
15		SourceLang   string    `json:"source_lang"`
16		TargetLang   string    `json:"target_lang"`
17		Engine       string    `json:"engine"`
18		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
19		Input        string    `json:"input"`
20		Output       string    `json:"output"`
21		Alternatives []string  `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
22		Notes        string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
23		Favorite     bool      `json:"favorite,omitempty"`
24	}
25	
26	// Store is the history persistence interface.
27	type Store interface {
28		// Add assigns an ID and timestamp, persists the record, and returns it.
29		Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error)
30		// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
31		Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error)
32		// Search returns records fuzzy-matching query (over input+output), best first.
33		Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error)
34		// Close releases any resources.
35		Close() error
36	}
37
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/jsonl.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strconv"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/sahilm/fuzzy"
16	)
17	
18	// jsonlStore is an append-only JSONL-backed Store with in-memory fuzzy search.
19	type jsonlStore struct {
20		path string
21		mu   sync.Mutex
22	}
23	
24	// OpenJSONL opens (creating parent dirs for) a JSONL history file.
25	func OpenJSONL(path string) (Store, error) {
26		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o700); err != nil {
27			return nil, err
28		}
29		return &jsonlStore{path: path}, nil
30	}
31	
32	func (s *jsonlStore) Close() error { return nil }
33	
34	// Add stamps the record with an id + timestamp and appends it as one JSON line.
35	func (s *jsonlStore) Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error) {
36		if r.ID == "" {
37			r.ID = newID()
38		}
39		if r.TS.IsZero() {
40			r.TS = time.Now()
41		}
42		line, err := json.Marshal(r)
43		if err != nil {
44			return r, err
45		}
46	
47		s.mu.Lock()
48		defer s.mu.Unlock()
49		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o600)
50		if err != nil {
51			return r, err
52		}
53		defer f.Close()
54		if _, err := f.Write(append(line, '\n')); err != nil {
55			return r, err
56		}
57		return r, nil
58	}
59	
60	// readAll loads every record in chronological (file) order.
61	func (s *jsonlStore) readAll() ([]Record, error) {
62		s.mu.Lock()
63		defer s.mu.Unlock()
64		b, err := os.ReadFile(s.path)
65		if err != nil {
66			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
67				return nil, nil
68			}
69			return nil, err
70		}
71		var out []Record
72		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
73			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
74			if line == "" {
75				continue
76			}
77			var r Record
78			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &r) == nil {
79				out = append(out, r)
80			}
81		}
82		return out, nil
83	}
84	
85	// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
86	func (s *jsonlStore) Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
87		all, err := s.readAll()
88		if err != nil {
89			return nil, err
90		}
91		reverse(all)
92		if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
93			all = all[:limit]
94		}
95		return all, nil
96	}
97	
98	// Search fuzzy-ranks records by "input ⏎ output", best match first.
99	func (s *jsonlStore) Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
100		all, err := s.readAll()
101		if err != nil {
102			return nil, err
103		}
104		q := strings.TrimSpace(query)
105		if q == "" {
106			reverse(all)
107			if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
108				all = all[:limit]
109			}
110			return all, nil
111		}
112		hay := make([]string, len(all))
113		for i, r := range all {
114			hay[i] = r.Input + " " + r.Output
115		}
116		matches := fuzzy.Find(q, hay)
117		out := make([]Record, 0, len(matches))
118		for _, m := range matches {
119			out = append(out, all[m.Index])
120			if limit > 0 && len(out) >= limit {
121				break
122			}
123		}
124		return out, nil
125	}
126	
127	func reverse(rs []Record) {
128		for i, j := 0, len(rs)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
129			rs[i], rs[j] = rs[j], rs[i]
130		}
131	}
132	
133	// newID is a sortable-enough unique id: base36 nanos + 4 random bytes.
134	func newID() string {
135		var b [4]byte
136		_, _ = rand.Read(b[:])
137		return strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 36) + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
138	}
139
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/xdgpath/paths.go`

```text
1	// Package xdgpath resolves XDG-style config/data/state directories for translate.
2	//
3	// It deliberately does NOT use github.com/adrg/xdg or os.UserConfigDir(): both
4	// return ~/Library/Application Support on macOS, which breaks this tool's
5	// ~/.config convention. Instead it honors the XDG_* environment variables and
6	// otherwise falls back to the freedesktop defaults (~/.config, ~/.local/share,
7	// ~/.local/state) on macOS AND Linux alike.
8	package xdgpath
9	
10	import (
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13	)
14	
15	// app is the per-tool subdirectory used under each base dir.
16	const app = "translate"
17	
18	// base returns $env if set, else $HOME/def.
19	func base(env, def string) string {
20		if v := os.Getenv(env); v != "" {
21			return v
22		}
23		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
24		if err != nil {
25			home = "."
26		}
27		return filepath.Join(home, def)
28	}
29	
30	// ConfigDir returns the translate config directory (e.g. ~/.config/translate).
31	func ConfigDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CONFIG_HOME", ".config"), app) }
32	
33	// DataDir returns the translate data directory (e.g. ~/.local/share/translate).
34	func DataDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_DATA_HOME", ".local/share"), app) }
35	
36	// StateDir returns the translate state directory (e.g. ~/.local/state/translate).
37	func StateDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_STATE_HOME", ".local/state"), app) }
38	
39	// CacheDir returns the translate cache directory (e.g. ~/.cache/translate). Used
40	// for regenerable artifacts like TTS audio; it is intentionally NOT created by
41	// EnsureDirs (cache is optional and created lazily on first write).
42	func CacheDir() string { return filepath.Join(base("XDG_CACHE_HOME", ".cache"), app) }
43	
44	// ConfigFile returns the full path to config.toml.
45	func ConfigFile() string { return filepath.Join(ConfigDir(), "config.toml") }
46	
47	// StateFile returns the full path to state.json.
48	func StateFile() string { return filepath.Join(StateDir(), "state.json") }
49	
50	// HistoryFile returns the default history path (JSONL).
51	func HistoryFile() string { return filepath.Join(DataDir(), "history.jsonl") }
52	
53	// EnsureDirs creates the config, data, and state directories (0700) if missing.
54	func EnsureDirs() error {
55		for _, d := range []string{ConfigDir(), DataDir(), StateDir()} {
56			if err := os.MkdirAll(d, 0o700); err != nil {
57				return err
58			}
59		}
60		return nil
61	}
62
```
</details></tool-use>

1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"regexp"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// datedSuffix matches trailing dated model-id forms like "-20250514" or
9	// "@2025-05-14" that the copilot-proxy rejects.
10	var datedSuffix = regexp.MustCompile(`(-\d{8}|@\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})$`)
11	
12	// NormalizeModelID canonicalizes a model id for the copilot-proxy and other
13	// OpenAI-compatible backends:
14	//
15	//   - strips a trailing "[1m]" (or any "[...]") context-window hint, which is a
16	//     Claude-Code-only annotation the proxy rejects,
17	//   - drops a trailing dated suffix ("-YYYYMMDD" / "@YYYY-MM-DD"),
18	//   - trims surrounding whitespace.
19	//
20	// It leaves hyphenated ids like "claude-sonnet-5" and "gpt-5.4-mini" and Ollama
21	// ids like "llama3.2:3b" untouched.
22	func NormalizeModelID(id string) string {
23		id = strings.TrimSpace(id)
24		if i := strings.Index(id, "["); i >= 0 {
25			id = strings.TrimSpace(id[:i])
26		}
27		id = datedSuffix.ReplaceAllString(id, "")
28		return id
29	}
30	
31	// ModelRec is a recommended model surfaced by `init` and `--help`.
32	type ModelRec struct {
33		Role string // "default" | "fast" | "max" | "offline"
34		ID   string
35		Note string
36	}
37	
38	// Recommended is the built-in model recommendation table. Claude models route
39	// through the Anthropic Messages API automatically. Only models whose provider
40	// probes up are offered.
41	var Recommended = []ModelRec{
42		{Role: "fast", ID: "claude-haiku-4-5", Note: "snappy; default for quick translations"},
43		{Role: "default", ID: "claude-sonnet-5", Note: "balanced quality/speed"},
44		{Role: "max", ID: "claude-opus-4-8", Note: "highest quality"},
45		{Role: "offline", ID: "llama3.2:3b", Note: "Ollama, no network"},
46	}

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import "errors"
4	
5	// Sentinel errors returned across the engine layer.
6	var (
7		// ErrEmptyInput is returned when a request has no text to translate.
8		ErrEmptyInput = errors.New("engine: empty input")
9		// ErrNoResult is returned when a channel closed without a terminal result.
10		ErrNoResult = errors.New("engine: stream closed without a result")
11		// ErrNoEngineForMode is returned by the chain when no engine supports the mode.
12		ErrNoEngineForMode = errors.New("engine: no engine supports this mode")
13		// ErrAllEnginesFailed is returned by the chain when every candidate failed.
14		ErrAllEnginesFailed = errors.New("engine: all engines failed")
15		// ErrNoDictEntry is returned when a dictionary lookup finds nothing.
16		ErrNoDictEntry = errors.New("engine: no dictionary entry")
17	)
18
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/smartauto.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
10	)
11	
12	// SmartAutoEngine is the "smart" default: it treats a single word/term as a
13	// dictionary lookup (delegating to the smart-dict engine, which itself falls back
14	// to the LLM on a miss) and a phrase or sentence as an LLM translation. It is
15	// bidirectional-friendly — the caller sets Pair/PairHome/PairAway and the routed
16	// Target, and both branches honor them.
17	//
18	// Because dictionary word lookups are direction-agnostic (script routes them to
19	// CC-CEDICT vs ECDICT) and phrase translation uses the pair-aware LLM prompt, a
20	// single word like "test" is answered from the dictionary instead of risking an
21	// LLM echo, and a full sentence is translated into the other pair language.
22	type SmartAutoEngine struct {
23		smart Engine // dictionary + LLM fallback (serves ModeDict)
24		llm   Engine // LLM translate, possibly a fallback chain (serves ModeTranslate)
25	}
26	
27	// NewSmartAuto builds a smart-auto engine over a smart-dict engine (word lookups)
28	// and an LLM/translate engine (phrases).
29	func NewSmartAuto(smart, llm Engine) *SmartAutoEngine {
30		return &SmartAutoEngine{smart: smart, llm: llm}
31	}
32	
33	// Name returns "smart-auto".
34	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Name() string { return "smart-auto" }
35	
36	// Supports translate mode (the main-path request mode); it dispatches to a
37	// dictionary lookup internally when the input looks like a single word.
38	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
39	
40	// Detect is not meaningful here (the LLM/dict sub-engines own detection).
41	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
42		return "", nil
43	}
44	
45	// Available reports true when either branch can serve.
46	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
47		return e.smart.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
48	}
49	
50	// Translate routes a single word/term to the dictionary (smart-dict) and a phrase
51	// to the LLM. Streaming is preserved by returning the chosen sub-engine's channel.
52	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
53		if isLookup(req.Text) {
54			debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → dictionary lookup (smart-dict), target=%s", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target)
55			r := req
56			r.Mode = ModeDict
57			return e.smart.Translate(ctx, r)
58		}
59		debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → LLM translate, target=%s pair=%v", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target, req.Pair)
60		r := req
61		r.Mode = ModeTranslate
62		return e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
63	}
64	
65	// isLookup reports whether text should be treated as a dictionary lookup (a
66	// single word or short term) rather than a phrase to translate. Latin/other: one
67	// token of letters (plus '-' / apostrophe), up to 32 runes. CJK: a short run of
68	// Han without spaces (a word or idiom, up to 4 characters). Anything with
69	// internal whitespace is a phrase.
70	func isLookup(text string) bool {
71		t := strings.TrimSpace(text)
72		if t == "" {
73			return false
74		}
75		if strings.ContainsAny(t, " \t\n\r") {
76			return false // multiple tokens → phrase
77		}
78		if lang.IsChinese(t) {
79			return len([]rune(t)) <= 4
80		}
81		for _, r := range t {
82			if !unicode.IsLetter(r) && r != '-' && r != '\'' {
83				return false // digits/punctuation → not a plain word
84			}
85		}
86		return len([]rune(t)) <= 32
87	}
88	
89	// truncateText shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
90	func truncateText(s string) string {
91		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
92		r := []rune(s)
93		if len(r) > 40 {
94			return string(r[:40]) + "…"
95		}
96		return s
97	}
98
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/smartdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
10	)
11	
12	// SmartDictConfig parameterizes the smart-dict engine.
13	type SmartDictConfig struct {
14		// CloseDistance is the English edit distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
15		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and returned as-is; a farther match (and
16		// any hard miss) triggers the LLM fallback. 0 disables the typo shortcut, so
17		// every non-exact lookup falls back.
18		CloseDistance int
19		// Preset is the LLM prompt style used for the fallback; "" => PresetDictionary
20		// (a gloss plus example sentences).
21		Preset string
22	}
23	
24	// SmartDictEngine composes a dictionary engine with an LLM: it answers from the
25	// dictionary when it can, and falls back to the LLM when the word is missing or the
26	// fuzzy match is too far off. It is a distinct, selectable engine — the plain
27	// dictionary engine is left untouched.
28	//
29	// This is the first engine to branch on a produced result's quality (the Chain
30	// only fails over on hard errors), so the fallback always stamps a Warning: the
31	// downgrade from an offline lookup to an LLM answer is never silent.
32	type SmartDictEngine struct {
33		dict Engine
34		llm  Engine
35		cfg  SmartDictConfig
36	}
37	
38	// NewSmartDict builds a smart-dict engine over a dictionary and an LLM backend.
39	func NewSmartDict(dict, llm Engine, cfg SmartDictConfig) *SmartDictEngine {
40		return &SmartDictEngine{dict: dict, llm: llm, cfg: cfg}
41	}
42	
43	// Name returns "smart-dict".
44	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Name() string { return "smart-dict" }
45	
46	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups.
47	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
48	
49	// Detect is not meaningful for a dictionary engine.
50	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
51		return "", nil
52	}
53	
54	// Available reports true when either backend can serve (a dictionary hit, or the
55	// LLM fallback).
56	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
57		return e.dict.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
58	}
59	
60	// Translate looks the word up in the dictionary and, on a miss or a too-weak fuzzy
61	// match, falls back to the LLM (streaming preserved).
62	func (e *SmartDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
63		dictCh, err := e.dict.Translate(ctx, req)
64		if err != nil {
65			// A setup failure (e.g. empty input) — nothing to fall back to.
66			return nil, err
67		}
68	
69		out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
70		go func() {
71			defer close(out)
72			// The dictionary engines are single-shot (no token stream), so drain fully
73			// before deciding whether the answer is good enough.
74			res, derr := Drain(dictCh, nil)
75			if e.dictAnswered(res, derr) {
76				debug.Logf("smart-dict: %q answered from dictionary", strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
77				out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
78				return
79			}
80			debug.Logf("smart-dict: %q → LLM fallback (no usable dictionary entry)", strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
81			e.fallback(ctx, req, derr, out)
82		}()
83		return out, nil
84	}
85	
86	// dictAnswered reports whether the dictionary produced a result good enough to
87	// return without asking the LLM: an exact entry, or a fuzzy suggestion close enough
88	// to be a likely typo (within CloseDistance).
89	func (e *SmartDictEngine) dictAnswered(res *TranslateResult, derr error) bool {
90		if derr != nil || res == nil {
91			return false // hard miss (ErrNoDictEntry) or transport error → fall back
92		}
93		if res.Dictionary != nil {
94			return true // exact hit
95		}
96		// A close typo (distance known and within threshold) stays as "did you mean".
97		// Unknown distance (0, e.g. Chinese prefix matches) counts as too far → fall back.
98		if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 && res.SuggestDistance != 0 && res.SuggestDistance <= e.cfg.CloseDistance {
99			return true
100		}
101		return false // suggestions too far, "not installed" notes, or empty → fall back
102	}
103	
104	// fallback runs the LLM in translate mode and pipes its chunks to out, stamping a
105	// warning on the terminal result so the downgrade is visible.
106	func (e *SmartDictEngine) fallback(ctx context.Context, req Request, derr error, out chan<- Chunk) {
107		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
108	
109		r := req
110		r.Mode = ModeTranslate
111		r.Preset = e.cfg.Preset
112		if r.Preset == "" {
113			r.Preset = PresetDictionary
114		}
115		r.Target = smartTarget(req.Text, req.Target)
116	
117		llmCh, err := e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
118		if err != nil {
119			out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("smart-dict: no dictionary entry for %q and LLM fallback failed: %w", word, err)}
120			return
121		}
122		for ch := range llmCh {
123			if ch.Kind == ChunkDone && ch.Result != nil {
124				ch.Result.Warnings = append(ch.Result.Warnings,
125					fmt.Sprintf("no dictionary entry for %q — defined via %s (LLM)", word, ch.Result.Engine))
126			}
127			out <- ch
128		}
129	}
130	
131	// smartTarget picks the LLM fallback's target language, mirroring the bilingual
132	// dictionary (Chinese ↔ English): Chinese input → English; English/other input →
133	// the caller's target, or Chinese when that target is English/unset.
134	func smartTarget(text, target string) string {
135		if lang.IsChinese(text) {
136			return "en"
137		}
138		t := strings.TrimSpace(target)
139		if t == "" || t == "auto" || strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(t), "en") {
140			return "zh"
141		}
142		return t
143	}
144
```
</details></tool-use>
