<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/llm.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"bytes"
6		"context"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"sort"
13		"strconv"
14		"strings"
15		"time"
16	
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
18	)
19	
20	// LLMConfig parameterizes an OpenAI-compatible backend (copilot-proxy, Ollama,
21	// OpenRouter, LiteLLM, or a generic base_url+key endpoint).
22	type LLMConfig struct {
23		Name      string        // "copilot", "ollama", "openrouter", ...
24		BaseURL   string        // e.g. "http://localhost:4141/v1"
25		Model     string        // e.g. "claude-sonnet-5"
26		APIKeyEnv string        // env var holding the key; "" => no Authorization header
27		Timeout   time.Duration // per-request timeout (0 => 60s)
28	}
29	
30	// LLMEngine talks to any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint.
31	type LLMEngine struct {
32		cfg  LLMConfig
33		key  string
34		http *http.Client
35	}
36	
37	// NewLLM builds an LLM engine, resolving the API key once from the environment.
38	func NewLLM(cfg LLMConfig) *LLMEngine {
39		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
40			cfg.Timeout = 60 * time.Second
41		}
42		key := ""
43		if cfg.APIKeyEnv != "" {
44			key = strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv))
45		}
46		return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
47	}
48	
49	// Name returns the provider name (e.g. "copilot").
50	func (e *LLMEngine) Name() string { return e.cfg.Name }
51	
52	// Supports reports that LLM engines translate. (Dictionary lookups route to the
53	// dedicated dictionary engine in v1, so the chain never sends dict mode here.)
54	func (e *LLMEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
55	
56	// auth adds the bearer token only when a key is configured. copilot-proxy needs
57	// no Authorization header, so an empty APIKeyEnv yields no header at all.
58	func (e *LLMEngine) auth(r *http.Request) {
59		if e.key != "" {
60			r.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+e.key)
61		}
62	}
63	
64	// endpoint joins the base URL with a path segment.
65	func (e *LLMEngine) endpoint(path string) string {
66		return strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.BaseURL, "/") + path
67	}
68	
69	// Available is a cheap health probe: GET {BaseURL}/models with a short timeout.
70	func (e *LLMEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
71		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 800*time.Millisecond)
72		defer cancel()
73		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
74		if err != nil {
75			return false
76		}
77		e.auth(req)
78		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
79		if err != nil {
80			return false
81		}
82		defer resp.Body.Close()
83		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
84		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
85	}
86	
87	// Detect is a no-op for the LLM engine in this slice; offline detection is added
88	// with the free-API slice. Callers fall back to lang.Detect when this returns "".
89	func (e *LLMEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
90		return "", nil
91	}
92	
93	// --- OpenAI chat wire types ---
94	
95	type chatMessage struct {
96		Role    string `json:"role"`
97		Content string `json:"content"`
98	}
99	
100	type chatRequest struct {
101		Model    string        `json:"model"`
102		Messages []chatMessage `json:"messages"`
103		Stream   bool          `json:"stream"`
104	}
105	
106	type chatResponse struct {
107		Choices []struct {
108			Message      chatMessage `json:"message"`
109			FinishReason string      `json:"finish_reason"`
110		} `json:"choices"`
111		Error *struct {
112			Message string `json:"message"`
113		} `json:"error"`
114	}
115	
116	// streamDelta is one OpenAI SSE chunk (stream:true). FinishReason is non-nil
117	// only on the terminal chunk ("stop" on a clean finish, "length" on a cap hit).
118	type streamDelta struct {
119		Choices []struct {
120			Delta struct {
121				Content string `json:"content"`
122			} `json:"delta"`
123			FinishReason *string `json:"finish_reason"`
124		} `json:"choices"`
125	}
126	
127	// --- Anthropic Messages API wire types (/v1/messages) ---
128	
129	const anthropicMaxTokens = 4096
130	
131	// learnMaxTokens is a larger cap for learn mode: a gloss-rich structured JSON reply
132	// can exceed the terse-translation budget, and a truncated JSON body fails to parse.
133	const learnMaxTokens = 8192
134	
135	type anthropicRequest struct {
136		Model     string             `json:"model"`
137		MaxTokens int                `json:"max_tokens"`
138		System    string             `json:"system,omitempty"`
139		Messages  []anthropicMessage `json:"messages"`
140		Stream    bool               `json:"stream"`
141	}
142	
143	type anthropicMessage struct {
144		Role    string `json:"role"`
145		Content string `json:"content"`
146	}
147	
148	type anthropicResponse struct {
149		Content []struct {
150			Type string `json:"type"`
151			Text string `json:"text"`
152		} `json:"content"`
153		StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
154		Error      *struct {
155			Message string `json:"message"`
156		} `json:"error"`
157	}
158	
159	type anthropicStreamEvent struct {
160		Type  string `json:"type"`
161		Delta struct {
162			Type string `json:"type"`
163			Text string `json:"text"`
164			// StopReason is carried on the terminal "message_delta" event
165			// ("end_turn"/"stop_sequence" on a clean finish, "max_tokens" on a cap
166			// hit); empty on "text_delta" events.
167			StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
168		} `json:"delta"`
169		Error *struct {
170			Message string `json:"message"`
171		} `json:"error"`
172	}
173	
174	// isAnthropicModel reports whether a model id must use the Anthropic Messages API
175	// (/v1/messages) rather than /chat/completions. copilot-proxy serves Claude
176	// models ONLY via the messages endpoint — they return HTTP 400
177	// "model_not_supported" on /chat/completions despite being listed in /v1/models.
178	func isAnthropicModel(model string) bool {
179		return strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(model), "claude")
180	}
181	
182	// Translate performs a translation, streaming tokens when req.Stream is set.
183	// It always returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk, and
184	// dispatches to the Anthropic Messages API for Claude models.
185	func (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
186		// A bilingual doc request carries its content in Segments, not Text.
187		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" && !(req.Bilingual && len(req.Segments) > 0) {
188			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
189		}
190		model := NormalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)
191		// Apply a per-request model override only when it targets this provider, so a
192		// copilot model id never reaches an Ollama fallback (which would 404).
193		if req.Model != "" && (req.ModelProvider == "" || req.ModelProvider == e.cfg.Name) {
194			model = NormalizeModelID(req.Model)
195		}
196		if isAnthropicModel(model) {
197			return e.translateAnthropic(ctx, req, model)
198		}
199		return e.translateOpenAI(ctx, req, model)
200	}
201	
202	// ModelLister is implemented by engines that can enumerate their models.
203	type ModelLister interface {
204		Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)
205	}
206	
207	type modelsResponse struct {
208		Data []struct {
209			ID                 string   `json:"id"`
210			SupportedEndpoints []string `json:"supported_endpoints"`
211		} `json:"data"`
212	}
213	
214	// Models fetches the provider's model ids, keeping only those usable through the
215	// transports this engine speaks: OpenAI /chat/completions, or (for claude-*) the
216	// Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint. Ids needing only /responses are dropped.
217	func (e *LLMEngine) Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
218		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 4*time.Second)
219		defer cancel()
220		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
221		if err != nil {
222			return nil, err
223		}
224		e.auth(req)
225		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
226		if err != nil {
227			return nil, err
228		}
229		defer resp.Body.Close()
230		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
231			return nil, e.httpError(resp)
232		}
233		var mr modelsResponse
234		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&mr); err != nil {
235			return nil, err
236		}
237		var out []string
238		for _, m := range mr.Data {
239			if usableModel(m.ID, m.SupportedEndpoints) {
240				out = append(out, m.ID)
241			}
242		}
243		sort.Strings(out)
244		return out, nil
245	}
246	
247	// usableModel reports whether a model id can be driven by this engine.
248	func usableModel(id string, endpoints []string) bool {
249		if isAnthropicModel(id) {
250			return true // routed via /v1/messages
251		}
252		for _, ep := range endpoints {
253			if ep == "/chat/completions" {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		// No endpoint metadata (e.g. Ollama) => assume chat-usable.
258		return len(endpoints) == 0
259	}
260	
261	// finalize builds the terminal result from the accumulated translation text.
262	func (e *LLMEngine) finalize(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
263		res := &TranslateResult{
264			Translation: strings.TrimSpace(full),
265			Target:      req.Target,
266			Engine:      e.cfg.Name,
267			Model:       model,
268		}
269		// The plain-text prompt returns only the translation, so fill the detected
270		// source offline when the caller asked to auto-detect.
271		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
272			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
273		}
274		return res
275	}
276	
277	// promptFor picks the (system, user) prompt pair for a request: the structured
278	// tutor prompt for learn mode, else the plain translate prompt.
279	func promptFor(req Request) (system, user string) {
280		switch {
281		case req.Bilingual:
282			return buildBilingualPrompt(req)
283		case req.Learn:
284			return buildLearnPrompt(req)
285		default:
286			return buildTranslatePrompt(req)
287		}
288	}
289	
290	// finalizeResult builds the terminal result, parsing structured learn output when
291	// the request asked for it.
292	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeResult(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
293		switch {
294		case req.Bilingual:
295			return e.finalizeBilingual(full, model, req)
296		case req.Learn:
297			return e.finalizeLearn(full, model, req)
298		default:
299			return e.finalize(full, model, req)
300		}
301	}
302	
303	// finalizeLearn parses the model's structured JSON reply into a LearnResult. It is
304	// defensive — it strips a markdown fence and slices to the outermost object before
305	// unmarshalling — and on any failure falls back to the raw text as the translation
306	// (with a warning), so a malformed reply degrades gracefully instead of erroring.
307	// res.Target is set to the FOREIGN (away) language because res.Translation always
308	// holds the foreign sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation).
309	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeLearn(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
310		res := &TranslateResult{
311			Target: req.PairAway,
312			Engine: e.cfg.Name,
313			Model:  model,
314		}
315		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
316			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
317		}
318		lr, err := parseLearn(full)
319		if err != nil {
320			res.Translation = strings.TrimSpace(full)
321			res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings, "learn: could not parse structured output — showing the raw reply")
322			return res
323		}
324		lr.Direction = learnDirection(req) // trust offline detection over the model's guess
325		if strings.TrimSpace(lr.Original) == "" {
326			lr.Original = req.Text
327		}
328		res.Learn = lr
329		if lr.Direction == "correct" && strings.TrimSpace(lr.Corrected) != "" {
330			res.Translation = strings.TrimSpace(lr.Corrected)
331		} else {
332			res.Translation = strings.TrimSpace(lr.Translation)
333		}
334		return res
335	}
336	
337	// parseLearn extracts and unmarshals a LearnResult from a model reply, returning an
338	// error when the JSON is absent/invalid or carries no main sentence.
339	func parseLearn(full string) (*LearnResult, error) {
340		var lr LearnResult
341		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(extractJSON(full)), &lr); err != nil {
342			return nil, err
343		}
344		if strings.TrimSpace(lr.Translation) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(lr.Corrected) == "" {
345			return nil, fmt.Errorf("learn: empty structured result")
346		}
347		return &lr, nil
348	}
349	
350	// finalizeBilingual parses the model's JSON reply (prose-segment number → translation)
351	// into TranslateResult.Bilingual. Defensive like finalizeLearn: on any parse failure
352	// it returns an empty result + a warning so the caller can fall back to per-block mode.
353	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeBilingual(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
354		res := &TranslateResult{
355			Target: req.Target,
356			Engine: e.cfg.Name,
357			Model:  model,
358		}
359		m, err := parseBilingual(full)
360		if err != nil {
361			res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings, "bilingual: could not parse structured output")
362			return res
363		}
364		res.Bilingual = m
365		return res
366	}
367	
368	// parseBilingual extracts a {"<n>": "<translation>"} JSON object from a model reply
369	// and converts it to a map keyed by the integer segment number, ignoring non-numeric
370	// keys defensively.
371	func parseBilingual(full string) (map[int]string, error) {
372		var raw map[string]string
373		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(extractJSON(full)), &raw); err != nil {
374			return nil, err
375		}
376		out := make(map[int]string, len(raw))
377		for k, v := range raw {
378			n, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(k))
379			if err != nil {
380				continue
381			}
382			out[n] = v
383		}
384		if len(out) == 0 {
385			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bilingual: no numeric segment keys")
386		}
387		return out, nil
388	}
389	
390	// extractJSON returns the outermost JSON object in s, tolerating a surrounding
391	// markdown code fence or stray prose around it.
392	func extractJSON(s string) string {
393		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
394		if strings.HasPrefix(s, "```") { // strip ```json … ``` fence
395			if i := strings.IndexByte(s, '\n'); i >= 0 {
396				s = s[i+1:]
397			}
398			s = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(s), "```"))
399		}
400		start := strings.IndexByte(s, '{')
401		end := strings.LastIndexByte(s, '}')
402		if start >= 0 && end > start {
403			return s[start : end+1]
404		}
405		return s
406	}
407	
408	// markTruncated flags a result whose stream ended before the model finished, so
409	// the caller keeps the partial text but never treats it as a complete answer.
410	func markTruncated(res *TranslateResult) {
411		res.Truncated = true
412		res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings,
413			"output was cut off before completion (stream truncated) — press Enter to retry")
414	}
415	
416	// translateOpenAI uses the OpenAI /chat/completions endpoint.
417	func (e *LLMEngine) translateOpenAI(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
418		system, user := promptFor(req)
419		stream := req.Stream && !req.Learn && !req.Bilingual // structured JSON output: parse at done
420		body := chatRequest{
421			Model: model,
422			Messages: []chatMessage{
423				{Role: "system", Content: system},
424				{Role: "user", Content: user},
425			},
426			Stream: stream,
427		}
428		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
429		if err != nil {
430			return nil, err
431		}
432		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/chat/completions"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
433		if err != nil {
434			return nil, err
435		}
436		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
437		if stream {
438			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
439		}
440		e.auth(httpReq)
441	
442		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32) // buffered so a fast stream doesn't block on renders
443		go func() {
444			defer close(ch)
445			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
446			if err != nil {
447				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
448				return
449			}
450			defer resp.Body.Close()
451			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
452				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
453				return
454			}
455	
456			var full strings.Builder
457			complete := true
458			if stream {
459				var err error
460				complete, err = e.readSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
461				if err != nil {
462					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
463					return
464				}
465			} else {
466				var cr chatResponse
467				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&cr); err != nil {
468					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
469					return
470				}
471				if cr.Error != nil {
472					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, cr.Error.Message)}
473					return
474				}
475				if len(cr.Choices) == 0 {
476					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: empty response", e.cfg.Name)}
477					return
478				}
479				full.WriteString(cr.Choices[0].Message.Content)
480				complete = cr.Choices[0].FinishReason != "length" // "length" => hit the cap
481			}
482			res := e.finalizeResult(full.String(), model, req)
483			if !complete {
484				markTruncated(res)
485			}
486			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
487		}()
488		return ch, nil
489	}
490	
491	// translateAnthropic uses the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages).
492	func (e *LLMEngine) translateAnthropic(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
493		system, user := promptFor(req)
494		stream := req.Stream && !req.Learn && !req.Bilingual // structured JSON output: parse at done
495		maxTokens := anthropicMaxTokens
496		if req.Learn || req.Bilingual {
497			maxTokens = learnMaxTokens
498		}
499		body := anthropicRequest{
500			Model:     model,
501			MaxTokens: maxTokens,
502			System:    system,
503			Messages:  []anthropicMessage{{Role: "user", Content: user}},
504			Stream:    stream,
505		}
506		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
507		if err != nil {
508			return nil, err
509		}
510		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/messages"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
511		if err != nil {
512			return nil, err
513		}
514		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
515		httpReq.Header.Set("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
516		if stream {
517			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
518		}
519		e.anthropicAuth(httpReq)
520	
521		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32)
522		go func() {
523			defer close(ch)
524			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
525			if err != nil {
526				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
527				return
528			}
529			defer resp.Body.Close()
530			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
531				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
532				return
533			}
534	
535			var full strings.Builder
536			complete := true
537			if stream {
538				var err error
539				complete, err = e.readAnthropicSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
540				if err != nil {
541					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
542					return
543				}
544			} else {
545				var ar anthropicResponse
546				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&ar); err != nil {
547					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
548					return
549				}
550				if ar.Error != nil {
551					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ar.Error.Message)}
552					return
553				}
554				for _, c := range ar.Content {
555					if c.Type == "text" {
556						full.WriteString(c.Text)
557					}
558				}
559				complete = ar.StopReason != "max_tokens" // "max_tokens" => hit the cap
560			}
561			res := e.finalizeResult(full.String(), model, req)
562			if !complete {
563				markTruncated(res)
564			}
565			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
566		}()
567		return ch, nil
568	}
569	
570	// anthropicAuth sets the Anthropic auth header (x-api-key, not Bearer). The
571	// copilot-proxy needs none, so an empty key yields no header.
572	func (e *LLMEngine) anthropicAuth(r *http.Request) {
573		if e.key != "" {
574			r.Header.Set("x-api-key", e.key)
575		}
576	}
577	
578	// readSSE parses an OpenAI-style event stream, emitting a ChunkToken per content
579	// delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports complete=true only
580	// when the stream ended on a terminal marker ([DONE] or finish_reason=="stop");
581	// a stream that closes with no marker, or on finish_reason=="length", is treated
582	// as truncated so the caller can flag it rather than silently accept partial text.
583	func (e *LLMEngine) readSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
584		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
585		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20) // tolerate long SSE lines
586		sawDone := false
587		finish := ""
588		for sc.Scan() {
589			line := sc.Text()
590			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
591				continue
592			}
593			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
594			if payload == "[DONE]" {
595				sawDone = true
596				break
597			}
598			var d streamDelta
599			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil || len(d.Choices) == 0 {
600				continue
601			}
602			if fr := d.Choices[0].FinishReason; fr != nil && *fr != "" {
603				finish = *fr
604			}
605			tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content
606			if tok == "" {
607				continue
608			}
609			full.WriteString(tok)
610			select {
611			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:
612			case <-ctx.Done():
613				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
614			}
615		}
616		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
617			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
618		}
619		complete := finish != "length" && (sawDone || finish == "stop")
620		return complete, nil
621	}
622	
623	// readAnthropicSSE parses an Anthropic Messages event stream, emitting a
624	// ChunkToken per text_delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports
625	// complete=true only when a terminal marker arrived (a message_stop event, or a
626	// message_delta with a natural stop_reason); a stream that closes with neither,
627	// or on stop_reason=="max_tokens", is treated as truncated.
628	func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
629		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
630		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
631		sawStop := false
632		stopReason := ""
633		for sc.Scan() {
634			line := sc.Text()
635			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
636				continue // ignore "event:" and blank lines
637			}
638			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
639			if payload == "" {
640				continue
641			}
642			var ev anthropicStreamEvent
643			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &ev) != nil {
644				continue
645			}
646			if ev.Error != nil {
647				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ev.Error.Message)
648			}
649			switch ev.Type {
650			case "message_stop":
651				sawStop = true
652				continue
653			case "message_delta":
654				if ev.Delta.StopReason != "" {
655					stopReason = ev.Delta.StopReason
656				}
657				continue
658			}
659			if ev.Type != "content_block_delta" || ev.Delta.Type != "text_delta" || ev.Delta.Text == "" {
660				continue
661			}
662			full.WriteString(ev.Delta.Text)
663			select {
664			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: ev.Delta.Text}:
665			case <-ctx.Done():
666				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
667			}
668		}
669		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
670			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
671		}
672		complete := stopReason != "max_tokens" &&
673			(sawStop || stopReason == "end_turn" || stopReason == "stop_sequence")
674		return complete, nil
675	}
676	
677	// httpError reads an error body (best effort) into a descriptive error.
678	func (e *LLMEngine) httpError(resp *http.Response) error {
679		b, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 2<<10))
680		msg := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
681		if msg == "" {
682			msg = resp.Status
683		}
684		return fmt.Errorf("%s: http %d: %s", e.cfg.Name, resp.StatusCode, msg)
685	}
686
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:02:28Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/engine.go`

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	
46		// Learn marks a language-learning request. It is bidirectional (implies Pair,
47		// with PairHome=native and PairAway=foreign) and adaptive: native-language input
48		// is translated-and-taught (glosses + examples), foreign-language input is
49		// grammar-corrected-and-explained. The LLM engine answers with a strict JSON
50		// object (parsed into TranslateResult.Learn) and, because the output is
51		// structured, always runs NON-STREAMING regardless of Stream.
52		Learn bool
53	
54		// Bilingual marks a whole-document bilingual ("doc mode") request: Segments
55		// carries the full ordered document (prose to translate + code shown only as
56		// context) and the LLM returns a JSON map of prose-segment number → translation.
57		// Context-aware and single-call. LLM engines only (google/dict ignore it), and
58		// like Learn it always runs NON-STREAMING.
59		Bilingual bool
60		Segments  []Segment
61	}
62	
63	// Segment is one unit of a Bilingual document request: a prose block to translate,
64	// or a command/code block included only as surrounding context (Code == true).
65	type Segment struct {
66		Text string
67		Code bool
68	}
69	
70	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
71	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
72	type TranslateResult struct {
73		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
74		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
75		Target         string   `json:"target"`
76		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
77		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
78		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
79	
80		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
81		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
82		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
83	
84		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
85		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
86		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
87		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
88		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
89	
90		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
91		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
92		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
93	
94		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
95		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
96		// Learn payload (Request.Learn); nil outside learn mode. Translation still holds
97		// the main foreign-language sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation)
98		// so copy/history/speak keep working unchanged; the structured extras ride here.
99		Learn *LearnResult `json:"learn,omitempty"`
100		// Bilingual is the doc-mode payload: prose-segment number (1-based, as numbered
101		// in the prompt) → translation. nil outside bilingual doc mode. Transient.
102		Bilingual map[int]string `json:"-"`
103		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
104		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
105		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
106		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
107	
108		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
109		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
110		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
111		// Transient: not serialized.
112		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
113	}
114	
115	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
116	type DictEntry struct {
117		Word      string    `json:"word"`
118		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
119		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
120		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
121	}
122	
123	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
124	type Meaning struct {
125		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
126		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
127		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
128		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
129	}
130	
131	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
132	type Definition struct {
133		Text    string `json:"definition"`
134		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
135	}
136	
137	// LearnResult is the structured payload for learn/tutor mode (Request.Learn). The
138	// model fills only the fields relevant to the auto-detected direction; all glosses,
139	// explanations, and notes are written in the native (home) language.
140	type LearnResult struct {
141		Direction   string         `json:"direction"`           // "teach" (native→foreign) | "correct" (foreign→native)
142		Original    string         `json:"original"`            // the user's input as received
143		Corrected   string         `json:"corrected,omitempty"` // correct-direction: the fixed foreign sentence
144		Translation string         `json:"translation"`         // teach: foreign translation; correct: native translation of intent
145		Notes       string         `json:"notes,omitempty"`     // short tip/encouragement, in the NATIVE language
146		Issues      []LearnIssue   `json:"issues,omitempty"`    // correct-direction grammar/usage fixes
147		Vocab       []LearnGloss   `json:"vocab,omitempty"`     // teach-direction glosses
148		Examples    []LearnExample `json:"examples,omitempty"`  // teach-direction usage examples
149	}
150	
151	// LearnIssue is one grammar/usage correction (correct-direction).
152	type LearnIssue struct {
153		Span        string `json:"span"`        // the problematic fragment of the input
154		Fix         string `json:"fix"`         // the corrected fragment
155		Explanation string `json:"explanation"` // why — in the NATIVE language
156	}
157	
158	// LearnGloss is one vocabulary explanation (teach-direction).
159	type LearnGloss struct {
160		Term     string `json:"term"`               // foreign word/term
161		Pos      string `json:"pos,omitempty"`      // part of speech
162		Phonetic string `json:"phonetic,omitempty"` // KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese
163		Meaning  string `json:"meaning"`            // meaning in the NATIVE language
164	}
165	
166	// LearnExample is one usage example (teach-direction).
167	type LearnExample struct {
168		Foreign string `json:"foreign"` // example sentence in the foreign language
169		Native  string `json:"native"`  // its native-language translation
170	}
171	
172	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
173	type ChunkKind int
174	
175	const (
176		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
177		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
178		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
179		ChunkDone
180		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
181		ChunkError
182	)
183	
184	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
185	type Chunk struct {
186		Kind   ChunkKind
187		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
188		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
189		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
190	}
191	
192	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
193	//
194	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
195	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
196	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
197	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
198	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
199	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
200	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
201	type Engine interface {
202		Name() string
203		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
204		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
205		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
206		Supports(m Mode) bool
207	}
208	
209	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
210	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
211	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
212		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
213		if err != nil {
214			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
215		} else {
216			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
217		}
218		close(ch)
219		return ch
220	}
221	
222	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
223	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
224	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
225	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
226		for c := range ch {
227			switch c.Kind {
228			case ChunkToken:
229				if onToken != nil {
230					onToken(c.Text)
231				}
232			case ChunkDone:
233				return c.Result, nil
234			case ChunkError:
235				return nil, c.Err
236			}
237		}
238		return nil, ErrNoResult
239	}
240
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/dict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"os"
10		"sort"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/agnivade/levenshtein"
16	)
17	
18	// suggestLimit caps the number of ranked "did you mean" candidates on a miss.
19	const suggestLimit = 7
20	
21	// DictConfig configures the dictionary engine.
22	type DictConfig struct {
23		Endpoint string // base, e.g. https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries
24		Lang     string // e.g. "en"
25		Fuzzy    bool
26		Wordlist string // path to a newline word list; "" => /usr/share/dict/words
27		Timeout  time.Duration
28	}
29	
30	// DictEngine looks up word definitions via the Free Dictionary API, with an
31	// exact match first and a local fuzzy (nearest-headword) fallback on 404.
32	type DictEngine struct {
33		cfg  DictConfig
34		wl   *wordIndex
35		http *http.Client
36	}
37	
38	// NewDict builds a dictionary engine.
39	func NewDict(cfg DictConfig) *DictEngine {
40		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
41			cfg.Endpoint = "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries"
42		}
43		if cfg.Lang == "" {
44			cfg.Lang = "en"
45		}
46		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
47			cfg.Timeout = 5 * time.Second
48		}
49		path := cfg.Wordlist
50		if path == "" {
51			path = "/usr/share/dict/words"
52		}
53		return &DictEngine{cfg: cfg, wl: &wordIndex{path: path}, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
54	}
55	
56	// Name returns "dictionary".
57	func (e *DictEngine) Name() string { return "dictionary" }
58	
59	// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups only.
60	func (e *DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
61	
62	// Detect is not meaningful for the dictionary engine.
63	func (e *DictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) { return "", nil }
64	
65	// Available probes the API with a common word.
66	func (e *DictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
67		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
68		defer cancel()
69		_, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, "test")
70		return err == nil && status == http.StatusOK
71	}
72	
73	// Translate performs a dictionary lookup (exact, then fuzzy on 404).
74	func (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
75		word := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Text))
76		if word == "" {
77			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
78		}
79	
80		entry, status, err := e.fetch(ctx, word)
81		if err == nil && status == http.StatusOK && entry != nil {
82			return single(e.result(entry, req), nil), nil
83		}
84		if err != nil {
85			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err)), nil
86		}
87	
88		// 404: return a ranked "did you mean" list (the frontend chooses one). Gated
89		// to English because the bundled wordlist (/usr/share/dict/words) is English.
90		if status == http.StatusNotFound && e.cfg.Fuzzy && strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(e.cfg.Lang), "en") {
91			if cands, best := e.wl.nearestN(word, 2, suggestLimit); len(cands) > 0 {
92				return single(&TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: e.Name(), Suggestions: cands, SuggestDistance: best}, nil), nil
93			}
94		}
95		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word)), nil
96	}
97	
98	func (e *DictEngine) result(entry *DictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
99		return &TranslateResult{
100			Translation: entry.gloss(),
101			Target:      req.Target,
102			Engine:      e.Name(),
103			Dictionary:  entry,
104		}
105	}
106	
107	// --- Free Dictionary API wire types ---
108	
109	type apiEntry struct {
110		Word       string        `json:"word"`
111		Phonetic   string        `json:"phonetic"`
112		Phonetics  []apiPhonetic `json:"phonetics"`
113		Meanings   []apiMeaning  `json:"meanings"`
114		SourceURLs []string      `json:"sourceUrls"`
115	}
116	type apiPhonetic struct {
117		Text string `json:"text"`
118	}
119	type apiMeaning struct {
120		PartOfSpeech string          `json:"partOfSpeech"`
121		Definitions  []apiDefinition `json:"definitions"`
122		Synonyms     []string        `json:"synonyms"`
123		Antonyms     []string        `json:"antonyms"`
124	}
125	type apiDefinition struct {
126		Definition string `json:"definition"`
127		Example    string `json:"example"`
128	}
129	
130	// fetch requests one word; returns (entry, httpStatus, err). A 404 yields
131	// (nil, 404, nil) so the caller can trigger the fuzzy fallback.
132	func (e *DictEngine) fetch(ctx context.Context, word string) (*DictEntry, int, error) {
133		u := fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s/%s", strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.Endpoint, "/"), e.cfg.Lang, urlPathEscape(word))
134		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
139		if err != nil {
140			return nil, 0, err
141		}
142		defer resp.Body.Close()
143		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {
144			_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
145			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
146		}
147		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
148			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
149			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))
150		}
151		var entries []apiEntry
152		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&entries); err != nil {
153			return nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("decode: %w", err)
154		}
155		if len(entries) == 0 {
156			return nil, http.StatusNotFound, nil
157		}
158		return mergeEntries(entries), http.StatusOK, nil
159	}
160	
161	// mergeEntries folds the API's per-entry results into one DictEntry.
162	func mergeEntries(entries []apiEntry) *DictEntry {
163		out := &DictEntry{Word: entries[0].Word}
164		for _, en := range entries {
165			if out.Phonetic == "" && en.Phonetic != "" {
166				out.Phonetic = en.Phonetic
167			}
168			for _, p := range en.Phonetics {
169				if out.Phonetic == "" && p.Text != "" {
170					out.Phonetic = p.Text
171				}
172			}
173			for _, m := range en.Meanings {
174				meaning := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: m.PartOfSpeech, Synonyms: m.Synonyms, Antonyms: m.Antonyms}
175				for _, d := range m.Definitions {
176					meaning.Definitions = append(meaning.Definitions, Definition{Text: d.Definition, Example: d.Example})
177				}
178				out.Meanings = append(out.Meanings, meaning)
179			}
180			if out.SourceURL == "" && len(en.SourceURLs) > 0 {
181				out.SourceURL = en.SourceURLs[0]
182			}
183		}
184		return out
185	}
186	
187	// gloss returns a short one-line definition for history/plain display.
188	func (d *DictEntry) gloss() string {
189		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
190			for _, def := range m.Definitions {
191				if def.Text != "" {
192					return def.Text
193				}
194			}
195		}
196		return d.Word
197	}
198	
199	func urlPathEscape(s string) string {
200		// dictionary words are simple; escape spaces defensively.
201		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, " ", "%20")
202	}
203	
204	// --- local fuzzy word index ---
205	
206	type wordIndex struct {
207		path  string
208		once  sync.Once
209		words []string
210	}
211	
212	func (wi *wordIndex) load() {
213		wi.once.Do(func() {
214			b, err := os.ReadFile(wi.path)
215			if err != nil {
216				return // no wordlist: fuzzy silently disabled
217			}
218			for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
219				w := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(line))
220				if w != "" {
221					wi.words = append(wi.words, w)
222				}
223			}
224		})
225	}
226	
227	// nearestN returns up to n headwords within maxDist edits of word, closest first
228	// (ties broken alphabetically), excluding word itself. The second return is the
229	// best (smallest) edit distance among the results, or 0 when there are none.
230	func (wi *wordIndex) nearestN(word string, maxDist, n int) ([]string, int) {
231		wi.load()
232		type cand struct {
233			w string
234			d int
235		}
236		var cs []cand
237		for _, w := range wi.words {
238			if w == word || abs(len(w)-len(word)) > maxDist {
239				continue
240			}
241			if d := levenshtein.ComputeDistance(word, w); d <= maxDist {
242				cs = append(cs, cand{w, d})
243			}
244		}
245		sort.Slice(cs, func(i, j int) bool {
246			if cs[i].d != cs[j].d {
247				return cs[i].d < cs[j].d
248			}
249			return cs[i].w < cs[j].w
250		})
251		if len(cs) > n {
252			cs = cs[:n]
253		}
254		out := make([]string, len(cs))
255		for i, c := range cs {
256			out[i] = c.w
257		}
258		best := 0
259		if len(cs) > 0 {
260			best = cs[0].d // slice is sorted by distance ascending
261		}
262		return out, best
263	}
264	
265	func abs(n int) int {
266		if n < 0 {
267			return -n
268		}
269		return n
270	}
271
```

 `./internal/engine/localdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
11	)
12	
13	// LocalDictConfig configures the offline bilingual dictionary engine.
14	type LocalDictConfig struct {
15		Dir          string
16		CedictURL    string
17		EcdictURL    string
18		AutoDownload bool        // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first Chinese lookup
19		Fuzzy        bool        // emit ranked suggestions on a miss
20		APIFallback  *DictEngine // nil unless configured; used for English when ECDICT is absent
21		Timeout      time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// LocalDictEngine looks up definitions offline: Chinese → CC-CEDICT (zh→en),
25	// English → ECDICT (en→zh). It satisfies the Engine interface and maps results
26	// into the same DictEntry/Suggestions shape the TUI already renders.
27	type LocalDictEngine struct {
28		cfg LocalDictConfig
29		ce  *cedictIndex
30		ec  *ecdictDB
31		wl  *wordIndex // /usr/share/dict/words, for English "did you mean"
32	}
33	
34	// NewLocalDict builds a local bilingual dictionary engine.
35	func NewLocalDict(cfg LocalDictConfig) *LocalDictEngine {
36		return &LocalDictEngine{
37			cfg: cfg,
38			ce:  newCedictIndex(CedictPath(cfg.Dir)),
39			ec:  newEcdictDB(EcdictDBPath(cfg.Dir)),
40			wl:  &wordIndex{path: "/usr/share/dict/words"},
41		}
42	}
43	
44	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Name() string         { return "dictionary" }
45	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
46	
47	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
48		return "", nil
49	}
50	
51	// Available reports true if at least one data source (or the API fallback) is usable.
52	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
53		if fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) || e.ec.available() {
54			return true
55		}
56		if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
57			return e.cfg.APIFallback.Available(ctx)
58		}
59		return false
60	}
61	
62	// Translate routes by script: Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT.
63	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
64		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
65		if word == "" {
66			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
67		}
68		if lang.IsChinese(word) {
69			return e.lookupZh(ctx, word, req), nil
70		}
71		return e.lookupEn(ctx, word, req), nil
72	}
73	
74	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupZh(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
75		if !fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) {
76			if e.cfg.AutoDownload {
77				if err := DownloadCedict(ctx, e.cfg.CedictURL, CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir), nil); err != nil {
78					return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
79				}
80			} else {
81				return single(notInstalled(req, "CC-CEDICT not installed — run `translate dict update cedict`"), nil)
82			}
83		}
84		if entries := e.ce.lookup(word); len(entries) > 0 {
85			return single(cedictResult(word, entries, req), nil)
86		}
87		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
88			if sugg := e.ce.prefixSuggest(word, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
89				// CC-CEDICT prefix matches carry no edit distance → 0 (unknown).
90				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, 0), nil)
91			}
92		}
93		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
94	}
95	
96	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupEn(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
97		if !e.ec.available() {
98			// No ECDICT yet: fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (English defs) if enabled.
99			if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
100				ch, err := e.cfg.APIFallback.Translate(ctx, req)
101				if err == nil {
102					return ch
103				}
104			}
105			return single(notInstalled(req, "ECDICT not installed — run `translate dict update ecdict`"), nil)
106		}
107		en, err := e.ec.lookup(ctx, word)
108		if err != nil {
109			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
110		}
111		if en != nil {
112			return single(ecdictResult(en, req), nil)
113		}
114		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
115			if sugg, best := e.wl.nearestN(strings.ToLower(word), 2, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
116				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, best), nil)
117			}
118		}
119		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
120	}
121	
122	// --- result mapping into the existing DictEntry / Suggestions shape ---
123	
124	func cedictResult(word string, entries []*cedictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
125		d := &DictEntry{Word: word}
126		if len(entries) > 0 {
127			d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin
128		}
129		for _, en := range entries {
130			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: en.Pinyin}
131			for _, def := range en.Defs {
132				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: def})
133			}
134			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
135		}
136		gloss := ""
137		if len(entries) > 0 && len(entries[0].Defs) > 0 {
138			gloss = entries[0].Defs[0]
139		}
140		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
141	}
142	
143	func ecdictResult(en *ecdictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
144		d := &DictEntry{Word: en.Word, Phonetic: en.Phonetic}
145		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
146			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "中文"}
147			for _, line := range zh {
148				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
149			}
150			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
151		}
152		if de := splitEcdict(en.Definition); len(de) > 0 {
153			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "definition"}
154			for _, line := range de {
155				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
156			}
157			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
158		}
159		gloss := ""
160		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
161			gloss = zh[0]
162		}
163		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
164	}
165	
166	func suggestResult(sugg []string, req Request, bestDist int) *TranslateResult {
167		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Suggestions: sugg, SuggestDistance: bestDist}
168	}
169	
170	func notInstalled(req Request, note string) *TranslateResult {
171		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Notes: note}
172	}
173	
174	func fileExists(p string) bool {
175		_, err := os.Stat(p)
176		return err == nil
177	}
178
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backlog/api-server.md`

```text
1	# Local HTTP API server (`translate serve`) + OpenAPI/Swagger
2	
3	**Status**: P? — needs a spike
4	**Effort**: L
5	**Related**: `TODO.md` P? · `internal/engine/` · [raycast-extension.md](raycast-extension.md) · [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	2026-07, surfaced while building the Raycast extension. Today every client (the
10	Raycast extension, script commands) **spawns the `translate` binary per call**. A
11	persistent local HTTP server (`translate serve`) with an OpenAPI/Swagger page was
12	proposed as "more usable." This doc records whether it is, and how.
13	
14	A related idea — folding `just raycast-dev` into the binary as `translate raycast
15	dev` — is **rejected** here (option C): it couples the Go CLI to the Node/`ray`
16	toolchain and the extension's on-disk location. Dev-tooling orchestration stays in
17	the `Justfile`. The useful subcommand is `translate serve`, not a `ray develop` wrapper.
18	
19	## Investigation
20	
21	- **Latency win is smaller than it looks.** Per-call spawn cost = config load +
22	  sqlite dict open + engine init. But LLM latency (~3–4 s) dominates, so a warm
23	  server mainly speeds up the **local dict/define** path, *not* LLM translation.
24	- **Streaming is the real win.** An HTTP server controls its own flushing → SSE
25	  (`text/event-stream`); clients consume via `fetch`/EventSource. Cleaner than the
26	  TTY / `--stream` / spawn path. (Caveat: upstream copilot-proxy still buffers claude
27	  `/v1/messages`, so visible streaming stays limited there regardless of transport.)
28	- **Multi-client + docs.** One loopback service → Raycast, Alfred, a browser
29	  extension, editor plugins, `curl`, a web UI. OpenAPI/Swagger documents it for all.
30	- **Sidesteps launchd-PATH** for request-time — the extension hits
31	  `http://127.0.0.1:PORT` instead of resolving/spawning the binary. But the *first*
32	  "start the daemon" still needs a spawn/LaunchAgent (PATH problem just moves).
33	- **Cheap to build.** Reuses `internal/engine` (pure Go, context-aware, TUI-free) as
34	  a thin HTTP adapter. stdlib `net/http` suffices (no framework dep). OpenAPI can be a
35	  hand-written `/openapi.json` + `go:embed`-ded Swagger UI, or generated
36	  (`swaggo/swag`, `danielgtaylor/huma`). Endpoints: `POST /v1/translate`,
37	  `POST /v1/define`, `GET /v1/history[?q=]`, SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`.
38	
39	## Options considered
40	
41	| Option | What | Verdict |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| A. Keep spawn (current) | extension spawns `translate --json` / `--stream` per call | simplest, works, no daemon; per-call startup cost is small vs LLM latency |
44	| B. `translate serve` (HTTP + SSE + OpenAPI) | persistent loopback service; extension opt-in via a "server URL" pref, spawn fallback | best for streaming + multi-client + no-PATH; adds daemon lifecycle + security surface |
45	| C. `translate raycast dev` subcommand | binary shells out to npm/`ray` | rejected — couples Go CLI to Node toolchain + extension location; keep in Justfile |
46	
47	## Current blocker / open questions
48	
49	- **Daemon lifecycle**: how to keep it running — menu-bar app, a `LaunchAgent`, or the
50	  extension auto-starting it (which reintroduces the PATH problem for the first spawn)?
51	- **Security**: loopback-only bind is mandatory (history is personal data); consider a
52	  token for `/v1/history`.
53	- **Concurrent history writes**: server + CLI both appending `history.jsonl` — the
54	  store must be concurrency-safe (append + lock).
55	- **Port**: fixed default (collision risk) vs dynamic (needs discovery). Lean to a
56	  config'd default (e.g. `4155`) + preference.
57	- **Is it worth it vs spawn?** Only if we want a local translation *service* (streaming
58	  UX, multiple clients, web UI). The raw-latency argument is weak (LLM-dominated).
59	
60	## Decision (if any)
61	
62	`2026-07 deferred` — spawn mode works and ships. Revisit if we want a streaming-first
63	UX, multiple clients, or a web UI. If built: start minimal (stdlib `net/http`,
64	loopback, SSE, hand-written OpenAPI + embedded Swagger), extension **opt-in** with a
65	spawn fallback so non-server users are unaffected.
66	
67	## References
68	
69	- stdlib `net/http`; `go:embed` for the Swagger UI assets
70	- OpenAPI generators: https://github.com/swaggo/swag · https://github.com/danielgtaylor/huma
71	- Raycast integration + the streaming caveat: [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
72
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:02:34Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/google.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"net/url"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
14	)
15	
16	// GoogleConfig configures the free, unofficial Google translate_a/single endpoint.
17	type GoogleConfig struct {
18		Endpoint  string
19		ExtraDT   []string // extra dt slots, e.g. "bd" (bilingual dict), "at" (alternates)
20		UserAgent string
21		Timeout   time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// GoogleEngine is a keyless translation engine using Google's web endpoint.
25	// It is unofficial and best-effort: it can rate-limit (HTTP 429) or change shape
26	// without notice, so it lives at the end of the fallback chain.
27	type GoogleEngine struct {
28		cfg  GoogleConfig
29		http *http.Client
30	}
31	
32	// NewGoogle builds a Google engine with sensible defaults.
33	func NewGoogle(cfg GoogleConfig) *GoogleEngine {
34		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
35			cfg.Endpoint = "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single"
36		}
37		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
38			cfg.Timeout = 4 * time.Second
39		}
40		if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
41			cfg.UserAgent = "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli"
42		}
43		return &GoogleEngine{cfg: cfg, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
44	}
45	
46	// Name returns "google".
47	func (e *GoogleEngine) Name() string { return "google" }
48	
49	// Supports reports that Google handles translation only (not dictionary).
50	func (e *GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
51	
52	func (e *GoogleEngine) buildURL(req Request) string {
53		sl := req.Source
54		if sl == "" {
55			sl = "auto"
56		}
57		q := url.Values{}
58		q.Set("client", "gtx")
59		q.Set("sl", sl)
60		q.Set("tl", req.Target)
61		q.Set("dt", "t")
62		for _, dt := range e.cfg.ExtraDT {
63			q.Add("dt", dt)
64		}
65		q.Set("q", req.Text)
66		return e.cfg.Endpoint + "?" + q.Encode()
67	}
68	
69	// Translate performs one keyless translation (non-streaming).
70	func (e *GoogleEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
71		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
72			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
73		}
74		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.buildURL(req), nil)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, err
77		}
78		httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
79	
80		resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
81		if err != nil {
82			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: %w", err)), nil
83		}
84		defer resp.Body.Close()
85		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
86			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
87			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))), nil
88		}
89	
90		var data []json.RawMessage
91		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
92			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: decode: %w", err)), nil
93		}
94		res, err := parseGoogle(data, req)
95		if err != nil {
96			return single(nil, err), nil
97		}
98		res.Engine = e.Name()
99		return single(res, nil), nil
100	}
101	
102	// parseGoogle defensively walks the nested-array response. Positions: data[0] is
103	// an array of [translated, original, ...] segments (long text splits into many);
104	// data[2] is the detected source language.
105	func parseGoogle(data []json.RawMessage, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {
106		if len(data) < 1 {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: empty payload")
108		}
109		var segs []json.RawMessage
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(data[0], &segs); err != nil {
111			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: bad segments: %w", err)
112		}
113		var b strings.Builder
114		for _, s := range segs {
115			var parts []json.RawMessage
116			if json.Unmarshal(s, &parts) != nil || len(parts) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			var txt string
120			if json.Unmarshal(parts[0], &txt) == nil {
121				b.WriteString(txt)
122			}
123		}
124		res := &TranslateResult{Translation: strings.TrimSpace(b.String()), Target: req.Target}
125		if res.Translation == "" {
126			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: no translation in response")
127		}
128		if len(data) > 2 {
129			var det string
130			if json.Unmarshal(data[2], &det) == nil {
131				res.DetectedSource = det
132			}
133		}
134		return res, nil
135	}
136	
137	// Detect uses the offline detector (cheap, no network round-trip).
138	func (e *GoogleEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
139		return lang.Detect(text), nil
140	}
141	
142	// Available probes the endpoint with a tiny translation request.
143	func (e *GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
144		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
145		defer cancel()
146		u := e.buildURL(Request{Text: "a", Source: "auto", Target: "en"})
147		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
148		if err != nil {
149			return false
150		}
151		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
152		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
153		if err != nil {
154			return false
155		}
156		defer resp.Body.Close()
157		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
158		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
159	}
160
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/prompt.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- ALWAYS translate into the target language. A single word, name, technical term, or
31	  loanword written in a DIFFERENT language must still be translated — never echo it back.
32	  Only when the text is ALREADY in the target language should you return it (lightly
33	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
34	
35	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
36	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
37	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
38	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
39	
40	Rules:
41	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
42	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
43	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
44	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
45	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
46	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
47	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
48	
49	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
50	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
51	that also gives brief usage examples.
52	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
53	
54	Rules:
55	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
56	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
57	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
58	- Format each example on two lines:
59	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
60	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
61	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
62	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
63	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
64	
65	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
66	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
67		switch preset {
68		case PresetContextual:
69			return translateSystemPromptContextual
70		case PresetDictionary:
71			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
72		default:
73			return translateSystemPromptConcise
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// pairDirective is appended to the system prompt in bidirectional pair mode. It
78	// makes the model own language detection (more robust than a pre-computed target
79	// for short/ambiguous input) and forbids echoing the input — the exact failure
80	// this fixes ("test" → "test" instead of "測試").
81	const pairDirective = `Bidirectional mode: the text is written in ONE of %s or %s. Detect which of the two it is, and translate it into the OTHER one. ALWAYS translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, a proper name, a technical term, or a loanword.`
82	
83	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
84	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
85	Target language: %s
86	
87	Text:
88	%s`
89	
90	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
91	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
92	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
93	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
94		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
95		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
96			src = "auto"
97		} else {
98			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
99		}
100		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
101		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
102		// In pair mode, let the model detect the input language and route to the other
103		// side, and never echo — this is appended to (not a replacement of) the preset
104		// so the chosen output format (concise/contextual/dictionary) is preserved.
105		if req.Pair && req.PairHome != "" && req.PairAway != "" {
106			system += "\n\n" + fmt.Sprintf(pairDirective, lang.Name(req.PairHome), lang.Name(req.PairAway))
107		}
108		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
109			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
110		}
111		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
112	}
113	
114	// translateBilingualSystemPrompt drives context-aware whole-document ("doc mode")
115	// translation. The model sees the FULL document (prose + code as context) and
116	// returns a JSON object mapping each prose segment's number to its translation, so
117	// it can use cross-segment context — e.g. that a bare `rg` is the command being
118	// documented, not an abbreviation — and stay in one consistent target variant.
119	// %[1]s is the readable target language name (used twice).
120	const translateBilingualSystemPrompt = `You translate terminal / CLI documentation into %[1]s.
121	
122	You are given the whole document as a numbered list of SEGMENTS. Some are prose to
123	translate; others are command/code lines marked "[code — context only]" and are
124	shown ONLY so you understand the document — never translate or echo them.
125	
126	Rules:
127	- Use the WHOLE document as context. A bare token like "rg" or "ls" on its own is
128	  the COMMAND being documented, not an abbreviation — do not expand or gloss it.
129	- Keep command names, subcommands, flags, file paths, URLs, and code verbatim.
130	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the intended meaning of typos; never refuse.
131	- Translate EVERY prose segment into %[1]s only — one consistent variant, never mixed.
132	- Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else: keys are the prose segment numbers
133	  (as strings), values are the translations. No markdown fence, no reasoning, no
134	  commentary before or after the JSON.`
135	
136	// bilingualUserPrompt frames the numbered document for a doc-mode request.
137	const bilingualUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
138	Target language: %s
139	
140	Document segments:
141	%s`
142	
143	// buildBilingualPrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a doc-mode bilingual
144	// request. Prose segments are numbered (1-based, matching TranslateResult.Bilingual
145	// keys); code segments are shown inline as context and are NOT numbered.
146	func buildBilingualPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
147		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
148		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
149			src = "auto"
150		} else {
151			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
152		}
153		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
154		system = fmt.Sprintf(translateBilingualSystemPrompt, tgt)
155		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
156			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
157		}
158	
159		var b strings.Builder
160		n := 0
161		for _, s := range req.Segments {
162			text := strings.TrimSpace(s.Text)
163			if s.Code {
164				b.WriteString("    [code — context only] " + text + "\n")
165				continue
166			}
167			n++
168			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "%d. %s\n", n, text)
169		}
170		return system, fmt.Sprintf(bilingualUserPrompt, src, tgt, b.String())
171	}
172	
173	// learnDirection reports the learn-mode direction, decided OFFLINE via the same
174	// script-aware router as pair mode (lang.PairTarget): native (home) input → "teach"
175	// (translate + glosses); foreign (away) input → "correct" (grammar-correct + explain).
176	// Trusting this over the model's own guess keeps the two output shapes deterministic.
177	func learnDirection(req Request) string {
178		if lang.PairTarget(req.PairHome, req.PairAway, req.Text) == req.PairAway {
179			return "teach"
180		}
181		return "correct"
182	}
183	
184	// learnTeachPrompt drives native→foreign tutoring. %[1]s = native (home) language,
185	// %[2]s = foreign (away) language being learned.
186	const learnTeachPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a native %[1]s speaker learn %[2]s.
187	The user writes in %[1]s. Translate their text into natural, idiomatic %[2]s, then teach it.
188	
189	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
190	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
191	{
192	  "direction": "teach",
193	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
194	  "translation": "<the idiomatic %[2]s translation>",
195	  "vocab": [
196	    {"term": "<key %[2]s word or phrase>", "pos": "<part of speech>", "phonetic": "<KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese>", "meaning": "<concise meaning in %[1]s>"}
197	  ],
198	  "examples": [
199	    {"foreign": "<a short natural %[2]s sentence using the translation>", "native": "<its %[1]s translation>"}
200	  ],
201	  "notes": "<one short usage or register tip in %[1]s, optional>"
202	}
203	
204	Rules:
205	- Write every meaning, note, and explanation in %[1]s (the learner's language).
206	- vocab: only the key content words/phrases worth learning (at most 8); skip trivial function words.
207	- examples: 1-2, short and idiomatic.
208	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
209	
210	// learnCorrectPrompt drives foreign→native correction. %[1]s = foreign (away)
211	// language being learned, %[2]s = native (home) language.
212	const learnCorrectPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a %[2]s speaker who is learning %[1]s.
213	The user writes a sentence in %[1]s that may contain mistakes. Correct it and explain, kindly.
214	
215	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
216	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
217	{
218	  "direction": "correct",
219	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
220	  "corrected": "<the grammatically correct, idiomatic %[1]s sentence>",
221	  "translation": "<a %[2]s translation of the intended meaning>",
222	  "issues": [
223	    {"span": "<the problematic fragment of the input>", "fix": "<the corrected fragment>", "explanation": "<why, in %[2]s>"}
224	  ],
225	  "notes": "<one short encouraging tip in %[2]s, optional>"
226	}
227	
228	Rules:
229	- Write every explanation and note in %[2]s (the learner's native language).
230	- If the sentence is already correct, echo it in "corrected" and return "issues": [].
231	- List each distinct mistake as one issue; keep explanations concise and beginner-friendly.
232	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
233	
234	// learnUserPrompt frames the concrete learn request. The system prompt already
235	// names both languages, so the user turn carries only the text.
236	const learnUserPrompt = `Text:
237	%s`
238	
239	// buildLearnPrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a learn-mode request,
240	// choosing the teach or correct prompt from the offline-detected direction. Codes
241	// are expanded to readable names so regional variants (zh-TW) read naturally.
242	func buildLearnPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
243		home := lang.Name(req.PairHome) // native
244		away := lang.Name(req.PairAway) // foreign
245		if learnDirection(req) == "teach" {
246			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnTeachPrompt, home, away)
247		} else {
248			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnCorrectPrompt, away, home)
249		}
250		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
251			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
252		}
253		return system, fmt.Sprintf(learnUserPrompt, req.Text)
254	}
255
```
</details></tool-use>
