<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/raycast-extension.md`

```text
1	# Raycast integration — reference
2	
3	How `translate` plugs into [Raycast](https://raycast.com), the options we
4	weighed, how the shipped integration works, and how it compares to existing
5	translation extensions. This is present-tense reference reading; the actual code
6	lives in [`../raycast/`](../raycast/), and store-publishing is tracked in
7	[`../backlog/raycast-extension.md`](../backlog/raycast-extension.md).
8	
9	**Core principle: reuse the binary, don't reimplement.** Every tier below shells
10	out to the existing `translate` CLI (`--json`) and renders the result. No
11	translation, engine, or dictionary logic is duplicated in TypeScript/bash.
12	
13	## Integration tiers
14	
15	Raycast offers four ways to surface functionality. We ship the middle two.
16	
17	| Tier | Reuses binary? | Effort | Selection → translate | Persisted defaults | Rich UI / history | Streaming | Distribution |
18	|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
19	| **Quick Link** | ✗ (can't exec) | 5 min | via deeplink only | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | launcher only |
20	| **Script Command** (bash) — *shipped* | ✓ | ~0.5 h | ✗ (needs `pbpaste`) | ✗ (no prefs) | ✗ (plain text) | only `fullOutput` | Add Script Directory |
21	| **TS Extension** (`@raycast/api`) — *shipped* | ✓ | 1–2 d | ✓ `getSelectedText()` | ✓ preferences | ✓ List/Detail/Actions | ✓ (manual `spawn`) | `npm run dev` / store |
22	| **AI Extension** (tools) | ✓ | +0.5 d | AI-orchestrated | — | AI Chat | `AI.ask` | store; **Pro-gated** |
23	
24	- **Quick Links** open a URL/file/app/`raycast://` deeplink — they have no
25	  shell-exec surface, so they can't run the CLI directly. Useful only as a hotkey
26	  that deeplinks into a real command.
27	- **AI Extensions** expose "tools" the Raycast AI can call. Feasible (a tool file
28	  can `execFile` the binary and return its `--json`), but the AI API requires
29	  **Raycast Pro**, so it's an additive Pro-only layer — deferred.
30	
31	## How Raycast extensions work
32	
33	- **Manifest** (`package.json`): a `commands[]` array; each command has a
34	  required `mode`:
35	  - `view` — default-exports a React (TSX) component (our `Translate`).
36	  - `no-view` — default-exports an async function; runs and exits, no UI
37	    (our `Translate Selection` → `getSelectedText` → `Clipboard.paste` + `showHUD`).
38	  - `menu-bar` — a persistent `MenuBarExtra` (not used yet).
39	- **Running the binary:** extensions run in a Node runtime, so `child_process`
40	  works. We use `execFile` (typed wrapper in
41	  [`../raycast/extension/src/lib/translate.ts`](../raycast/extension/src/lib/translate.ts))
42	  rather than the `useExec` hook, because `useExec` buffers with a 10 s default
43	  timeout that LLM engines exceed (and its `timeout: 0` is coerced back to 10000).
44	  The CLI's **`--stream` flag** forces token streaming even when stdout is piped
45	  (which Raycast always is; without it the CLI treats non-TTY as buffered). A
46	  streaming `Detail` view can spawn `translate … --stream` and append `stdout`
47	  chunks into React state bound to `Detail.markdown`. Caveat: visible progressive
48	  output depends on the provider — ollama streams; **copilot-proxy currently buffers
49	  its claude `/v1/messages` responses**, so the result appears after first-token
50	  latency. The default live view uses `--json` (buffered, structured), which returns
51	  fast enough.
52	- **Preferences** (`getPreferenceValues()`): persisted per-extension settings —
53	  our `binaryPath`, `defaultTarget`, `engine`, `tier`.
54	- **Input/UI:** `getSelectedText()` (frontmost app's selection),
55	  `Clipboard.copy/paste`, `Action.CopyToClipboard`/`Action.Paste`; `List` +
56	  `List.Dropdown` (searchBarAccessory, `storeValue` to remember the last target);
57	  `Detail`/`List.Item.Detail` with `.Metadata` for structured fields; `showHUD`/
58	  `showToast` for feedback.
59	- **Tooling:** the `ray` CLI (ships in the extension's dev deps). `ray develop`
60	  (`npm run dev`) registers the extension with the running Raycast app and
61	  hot-reloads; **it persists in root search after you stop dev** — no store
62	  publish needed for personal use. `ray build`/`ray lint` type-check and lint.
63	
64	## Gotchas (learned the hard way)
65	
66	- **launchd PATH:** Raycast launches under launchd and does *not* inherit your
67	  shell PATH, so a bare `translate` fails with `ENOENT` / `command not found`.
68	  Both tracks resolve an **absolute** path (preference first, then probe
69	  `~/.local/bin`, `/opt/homebrew/bin`, `/usr/local/bin`, `~/go/bin`). Always test
70	  **from Raycast**, not a terminal (a terminal's PATH hides the bug). See
71	  [`../pitfalls/raycast-launchd-path-translate-not-found.md`](../pitfalls/raycast-launchd-path-translate-not-found.md).
72	- **HOME / config, not shell env:** the CLI reads providers/keys from
73	  `~/.config/translate/config.toml`, *not* environment variables. A key exported
74	  in `~/.zshrc` is absent under launchd — put it in the config (`translate init`).
75	  We pass `HOME` through so the CLI finds its config.
76	- **Exit code is 0 even on engine failure** (auto-fallback), so we read
77	  `warnings[]` from `--json` rather than branching on exit codes. The extension
78	  surfaces warnings in the Detail pane / HUD; the plain-text scripts don't.
79	- **Extension icons must be a real PNG** (512×512) — emoji icons work only for
80	  Script Commands. `ray build`/`lint` complains otherwise.
81	
82	## Competitive landscape (2026)
83	
84	The Raycast translation category is crowded, but the field is almost entirely
85	remote-API/cloud-LLM wrappers configured via extension preferences.
86	
87	| Extension | Backend | Streaming | TTS | History | Offline dict |
88	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
89	| gebeto/translate (Google) | Google web (keyless) | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
90	| tisfeng/Easydict | 10 remote providers + Apple `say`/Shortcuts | ✗ | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ (remote) |
91	| douo/openai-translator | multi-LLM incl. Ollama / custom endpoint | ✓ | ✗ (roadmap) | ✓ | ✗ |
92	| deepcast / itranslate | DeepL / multi (BYO API key) | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
93	| Raycast built-in Translate | unstated | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ (Pro-gated) |
94	
95	**What `translate` does that they don't:**
96	
97	- **Shells out to a dedicated local translator CLI** — no surveyed extension
98	  wraps a standalone translator binary (Easydict shells to `osascript`/Apple
99	  Shortcuts; none to a CLI like this).
100	- **Automatic multi-engine failover** — competitors that support multiple engines
101	  do *parallel aggregation*, not "try A, on failure fall back to B".
102	- **Offline CC-CEDICT / ECDICT dictionaries** via `translate define` — all
103	  competitors' dictionaries are remote.
104	- **Unified, searchable history** (`translate history [search]`).
105	- **Free local TTS** (`--speak`) — note openai-translator lists TTS as an
106	  unshipped roadmap item, so this is a real gap.
107	- **Fuzzy language resolution** (`translate lang resolve`, `chinees → zh`).
108	
109	Frame differentiation as **offline-resilient + auto-fallback + unified history**,
110	*not* "local LLM" (openai-translator already reaches Ollama) and *not* "study
111	modes" (Anki / Vocabulary Builder already exist in the store).
112	
113	## How ours is built
114	
115	- **Script Commands** — [`../raycast/script-commands/`](../raycast/script-commands/):
116	  `translate-text` (dropdown language + text, `fullOutput`), `translate-and-copy`
117	  (`silent`, copies; blank arg → clipboard), `define-word`. Install:
118	  `just raycast-scripts`, then add the directory in Raycast once.
119	- **TS extension** — [`../raycast/extension/`](../raycast/extension/): commands
120	  `translate` (view: type-to-translate, language dropdown, engine-override submenu,
121	  Copy/Paste/Speak, selection/clipboard prefill), `translate-selection` (no-view:
122	  grabs the selection/clipboard and opens Translate prefilled via `launchCommand` —
123	  editable, not blind-paste), `define` (view: dictionary lookup +
124	  LLM fallback + "did you mean" suggestions), and `history` (view: browse/search
125	  past translations). All share `src/lib/translate.ts` (binary resolve + typed
126	  `execFile` wrappers mirroring `internal/engine/engine.go`'s `TranslateResult`).
127	  Run: `just raycast-dev` (`build`/`lint` variants exist). Live translate is
128	  debounced + abortable to avoid an LLM call per keystroke, with an opt-in `⌘↵`
129	  streaming view (`spawnTranslateStream` → `translate … --stream` → live `Detail`).
130	
131	## References
132	
133	- Raycast API — manifest, commands, UI: https://developers.raycast.com/
134	- `useExec`: https://developers.raycast.com/utilities/react-hooks/useexec
135	- AI Extensions: https://developers.raycast.com/ai
136	- Script Commands: https://github.com/raycast/script-commands
137	- Prepare / Publish / Teams: https://developers.raycast.com/basics/prepare-an-extension-for-store
138
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backlog/raycast-extension.md`

```text
1	# Publish the Raycast extension to the store
2	
3	**Status**: P? — local-dev shipped; publish deferred
4	**Effort**: L
5	**Related**: `TODO.md` P? · `raycast/extension/` · [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md) · [homebrew-distribution.md](homebrew-distribution.md)
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	2026-07. The Raycast integration shipped as two local tracks (`raycast/script-commands/`
10	bash + `raycast/extension/` TypeScript). The TS extension runs via `just raycast-dev`
11	(`ray develop`) and **persists in root search after dev stops**, so personal use needs
12	no store at all. The open question is whether — and how — to distribute it beyond this
13	machine.
14	
15	## Investigation
16	
17	- **Calling a user-installed binary is a sanctioned pattern** ("✅ Calling known system
18	  binaries"). The store's hard rule targets *bundling opaque/heavy binaries*, which we
19	  don't do — we shell out to a Homebrew/`go install` `translate`.
20	- **Soft guideline friction**: the store discourages requiring users to manually install
21	  external tools ("avoid... automate where possible"). Our binary dependency is exactly
22	  that — advisory, not an auto-reject, but the most likely reviewer comment. Mitigate
23	  with graceful binary detection + a "not installed → install instructions" onboarding
24	  view + README.
25	- **Private org store** (Raycast for Teams/Pro): publish via `owner` in package.json +
26	  `npm run publish`; visible only to org members, **no public human review**. Best fit if
27	  distributing to a controlled group who all have the binary.
28	- **Public store requirements**: `author` = a *registered Raycast username* (currently
29	  fails `ray lint` — `daviddwlee84` 404s against Raycast's user API), `license: MIT`
30	  (wrapper only; the Go binary's license is untouched since we don't bundle it), a custom
31	  512×512 icon (the current one is a placeholder), ≥3 screenshots (2000×1250), a
32	  `CHANGELOG.md`, a Title-Case category, `package-lock.json`, and `ray lint` clean.
33	- **No telemetry, no Keychain**. If published, expose the openrouter API key via the
34	  Raycast preferences API (`required: true`), not by reading `config.toml`.
35	- **Platform**: mark `platforms: ["macOS"]` (already set) — the binary shell-out doesn't
36	  work on Raycast's iOS/Windows clients.
37	
38	## Options considered
39	
40	| Option | What | Verdict |
41	|---|---|---|
42	| A. Local dev only (personal) | `ray develop`, per-machine, no review | current default — done |
43	| B. Private org store (Pro/Team) | `owner` + `npm run publish`, internal-only | best if sharing to a team; needs Pro |
44	| C. Public store | PR to raycast/extensions, automated + human review | widest reach; expect binary-dependency review friction |
45	
46	## Current blocker / open questions
47	
48	- Is public publication worth it given the crowded category, or is local/private enough?
49	  (Differentiators are real — see [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
50	  competitive section — but the binary dependency is a review headwind.)
51	- What's the binary-not-found onboarding UX (detection + actionable install view)?
52	- Register/confirm the Raycast `author` handle before any publish (blocks `ray lint`).
53	
54	## Decision (if any)
55	
56	`2026-07 deferred` — ship the local-dev extension first and dogfood it. Revisit B/C once
57	the daily-driver UX is proven and (for C) a designed icon + screenshots + registered
58	author handle are in place.
59	
60	## References
61	
62	- Prepare for Store: https://developers.raycast.com/basics/prepare-an-extension-for-store
63	- Publish: https://developers.raycast.com/basics/publish-an-extension
64	- Teams / private extensions: https://developers.raycast.com/teams/publish-a-private-extension
65
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backlog/README.md`

```text
1	# Backlog Research
2	
3	Long-form research, design notes, and paused troubleshooting for items listed in
4	[`../TODO.md`](../TODO.md). One file per topic, named with a slug that matches
5	the TODO entry.
6	
7	## Why this exists
8	
9	`TODO.md` is the **index** — short titles grouped by priority section with
10	effort tags. This folder
11	is the **knowledge base** — the actual investigation, options considered,
12	benchmarks, error messages, and decisions that informed the TODO entry. The goal
13	is **resume-friendliness**: when you (or an agent) come back to an item three
14	months later, the doc here lets you pick up in 5 minutes instead of re-running
15	30 minutes of investigation.
16	
17	This folder is maintainer-facing repo metadata. It lives in the git repo but
18	never reaches users — `go install` / `go build` compile only Go sources, so it
19	has no effect on the built binary.
20	
21	## When to add a doc here
22	
23	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` file when **any** of these apply:
24	
25	- The TODO item carries a `P?` tag (it requires evaluation; record what was tried)
26	- You did meaningful troubleshooting but didn't ship a fix (capture the trace
27	  before it evaporates)
28	- Multiple options were considered (record the trade-offs, not just the winner)
29	- An external blocker exists (waiting on upstream release, host availability)
30	- The implementation is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
31	
32	`[S]` items rarely need a doc unless there's surprising context.
33	
34	## When NOT to add a doc here
35	
36	- Item is `[S]` and obvious — just put the file path in the TODO entry
37	- Already covered by an existing doc page (cross-link instead — that's
38	  user-facing reference, this is maintainer-facing speculation)
39	- Speculation only ("would be cool to...") with no investigation — keep it as a
40	  one-liner in `TODO.md` first; promote to a doc when you actually look into it
41	
42	## File template
43	
44	See [`backlog-doc.md.template`] in the skill's `assets/` for the per-doc
45	template, or copy the structure of an existing entry below.
46	
47	## Index
48	
49	Add new entries here as you create them. Keep alphabetical.
50	
51	| Slug | Status | TODO entry |
52	|---|---|---|
53	| `api-server` | P? — deferred (2026-07) | P? "Local HTTP API server + OpenAPI/Swagger…" |
54	| `bilingual-immersive-mode` | shipped (2026-07) | Done "Bilingual `--bilingual`/`-2` pipe mode…" |
55	| `chezmoi-go-tool-integration` | spike done, paused on scope (2026-07) | P? "Wire `translate` into chezmoi…" |
56	| `dict-bundling` | deferred (2026-07) | P? "Bundle or prebuild the dictionary…" |
57	| `homebrew-distribution` | shipped (2026-07) | Done "Homebrew tap distribution…" |
58	| `raycast-extension` | P? — local dev shipped, publish deferred (2026-07) | P? "Publish the Raycast extension to the store…" |
59	| `release-binaries` | deferred (2026-07) | P? "Ship prebuilt release binaries…" |
60
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/localdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
11	)
12	
13	// LocalDictConfig configures the offline bilingual dictionary engine.
14	type LocalDictConfig struct {
15		Dir          string
16		CedictURL    string
17		EcdictURL    string
18		AutoDownload bool        // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first Chinese lookup
19		Fuzzy        bool        // emit ranked suggestions on a miss
20		APIFallback  *DictEngine // nil unless configured; used for English when ECDICT is absent
21		Timeout      time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// LocalDictEngine looks up definitions offline: Chinese → CC-CEDICT (zh→en),
25	// English → ECDICT (en→zh). It satisfies the Engine interface and maps results
26	// into the same DictEntry/Suggestions shape the TUI already renders.
27	type LocalDictEngine struct {
28		cfg LocalDictConfig
29		ce  *cedictIndex
30		ec  *ecdictDB
31		wl  *wordIndex // /usr/share/dict/words, for English "did you mean"
32	}
33	
34	// NewLocalDict builds a local bilingual dictionary engine.
35	func NewLocalDict(cfg LocalDictConfig) *LocalDictEngine {
36		return &LocalDictEngine{
37			cfg: cfg,
38			ce:  newCedictIndex(CedictPath(cfg.Dir)),
39			ec:  newEcdictDB(EcdictDBPath(cfg.Dir)),
40			wl:  &wordIndex{path: "/usr/share/dict/words"},
41		}
42	}
43	
44	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Name() string         { return "dictionary" }
45	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
46	
47	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
48		return "", nil
49	}
50	
51	// Available reports true if at least one data source (or the API fallback) is usable.
52	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
53		if fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) || e.ec.available() {
54			return true
55		}
56		if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
57			return e.cfg.APIFallback.Available(ctx)
58		}
59		return false
60	}
61	
62	// Translate routes by script: Chinese → CC-CEDICT, else → ECDICT.
63	func (e *LocalDictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
64		word := strings.TrimSpace(req.Text)
65		if word == "" {
66			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
67		}
68		if lang.IsChinese(word) {
69			return e.lookupZh(ctx, word, req), nil
70		}
71		return e.lookupEn(ctx, word, req), nil
72	}
73	
74	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupZh(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
75		if !fileExists(CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir)) {
76			if e.cfg.AutoDownload {
77				if err := DownloadCedict(ctx, e.cfg.CedictURL, CedictPath(e.cfg.Dir), nil); err != nil {
78					return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
79				}
80			} else {
81				return single(notInstalled(req, "CC-CEDICT not installed — run `translate dict update cedict`"), nil)
82			}
83		}
84		if entries := e.ce.lookup(word); len(entries) > 0 {
85			return single(cedictResult(word, entries, req), nil)
86		}
87		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
88			if sugg := e.ce.prefixSuggest(word, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
89				// CC-CEDICT prefix matches carry no edit distance → 0 (unknown).
90				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, 0), nil)
91			}
92		}
93		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
94	}
95	
96	func (e *LocalDictEngine) lookupEn(ctx context.Context, word string, req Request) <-chan Chunk {
97		if !e.ec.available() {
98			// No ECDICT yet: fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (English defs) if enabled.
99			if e.cfg.APIFallback != nil {
100				ch, err := e.cfg.APIFallback.Translate(ctx, req)
101				if err == nil {
102					return ch
103				}
104			}
105			return single(notInstalled(req, "ECDICT not installed — run `translate dict update ecdict`"), nil)
106		}
107		en, err := e.ec.lookup(ctx, word)
108		if err != nil {
109			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w", err))
110		}
111		if en != nil {
112			return single(ecdictResult(en, req), nil)
113		}
114		if e.cfg.Fuzzy {
115			if sugg, best := e.wl.nearestN(strings.ToLower(word), 2, suggestLimit); len(sugg) > 0 {
116				return single(suggestResult(sugg, req, best), nil)
117			}
118		}
119		return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("dictionary: %w: %q", ErrNoDictEntry, word))
120	}
121	
122	// --- result mapping into the existing DictEntry / Suggestions shape ---
123	
124	func cedictResult(word string, entries []*cedictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
125		d := &DictEntry{Word: word}
126		if len(entries) > 0 {
127			d.Phonetic = entries[0].Pinyin
128		}
129		for _, en := range entries {
130			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: en.Pinyin}
131			for _, def := range en.Defs {
132				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: def})
133			}
134			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
135		}
136		gloss := ""
137		if len(entries) > 0 && len(entries[0].Defs) > 0 {
138			gloss = entries[0].Defs[0]
139		}
140		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
141	}
142	
143	func ecdictResult(en *ecdictEntry, req Request) *TranslateResult {
144		d := &DictEntry{Word: en.Word, Phonetic: en.Phonetic}
145		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
146			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "中文"}
147			for _, line := range zh {
148				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
149			}
150			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
151		}
152		if de := splitEcdict(en.Definition); len(de) > 0 {
153			mn := Meaning{PartOfSpeech: "definition"}
154			for _, line := range de {
155				mn.Definitions = append(mn.Definitions, Definition{Text: line})
156			}
157			d.Meanings = append(d.Meanings, mn)
158		}
159		gloss := ""
160		if zh := splitEcdict(en.Translation); len(zh) > 0 {
161			gloss = zh[0]
162		}
163		return &TranslateResult{Translation: gloss, Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Dictionary: d}
164	}
165	
166	func suggestResult(sugg []string, req Request, bestDist int) *TranslateResult {
167		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Suggestions: sugg, SuggestDistance: bestDist}
168	}
169	
170	func notInstalled(req Request, note string) *TranslateResult {
171		return &TranslateResult{Target: req.Target, Engine: "dictionary", Notes: note}
172	}
173	
174	func fileExists(p string) bool {
175		_, err := os.Stat(p)
176		return err == nil
177	}
178
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/dictdata.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"compress/gzip"
5		"context"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/csv"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strconv"
14		"strings"
15	
16		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
17	
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
19	)
20	
21	// DictDir returns the directory holding local dictionary data.
22	func DictDir(dir string) string {
23		if dir != "" {
24			return dir
25		}
26		return filepath.Join(xdgpath.DataDir(), "dict")
27	}
28	
29	// CedictPath / EcdictDBPath are the on-disk locations of the two data sources.
30	func CedictPath(dir string) string   { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "cedict_ts.u8") }
31	func EcdictDBPath(dir string) string { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "ecdict.db") }
32	
33	const ecdictSchema = `CREATE TABLE entries(
34	  word TEXT, word_lc TEXT, phonetic TEXT, translation TEXT,
35	  definition TEXT, pos TEXT, frq INTEGER, exchange TEXT);`
36	
37	// DownloadCedict fetches the gzipped CC-CEDICT, decompresses it, and writes the
38	// plain text file atomically (~9.4 MB).
39	func DownloadCedict(ctx context.Context, url, dst string, prog func(string)) error {
40		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o700); err != nil {
41			return err
42		}
43		if prog != nil {
44			prog("downloading CC-CEDICT…")
45		}
46		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
51		if err != nil {
52			return err
53		}
54		defer resp.Body.Close()
55		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
56			return fmt.Errorf("cedict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
57		}
58		gz, err := gzip.NewReader(resp.Body)
59		if err != nil {
60			return err
61		}
62		defer gz.Close()
63	
64		tmp := dst + ".tmp"
65		f, err := os.Create(tmp)
66		if err != nil {
67			return err
68		}
69		if _, err := io.Copy(f, gz); err != nil {
70			f.Close()
71			os.Remove(tmp)
72			return err
73		}
74		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
75			os.Remove(tmp)
76			return err
77		}
78		return os.Rename(tmp, dst)
79	}
80	
81	// BuildEcdictDB downloads the ECDICT CSV (~63 MB, MIT) and builds a queryable
82	// SQLite database (~80-100 MB) from it, atomically. Streams the CSV so it never
83	// holds the whole file in memory.
84	func BuildEcdictDB(ctx context.Context, csvURL, dbPath string, prog func(string)) (err error) {
85		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o700); err != nil {
86			return err
87		}
88		if prog != nil {
89			prog("downloading ECDICT CSV (~63 MB)…")
90		}
91		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, csvURL, nil)
92		if err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer resp.Body.Close()
100		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
101			return fmt.Errorf("ecdict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
102		}
103	
104		tmp := dbPath + ".tmp"
105		_ = os.Remove(tmp)
106		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+tmp+"?_pragma=journal_mode(off)&_pragma=synchronous(off)")
107		if err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110		// On any error, close and remove the partial db.
111		defer func() {
112			if err != nil {
113				db.Close()
114				os.Remove(tmp)
115			}
116		}()
117	
118		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, ecdictSchema); err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if prog != nil {
122			prog("importing entries…")
123		}
124		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
125		if err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128		stmt, err := tx.PrepareContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO entries(word,word_lc,phonetic,translation,definition,pos,frq,exchange) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
129		if err != nil {
130			return err
131		}
132	
133		r := csv.NewReader(resp.Body)
134		r.FieldsPerRecord = -1 // tolerate ragged rows
135		r.LazyQuotes = true
136		if _, err = r.Read(); err != nil { // header row
137			return err
138		}
139		n := 0
140		for {
141			if err = ctx.Err(); err != nil {
142				return err
143			}
144			rec, rerr := r.Read()
145			if rerr == io.EOF {
146				break
147			}
148			if rerr != nil || len(rec) < 4 {
149				continue
150			}
151			frq := 0
152			if len(rec) > 9 {
153				frq, _ = strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(rec[9]))
154			}
155			pos, exchange := "", ""
156			if len(rec) > 4 {
157				pos = rec[4]
158			}
159			if len(rec) > 10 {
160				exchange = rec[10]
161			}
162			if _, err = stmt.ExecContext(ctx, rec[0], strings.ToLower(rec[0]), rec[1], rec[3], rec[2], pos, frq, exchange); err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165			n++
166		}
167		if err = tx.Commit(); err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, `CREATE INDEX idx_word_lc ON entries(word_lc)`); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if err = db.Close(); err != nil {
174			return err
175		}
176		if prog != nil {
177			prog(fmt.Sprintf("built %d entries", n))
178		}
179		return os.Rename(tmp, dbPath)
180	}
181
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/define.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		if flagSpeak && cfg.TTS.Enabled {
64			speakDefine(cmd.Context(), cfg, res2)
65		}
66		return nil
67	}
68	
69	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
70	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
71	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
72	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
73		cfg := res.Cfg
74		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
75			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
76		}
77		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
78			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
79		}
80		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
81	}
82	
83	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
84	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
85		d := res.Dictionary
86		if d == nil {
87			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
88				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
89			}
90			return res.Translation + "\n"
91		}
92		var b strings.Builder
93		head := d.Word
94		if d.Phonetic != "" {
95			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
96		}
97		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
98		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
99			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
100			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
101				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
102					break
103				}
104				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
105				if def.Example != "" {
106					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
107				}
108			}
109		}
110		return b.String()
111	}
112
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/history.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"text/tabwriter"
9	
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
14	)
15	
16	func newHistoryCmd() *cobra.Command {
17		var limit int
18		var tsv bool
19	
20		c := &cobra.Command{
21			Use:   "history",
22			Short: "Show recent translation history",
23			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
24				recs, err := loadHistory(cmd, func(st store.Store) ([]store.Record, error) {
25					return st.Recent(cmd.Context(), limit)
26				})
27				if err != nil {
28					return err
29				}
30				return printRecords(recs, tsv)
31			},
32		}
33		c.Flags().IntVar(&limit, "limit", 20, "max entries")
34		c.Flags().BoolVar(&tsv, "tsv", false, "tab-separated output (for scripting / tv)")
35	
36		search := &cobra.Command{
37			Use:   "search <query>",
38			Short: "Fuzzy-search translation history",
39			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
40			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
41				q := strings.Join(args, " ")
42				recs, err := loadHistory(cmd, func(st store.Store) ([]store.Record, error) {
43					return st.Search(cmd.Context(), q, limit)
44				})
45				if err != nil {
46					return err
47				}
48				return printRecords(recs, tsv)
49			},
50		}
51		search.Flags().IntVar(&limit, "limit", 20, "max entries")
52		search.Flags().BoolVar(&tsv, "tsv", false, "tab-separated output")
53		c.AddCommand(search)
54		return c
55	}
56	
57	// loadHistory opens the store and runs a query, handling the disabled case.
58	func loadHistory(cmd *cobra.Command, query func(store.Store) ([]store.Record, error)) ([]store.Record, error) {
59		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
60		if err != nil {
61			return nil, err
62		}
63		st := openStore(cfg)
64		if st == nil {
65			return nil, fmt.Errorf("history is disabled in %s", config.Path())
66		}
67		defer st.Close()
68		return query(st)
69	}
70	
71	func printRecords(recs []store.Record, tsv bool) error {
72		if flagJSON {
73			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
74			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
75			return enc.Encode(recs)
76		}
77		if tsv {
78			for _, r := range recs {
79				fmt.Printf("%s\t%s\t%s>%s\t%s\t%s\t%s\n",
80					r.ID, r.TS.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05"),
81					r.SourceLang, r.TargetLang, r.Engine,
82					escapeTSV(r.Input), escapeTSV(r.Output))
83			}
84			return nil
85		}
86		w := tabwriter.NewWriter(os.Stdout, 0, 2, 2, ' ', 0)
87		for _, r := range recs {
88			fmt.Fprintf(w, "%s→%s\t%s\t→ %s\t(%s)\n",
89				r.SourceLang, r.TargetLang, oneline(r.Input, 40), oneline(r.Output, 40), r.Engine)
90		}
91		return w.Flush()
92	}
93	
94	func escapeTSV(s string) string {
95		r := strings.NewReplacer("\t", " ", "\n", " ", "\r", " ")
96		return r.Replace(s)
97	}
98	
99	func oneline(s string, n int) string {
100		s = strings.ReplaceAll(strings.TrimSpace(s), "\n", " ")
101		rs := []rune(s)
102		if len(rs) <= n {
103			return s
104		}
105		return string(rs[:n-1]) + "…"
106	}
107
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/build.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
10	)
11	
12	// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
13	// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
14	// engine="smartauto" builds the smart default (dictionary for words, LLM for
15	// phrases, bidirectional); a named provider builds a single LLM engine.
16	func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
17		cfg := res.Cfg
18	
19		switch res.Engine {
20		case "auto":
21			return buildAutoChain(res)
22		case "smartauto":
23			// Needs an LLM provider for the word-miss fallback and phrase translation;
24			// without one, behave like the plain auto chain (google/dict).
25			if res.Provider == nil {
26				return buildAutoChain(res)
27			}
28			return smartAutoFromConfig(res), nil
29		case "google":
30			return googleFromConfig(cfg), nil
31		case "llm":
32			// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
33			if res.Provider != nil {
34				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
35			}
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
37		default:
38			// A named provider (e.g. "copilot", "ollama").
39			if res.Provider != nil {
40				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
41			}
42			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
43		}
44	}
45	
46	// buildAutoChain builds the "auto" fallback Chain over the providers/google named
47	// in chain.order. (google/dict engines otherwise join only via smart-auto or the
48	// TUI ^e cycle.)
49	func buildAutoChain(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
50		cfg := res.Cfg
51		var engines []engine.Engine
52		for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
53			switch {
54			case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
55				p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
56				engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
57			case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
58				engines = append(engines, googleFromConfig(cfg))
59			}
60			// "dict" is wired in via smart-auto / the TUI engine cycle.
61		}
62		if len(engines) == 0 {
63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
64		}
65		if len(engines) == 1 {
66			return engines[0], nil
67		}
68		return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
69	}
70	
71	// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
72	func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
73		if model == "" {
74			model = p.Model
75		}
76		return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
77			Name:      p.Name,
78			BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
79			Model:     model,
80			APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
81		})
82	}
83	
84	// googleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
85	func googleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
86		return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
87			Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
88			ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
89			UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
90			Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
91		})
92	}
93	
94	// dictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
95	// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
96	func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
97		if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
98			return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
99				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
100				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
101				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
102				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
103			})
104		}
105		var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
106		if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
107			apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
108				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
109				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
110				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
111				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
112			})
113		}
114		return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
115			Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
116			CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
117			EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
118			AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
119			Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
120			APIFallback:  apiFb,
121		})
122	}
123	
124	// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
125	// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
126	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
127	func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
128		cfg := res.Cfg
129		return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
130			CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
131			Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
132		})
133	}
134	
135	// smartAutoFromConfig builds the smart-auto default: a single word/term is a
136	// dictionary lookup (smart-dict, with an LLM fallback), and a phrase is an LLM
137	// translation via the auto chain (so phrases keep provider→google resilience).
138	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
139	func smartAutoFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
140		llm, err := buildAutoChain(res)
141		if err != nil {
142			// No chain engines (unlikely once a provider exists): fall back to the bare
143			// resolved provider so phrases can still translate.
144			llm = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
145		}
146		return engine.NewSmartAuto(smartDictFromConfig(res), llm)
147	}
148	
149	// learnEngineFromConfig builds the engine for learning mode: a bare LLM engine
150	// (the resolved provider/model), bypassing smart-auto/dictionary routing so a
151	// single word still gets the structured tutor treatment. Caller ensures Provider != nil.
152	func learnEngineFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
153		return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
154	}
155	
156	// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
157	// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
158	// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
159	// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
160	func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
161		cfg := res.Cfg
162		primaryName := primary.Name() // "auto", "smart-auto", "copilot", "google", …
163		if primaryName == "" {
164			primaryName = "auto"
165		}
166		set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
167		if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
168			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: googleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
169		}
170		if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
171			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: dictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
172			if res.Provider != nil {
173				set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: smartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
174			}
175		}
176		return set
177	}
178
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/ecdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"sync"
9	)
10	
11	type ecdictEntry struct {
12		Word, Phonetic, Translation, Definition string
13	}
14	
15	// ecdictDB queries the locally-built ECDICT SQLite (English → Chinese). Point
16	// lookups are on-disk (near-zero steady-state RAM). Opened lazily.
17	type ecdictDB struct {
18		path string
19		once sync.Once
20		err  error
21		db   *sql.DB
22	}
23	
24	func newEcdictDB(path string) *ecdictDB { return &ecdictDB{path: path} }
25	
26	func (e *ecdictDB) available() bool {
27		_, err := os.Stat(e.path)
28		return err == nil
29	}
30	
31	func (e *ecdictDB) open() error {
32		e.once.Do(func() {
33			if _, err := os.Stat(e.path); err != nil {
34				e.err = err
35				return
36			}
37			db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+e.path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")
38			if err != nil {
39				e.err = err
40				return
41			}
42			e.db = db
43		})
44		return e.err
45	}
46	
47	// lookup returns the exact (case-insensitive) entry, or nil if not found.
48	func (e *ecdictDB) lookup(ctx context.Context, word string) (*ecdictEntry, error) {
49		if err := e.open(); err != nil {
50			return nil, err
51		}
52		row := e.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
53			`SELECT word, phonetic, translation, definition FROM entries WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1`,
54			strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(word)))
55		var en ecdictEntry
56		switch err := row.Scan(&en.Word, &en.Phonetic, &en.Translation, &en.Definition); err {
57		case nil:
58			return &en, nil
59		case sql.ErrNoRows:
60			return nil, nil
61		default:
62			return nil, err
63		}
64	}
65	
66	// splitEcdict splits ECDICT translation/definition fields — their senses are
67	// joined by a literal backslash-n, not a real newline.
68	func splitEcdict(s string) []string {
69		var out []string
70		for _, p := range strings.Split(s, `\n`) {
71			if p = strings.TrimSpace(p); p != "" {
72				out = append(out, p)
73			}
74		}
75		return out
76	}
77
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/cedict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"os"
6		"regexp"
7		"sort"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// cedictLine matches: Traditional Simplified [pin1 yin1] /def1/def2/
13	var cedictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/\s*$`)
14	
15	type cedictEntry struct {
16		Trad, Simp, Pinyin string
17		Defs               []string
18	}
19	
20	// cedictIndex is an in-memory CC-CEDICT (Chinese → English), keyed on both the
21	// traditional and simplified forms. Loaded lazily on first lookup.
22	type cedictIndex struct {
23		path  string
24		once  sync.Once
25		err   error
26		byKey map[string][]*cedictEntry
27		keys  []string // sorted, for prefix suggestions
28	}
29	
30	func newCedictIndex(path string) *cedictIndex { return &cedictIndex{path: path} }
31	
32	func (ci *cedictIndex) load() error {
33		ci.once.Do(func() {
34			f, err := os.Open(ci.path)
35			if err != nil {
36				ci.err = err
37				return
38			}
39			defer f.Close()
40			ci.byKey = map[string][]*cedictEntry{}
41			sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
42			sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
43			for sc.Scan() {
44				line := sc.Text()
45				if strings.HasPrefix(line, "#") {
46					continue
47				}
48				m := cedictLine.FindStringSubmatch(line)
49				if m == nil {
50					continue
51				}
52				e := &cedictEntry{Trad: m[1], Simp: m[2], Pinyin: m[3]}
53				for _, d := range strings.Split(m[4], "/") {
54					if d = strings.TrimSpace(d); d != "" {
55						e.Defs = append(e.Defs, d)
56					}
57				}
58				ci.byKey[e.Simp] = append(ci.byKey[e.Simp], e)
59				if e.Trad != e.Simp {
60					ci.byKey[e.Trad] = append(ci.byKey[e.Trad], e)
61				}
62			}
63			ci.err = sc.Err()
64			ci.keys = make([]string, 0, len(ci.byKey))
65			for k := range ci.byKey {
66				ci.keys = append(ci.keys, k)
67			}
68			sort.Strings(ci.keys)
69		})
70		return ci.err
71	}
72	
73	func (ci *cedictIndex) lookup(word string) []*cedictEntry {
74		if ci.load() != nil {
75			return nil
76		}
77		return ci.byKey[word]
78	}
79	
80	// prefixSuggest returns up to n headwords beginning with word (excluding it).
81	// Chinese uses prefix, not edit distance (CJK edit distance is noisy).
82	func (ci *cedictIndex) prefixSuggest(word string, n int) []string {
83		if ci.load() != nil {
84			return nil
85		}
86		i := sort.SearchStrings(ci.keys, word)
87		var out []string
88		for ; i < len(ci.keys) && len(out) < n; i++ {
89			k := ci.keys[i]
90			if !strings.HasPrefix(k, word) {
91				break
92			}
93			if k != word {
94				out = append(out, k)
95			}
96		}
97		return out
98	}
99
```

12	
13	<!-- Use the exact section order: P1, P2, P3, P?, Done.
14	     The bundled scripts/todo-kanban.sh validator only inspects top-level
15	     `- [ ]` and `- ✅` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
16	     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
17	     ignored — feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
18	     machine readability. -->

20	## P1
21	
22	Likely next batch — items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.
23	
24	## P2
25	
26	Worth doing, no rush.
27	
28	## P3
29	
30	Someday / nice-to-have.
31	
32	- [ ] **[S] Automate the Homebrew formula bump on release** — the tap formula (`daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap` `Formula/translate.rb`) is currently hand-bumped (url + sha256) per tag. Add an on-tag GitHub Action (e.g. `dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula`, needs a cross-repo PAT) so a new `vX.Y.Z` auto-updates the formula. Keeps build-from-source (no goreleaser/cask). See [backlog/homebrew-distribution.md](backlog/homebrew-distribution.md).
33	- [ ] **[M] Table/wrapped-prose-aware bilingual mode** — the `--bilingual`/`-2` reading view interleaves a translation per blank-line-delimited prose block, which breaks on tabular output (`ls -l`, `kubectl get`, `git status`) and on hard-wrapped prose split by blank lines. Add column/table detection (skip or column-align) and soft-wrap coalescing; consider per-span parsing via the `charmbracelet/x/ansi` tokenizer already imported. See [backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md](backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md).
34	- [ ] **[M] lingua-go detection upgrade behind the `Detector` seam** — whatlanggo is light/fast but weak on short and mixed-script text; lingua-go is more accurate but heavy (embedded n-gram models → bigger binary, slower init). Swap behind the existing `internal/lang` interface only if short-text detection proves insufficient. Detection is mostly a fallback (Google returns the source, LLM returns `DetectedSource`), so keeping it light is defensible.
35	- [ ] **[S] MyMemory fallback engine** — trivial flat JSON (`responseData.translatedText`, `matches[]`); wire as an easy secondary free path after Google. Limits: 5k chars/day anon (50k with `&de=email`), 500 B max per `q`, no source auto-detect.
36	- [ ] **[M] Azure Translator engine behind the engine seam** — the only supported Microsoft path (needs an Azure subscription key). Keyless Bing scraping (`ttranslatev3` + transient `IG`/`IID`/token) is too fragile/ToS-risky for v1. Leave the engine interface ready; add when a key is available.
37	- [ ] **[S] Drop the whole-request `http.Client{Timeout: 60s}` on the streaming path** — in `internal/engine/llm.go`, `NewLLM` sets a client-wide `Timeout` that Go applies to the *entire* request including reading the streamed body, so a long translation is cut at 60s mid-stream. Now surfaced as `⚠ truncated` (see [pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md](pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md)) instead of silently accepted, but the long output still can't finish. Fix by relying on `ctx` deadlines for the streaming `Do()` (keep the short timeout only for `Available`/`Models` probes).
38	
39	## P?
40	
41	Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.
42	
43	- [ ] **[?/L] Ship prebuilt release binaries (goreleaser + GitHub Releases)** — cross-compile per OS/arch (pure-Go, no cgo — trivial) so hosts without a Go toolchain install via chezmoi `.chezmoiexternal` with a templated `{{ .chezmoi.os }}/{{ .chezmoi.arch }}` URL instead of `go install`. Also unlocks shipping the dictionary DB as a release asset. → [research](backlog/release-binaries.md)
44	- [ ] **[?/M] Bundle or prebuild the dictionary vs the 67 MB runtime `dict update`** — evaluate embedding a trimmed DB via `go:embed`, or shipping the built ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT as release assets, so first run isn't a big download/build. Weigh binary-size blowup vs first-run friction. → [research](backlog/dict-bundling.md)
45	- [ ] **[?/L] Publish the Raycast extension to the store** — the local-dev extension (`raycast/extension`) works via `npm run dev`; publishing publicly hits the "avoid requiring manual installs" review guideline (it depends on the `translate` binary). Evaluate private org store vs public, icon/screenshots/CHANGELOG, and graceful binary-not-found onboarding. → [research](backlog/raycast-extension.md)
46	- [ ] **[?/L] Local HTTP API server (`translate serve`) + OpenAPI/Swagger** — a persistent loopback service (`POST /v1/translate`, `/v1/define`, `GET /v1/history`, SSE stream) would give the Raycast extension (and other clients: Alfred, browser, editors) cleaner streaming, multi-client reuse, and no launchd-PATH resolution. Trade-off: daemon lifecycle + loopback security + concurrent history writes; latency win is small (LLM-dominated). Reuses `internal/engine`. → [research](backlog/api-server.md)
47	
48	## Done
49	
50	- ✅ [2026-07-22] [P3/S] `--stream` flag to force token streaming on non-TTY — piped consumers (`foo | translate`, the Raycast extension) can now force streaming; `cmd/root.go` ORs `--stream` into the TTY gate. Visible progressive output still depends on the provider (copilot-proxy buffers its claude `/v1/messages` responses; ollama streams). Part of the Raycast integration ([backlog/raycast-extension.md](backlog/raycast-extension.md), [docs/raycast-extension.md](docs/raycast-extension.md)).
51	- ✅ [2026-07-22] [—/S] TUI result pane: collapse excess blank lines between paragraphs — multi-paragraph translations (esp. terminal-scrape input) showed 2–4 blank rows per paragraph break while `^y` copy stayed clean. Root cause was `lipgloss.Style.Render` block-padding a multi-line block's blank lines out to the widest line's width, which the `SoftWrap` viewport then re-wrapped into several blank rows. Fixed by styling per line (`styles.renderTranslation`) + Unicode-aware blank-run collapsing (`collapseBlankLines`, handles `U+3000`/NBSP/CRLF). End-to-end guard drives the real Model with long wrapping paragraphs. See [pitfalls/tui-lipgloss-block-padding-inflates-blank-lines-in-viewport.md](pitfalls/tui-lipgloss-block-padding-inflates-blank-lines-in-viewport.md).
52	- ✅ [2026-07-22] [P2/S] Align `just install` with `~/.local/bin` — the `install` recipe now installs to `~/.local/bin` (first on PATH, where `go install` lands), with a `DIR=` override, instead of `~/.dotfiles/bin`; both paths agree so a stale copy can't shadow. See [pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md](pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md).
53	- ✅ [2026-07-20] [P3/M] Homebrew tap distribution — `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate` is live (build-from-source personal tap). Landed the two prereqs in v0.3.1 (injectable `var version` in `cmd/version.go` + MIT `LICENSE`) and published `Formula/translate.rb` to `daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap`. Verified install/`brew test` on macos_intel; no Gatekeeper/quarantine (source build). → [backlog/homebrew-distribution.md](backlog/homebrew-distribution.md).
54	- ✅ [2026-07-20] [P2/M] Bilingual `--bilingual`/`-2` immersive pipe mode + ANSI-strip-on-input — piped colored input is now stripped (`bitext.Strip`) before reaching the LLM; `--bilingual` keeps each original block (color intact) and prints the translation beneath prose blocks, dimmed on a TTY, echoing indented command/code blocks untranslated. New pure `internal/bitext` pkg (Split/Render). Per-word recoloring (approaches A/B) rejected as over-engineering. → [backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md](backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md).
55	- ✅ [2026-07-09] [P?/L] Wire `translate` into chezmoi dotfiles as an auto-installed go tool — go_tools ansible role added to dotfiles (commit 306bfb0): go install → ~/.local/bin, mise-gated, + cat_go upgrade path. Pending chezmoi apply on normal cadence.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/ecdict.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"sync"
9	)
10	
11	type ecdictEntry struct {
12		Word, Phonetic, Translation, Definition string
13	}
14	
15	// ecdictDB queries the locally-built ECDICT SQLite (English → Chinese). Point
16	// lookups are on-disk (near-zero steady-state RAM). Opened lazily.
17	type ecdictDB struct {
18		path string
19		once sync.Once
20		err  error
21		db   *sql.DB
22	}
23	
24	func newEcdictDB(path string) *ecdictDB { return &ecdictDB{path: path} }
25	
26	func (e *ecdictDB) available() bool {
27		_, err := os.Stat(e.path)
28		return err == nil
29	}
30	
31	func (e *ecdictDB) open() error {
32		e.once.Do(func() {
33			if _, err := os.Stat(e.path); err != nil {
34				e.err = err
35				return
36			}
37			db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+e.path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")
38			if err != nil {
39				e.err = err
40				return
41			}
42			e.db = db
43		})
44		return e.err
45	}
46	
47	// lookup returns the exact (case-insensitive) entry, or nil if not found.
48	func (e *ecdictDB) lookup(ctx context.Context, word string) (*ecdictEntry, error) {
49		if err := e.open(); err != nil {
50			return nil, err
51		}
52		row := e.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
53			`SELECT word, phonetic, translation, definition FROM entries WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1`,
54			strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(word)))
55		var en ecdictEntry
56		switch err := row.Scan(&en.Word, &en.Phonetic, &en.Translation, &en.Definition); err {
57		case nil:
58			return &en, nil
59		case sql.ErrNoRows:
60			return nil, nil
61		default:
62			return nil, err
63		}
64	}
65	
66	// splitEcdict splits ECDICT translation/definition fields — their senses are
67	// joined by a literal backslash-n, not a real newline.
68	func splitEcdict(s string) []string {
69		var out []string
70		for _, p := range strings.Split(s, `\n`) {
71			if p = strings.TrimSpace(p); p != "" {
72				out = append(out, p)
73			}
74		}
75		return out
76	}
77
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/build.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
10	)
11	
12	// buildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
13	// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
14	// engine="smartauto" builds the smart default (dictionary for words, LLM for
15	// phrases, bidirectional); a named provider builds a single LLM engine.
16	func buildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
17		cfg := res.Cfg
18	
19		switch res.Engine {
20		case "auto":
21			return buildAutoChain(res)
22		case "smartauto":
23			// Needs an LLM provider for the word-miss fallback and phrase translation;
24			// without one, behave like the plain auto chain (google/dict).
25			if res.Provider == nil {
26				return buildAutoChain(res)
27			}
28			return smartAutoFromConfig(res), nil
29		case "google":
30			return googleFromConfig(cfg), nil
31		case "llm":
32			// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
33			if res.Provider != nil {
34				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
35			}
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
37		default:
38			// A named provider (e.g. "copilot", "ollama").
39			if res.Provider != nil {
40				return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
41			}
42			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
43		}
44	}
45	
46	// buildAutoChain builds the "auto" fallback Chain over the providers/google named
47	// in chain.order. (google/dict engines otherwise join only via smart-auto or the
48	// TUI ^e cycle.)
49	func buildAutoChain(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
50		cfg := res.Cfg
51		var engines []engine.Engine
52		for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
53			switch {
54			case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
55				p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
56				engines = append(engines, llmFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
57			case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
58				engines = append(engines, googleFromConfig(cfg))
59			}
60			// "dict" is wired in via smart-auto / the TUI engine cycle.
61		}
62		if len(engines) == 0 {
63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
64		}
65		if len(engines) == 1 {
66			return engines[0], nil
67		}
68		return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
69	}
70	
71	// llmFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
72	func llmFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
73		if model == "" {
74			model = p.Model
75		}
76		return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
77			Name:      p.Name,
78			BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
79			Model:     model,
80			APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
81		})
82	}
83	
84	// googleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
85	func googleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
86		return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
87			Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
88			ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
89			UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
90			Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
91		})
92	}
93	
94	// dictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
95	// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
96	func dictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
97		if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
98			return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
99				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
100				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
101				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
102				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
103			})
104		}
105		var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
106		if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
107			apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
108				Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
109				Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
110				Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
111				Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
112			})
113		}
114		return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
115			Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
116			CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
117			EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
118			AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
119			Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
120			APIFallback:  apiFb,
121		})
122	}
123	
124	// smartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
125	// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
126	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
127	func smartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
128		cfg := res.Cfg
129		return engine.NewSmartDict(dictFromConfig(cfg), llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
130			CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
131			Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
132		})
133	}
134	
135	// smartAutoFromConfig builds the smart-auto default: a single word/term is a
136	// dictionary lookup (smart-dict, with an LLM fallback), and a phrase is an LLM
137	// translation via the auto chain (so phrases keep provider→google resilience).
138	// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
139	func smartAutoFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
140		llm, err := buildAutoChain(res)
141		if err != nil {
142			// No chain engines (unlikely once a provider exists): fall back to the bare
143			// resolved provider so phrases can still translate.
144			llm = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
145		}
146		return engine.NewSmartAuto(smartDictFromConfig(res), llm)
147	}
148	
149	// learnEngineFromConfig builds the engine for learning mode: a bare LLM engine
150	// (the resolved provider/model), bypassing smart-auto/dictionary routing so a
151	// single word still gets the structured tutor treatment. Caller ensures Provider != nil.
152	func learnEngineFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
153		return llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
154	}
155	
156	// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
157	// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
158	// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
159	// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
160	func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
161		cfg := res.Cfg
162		primaryName := primary.Name() // "auto", "smart-auto", "copilot", "google", …
163		if primaryName == "" {
164			primaryName = "auto"
165		}
166		set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
167		if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
168			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: googleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
169		}
170		if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
171			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: dictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
172			if res.Provider != nil {
173				set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: smartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
174			}
175		}
176		return set
177	}
178
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/config/resolve.go`

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Learn        bool // --learn forces learning mode on (implies pair)
21		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
22	}
23	
24	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
25	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
26	type Mode int
27	
28	const (
29		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
30		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
31	)
32	
33	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
34	type Resolved struct {
35		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
36		Target       string
37		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
38		Provider     *Provider
39		Model        string
40		Tier         string
41		Preset       string
42		Instructions string
43		Pair         bool
44		PairWith     string
45		Learn        bool
46		Stream       bool
47		Color        string
48		Debug        bool
49	
50		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
51		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
52		LiveTranslate bool
53		DebounceMs    int
54	
55		Cfg *Config
56	}
57	
58	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
59	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
60	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
61	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
62	
63	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
64	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
65		switch mode {
66		case ModeCLI:
67			return c.CLI
68		case ModeTUI:
69			return c.TUI
70		}
71		return nil
72	}
73	
74	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
75	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
76	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
77		if ov == nil {
78			return
79		}
80		if ov.Engine != nil {
81			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
82		}
83		if ov.Tier != nil {
84			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
85		}
86		if ov.Preset != nil {
87			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
88		}
89		if ov.Instructions != nil {
90			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
91		}
92		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
93			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
94		}
95		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
96			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
97		}
98		if ov.Pair != nil {
99			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
100		}
101		if ov.PairWith != nil {
102			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
103		}
104		if ov.Learn != nil {
105			g.Learn = *ov.Learn
106		}
107		if ov.Stream != nil {
108			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
109		}
110		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
111			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
112		}
113		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
114			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
115		}
116		if ov.Debug != nil {
117			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
122	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
123	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
124		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
125		// the on-disk config is untouched).
126		g := c.General
127		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
128		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
129	
130		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
131			if flag != "" {
132				return flag
133			}
134			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
135				return v
136			}
137			return cfgVal
138		}
139	
140		r := Resolved{
141			Cfg:           c,
142			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
143			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
144			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
145			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
146			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
147			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
148			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
149			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
150			Learn:         o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "",
151			Color:         g.Color,
152			Stream:        g.Stream,
153			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
154			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
155			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
156		}
157	
158		// Learning mode is inherently bidirectional; it reuses pair's home/away
159		// direction routing, so enabling learn enables pair.
160		if r.Learn {
161			r.Pair = true
162		}
163	
164		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
165		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
166		if provName == "" {
167			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
168				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
169			} else {
170				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
171			}
172		}
173		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
174	
175		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
176		// provider's tier model.
177		cfgModel := ""
178		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
179			cfgModel = *ov.Model
180		}
181		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
182		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
183			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
184		}
185		return r
186	}
187	
188	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
189	// else the first configured provider, else "".
190	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
191		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
192			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
193				return name
194			}
195		}
196		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
197			return c.Providers[0].Name
198		}
199		return ""
200	}
201
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/dictupdate.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6	
7		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
11	)
12	
13	func newDictCmd() *cobra.Command {
14		c := &cobra.Command{
15			Use:   "dict",
16			Short: "Manage the local bilingual dictionary (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT)",
17		}
18		c.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
19			Use:   "update [cedict|ecdict|all]",
20			Short: "Download/build the local dictionaries (~67 MB one-time)",
21			Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
22			RunE:  runDictUpdate,
23		})
24		return c
25	}
26	
27	func runDictUpdate(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
28		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
29		if err != nil {
30			return err
31		}
32		what := "all"
33		if len(args) == 1 {
34			what = args[0]
35		}
36		dir := engine.DictDir(cfg.Dict.Dir)
37		ctx := cmd.Context()
38		prog := func(s string) { fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "  "+s) }
39	
40		if what == "cedict" || what == "all" {
41			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "CC-CEDICT (Chinese→English):\n")
42			if err := engine.DownloadCedict(ctx, cfg.Dict.CedictURL, engine.CedictPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
43				return fmt.Errorf("cedict: %w", err)
44			}
45			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.CedictPath(dir))
46		}
47		if what == "ecdict" || what == "all" {
48			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ECDICT (English→Chinese, this takes a minute):\n")
49			if err := engine.BuildEcdictDB(ctx, cfg.Dict.EcdictURL, engine.EcdictDBPath(dir), prog); err != nil {
50				return fmt.Errorf("ecdict: %w", err)
51			}
52			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  -> %s\n", engine.EcdictDBPath(dir))
53		}
54		if what != "cedict" && what != "ecdict" && what != "all" {
55			return fmt.Errorf("unknown target %q (use cedict|ecdict|all)", what)
56		}
57		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "done. Dictionary mode (^e) is now bilingual zh↔en.")
58		return nil
59	}
60
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./raycast/extension/src/lib/translate.ts`

```text
1	import { getPreferenceValues } from "@raycast/api";
2	import { execFile, spawn } from "node:child_process";
3	import { promisify } from "node:util";
4	import { existsSync } from "node:fs";
5	import { homedir } from "node:os";
6	import { join } from "node:path";
7	
8	const pexecFile = promisify(execFile);
9	
10	/** The target languages offered in dropdowns. Keep in sync with the static
11	 *  dropdowns declared in package.json (command arguments can't read this at runtime). */
12	export const LANGS = [
13	  { title: "English", value: "en" },
14	  { title: "Chinese (Traditional)", value: "zh-TW" },
15	  { title: "Chinese (Simplified)", value: "zh-CN" },
16	  { title: "Japanese", value: "ja" },
17	  { title: "Korean", value: "ko" },
18	  { title: "Spanish", value: "es" },
19	  { title: "French", value: "fr" },
20	  { title: "German", value: "de" },
21	  { title: "Italian", value: "it" },
22	  { title: "Portuguese", value: "pt" },
23	];
24	
25	/** Mirrors internal/engine/engine.go TranslateResult (JSON tags). */
26	export interface Definition {
27	  definition: string;
28	  example?: string;
29	}
30	export interface Meaning {
31	  part_of_speech: string;
32	  definitions: Definition[];
33	  synonyms?: string[];
34	  antonyms?: string[];
35	}
36	export interface DictEntry {
37	  word: string;
38	  phonetic?: string;
39	  meanings?: Meaning[];
40	  source_url?: string;
41	}
42	export interface TranslateResult {
43	  translation: string;
44	  detected_source?: string;
45	  target: string;
46	  alternatives?: string[];
47	  notes?: string;
48	  confidence?: number;
49	  warnings?: string[];
50	  engine?: string;
51	  model?: string;
52	  dictionary?: DictEntry;
53	  suggestions?: string[];
54	}
55	
56	/** One row of `translate history --json`. */
57	export interface HistoryEntry {
58	  id: string;
59	  ts: string;
60	  source_lang: string;
61	  target_lang: string;
62	  engine?: string;
63	  model?: string;
64	  input: string;
65	  output: string;
66	}
67	
68	interface Prefs {
69	  binaryPath?: string;
70	  defaultTarget?: string;
71	  engine?: string;
72	  tier?: string;
73	}
74	
75	const PROBE_DIRS = [
76	  join(homedir(), ".local", "bin"),
77	  "/opt/homebrew/bin",
78	  "/usr/local/bin",
79	  join(homedir(), "go", "bin"),
80	];
81	
82	let cachedBin: string | undefined;
83	
84	/**
85	 * Resolve an ABSOLUTE path to the translate binary. Raycast runs under launchd
86	 * with a restricted PATH that does not inherit the shell rc, so a bare
87	 * `translate` throws ENOENT — we probe known install dirs (preference first).
88	 */
89	export function resolveBinary(): string {
90	  const prefs = getPreferenceValues<Prefs>();
91	  if (prefs.binaryPath && existsSync(prefs.binaryPath)) return prefs.binaryPath;
92	  if (cachedBin) return cachedBin;
93	  for (const dir of PROBE_DIRS) {
94	    const candidate = join(dir, "translate");
95	    if (existsSync(candidate)) {
96	      cachedBin = candidate;
97	      return candidate;
98	    }
99	  }
100	  throw new Error(
101	    "translate CLI not found. Set the binary path in extension preferences, or install it (just install / brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate).",
102	  );
103	}
104	
105	function baseEnv(): NodeJS.ProcessEnv {
106	  // Ensure the CLI can locate its config.toml (providers/API keys) under launchd.
107	  return { ...process.env, HOME: process.env.HOME ?? homedir() };
108	}
109	
110	export interface TranslateOptions {
111	  to?: string;
112	  from?: string;
113	  engine?: string;
114	  tier?: string;
115	  noHistory?: boolean;
116	  signal?: AbortSignal;
117	}
118	
119	export async function runTranslate(
120	  text: string,
121	  opts: TranslateOptions = {},
122	): Promise<TranslateResult> {
123	  const prefs = getPreferenceValues<Prefs>();
124	  const bin = resolveBinary();
125	  const args = [text, "--to", opts.to ?? prefs.defaultTarget ?? "en", "--json"];
126	  if (opts.from) args.push("--from", opts.from);
127	  const engine = opts.engine ?? prefs.engine;
128	  if (engine) args.push("--engine", engine);
129	  const tier = opts.tier ?? prefs.tier;
130	  if (tier) args.push("--tier", tier);
131	  if (opts.noHistory) args.push("--no-history");
132	
133	  const { stdout } = await pexecFile(bin, args, {
134	    timeout: 60_000, // LLM engines routinely exceed useExec's 10s default
135	    maxBuffer: 16 * 1024 * 1024,
136	    env: baseEnv(),
137	    signal: opts.signal, // cancel a superseded call when the user keeps typing
138	  });
139	  return JSON.parse(stdout) as TranslateResult;
140	}
141	
142	/**
143	 * `translate define <word> --json`. The top-level payload is a TranslateResult
144	 * whose `.dictionary` holds the entry (on a dict hit); on a miss it falls back
145	 * to an LLM definition in `.translation` with a `warnings[]` note.
146	 */
147	export async function runDefine(
148	  word: string,
149	  signal?: AbortSignal,
150	): Promise<TranslateResult> {
151	  const bin = resolveBinary();
152	  const { stdout } = await pexecFile(bin, ["define", word, "--json"], {
153	    timeout: 60_000,
154	    maxBuffer: 16 * 1024 * 1024,
155	    env: baseEnv(),
156	    signal,
157	  });
158	  return JSON.parse(stdout) as TranslateResult;
159	}
160	
161	/**
162	 * `translate history --json` (recent) or `translate history search <q> --json`.
163	 * Both return an array of HistoryEntry. Local + fast (no network).
164	 */
165	export async function runHistory(
166	  query?: string,
167	  limit = 200,
168	  signal?: AbortSignal,
169	): Promise<HistoryEntry[]> {
170	  const bin = resolveBinary();
171	  const q = query?.trim();
172	  const args = q
173	    ? ["history", "search", q, "--json"]
174	    : ["history", "--json", "--limit", String(limit)];
175	  const { stdout } = await pexecFile(bin, args, {
176	    timeout: 30_000,
177	    maxBuffer: 32 * 1024 * 1024,
178	    env: baseEnv(),
179	    signal,
180	  });
181	  const parsed = JSON.parse(stdout);
182	  return Array.isArray(parsed) ? (parsed as HistoryEntry[]) : [];
183	}
184	
185	/** Fire-and-forget TTS via `translate <text> --to <lang> --speak`. */
186	export function speak(text: string, to: string): void {
187	  const bin = resolveBinary();
188	  execFile(bin, [text, "--to", to, "--speak"], { env: baseEnv() }, () => {
189	    /* ignore — best-effort audio */
190	  });
191	}
192	
193	export interface StreamHandlers {
194	  onData: (chunk: string) => void;
195	  onDone: (code: number | null) => void;
196	  onError: (err: Error) => void;
197	}
198	
199	/**
200	 * Spawn `translate <text> --to <lang> [--engine] --stream` and stream plain-text
201	 * stdout chunks (the `--stream` flag forces streaming over a pipe). Returns a
202	 * cancel function that kills the child (call it on unmount). Whether output arrives
203	 * progressively depends on the provider — ollama streams; copilot-proxy buffers its
204	 * claude responses. `--no-history` avoids duplicating a history row the live view
205	 * may already have recorded.
206	 */
207	export function spawnTranslateStream(
208	  text: string,
209	  opts: TranslateOptions,
210	  h: StreamHandlers,
211	): () => void {
212	  const bin = resolveBinary();
213	  const args = [text, "--to", opts.to ?? "en", "--stream", "--no-history"];
214	  if (opts.engine) args.push("--engine", opts.engine);
215	  if (opts.from) args.push("--from", opts.from);
216	  if (opts.tier) args.push("--tier", opts.tier);
217	
218	  const child = spawn(bin, args, { env: baseEnv() });
219	  child.stdout?.setEncoding("utf8");
220	  child.stdout?.on("data", (d: Buffer | string) => h.onData(d.toString()));
221	  child.on("close", (code) => h.onDone(code));
222	  child.on("error", (err) => h.onError(err));
223	  return () => {
224	    if (!child.killed) child.kill("SIGTERM");
225	  };
226	}
227
```
</details></tool-use>
