<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./raycast/extension/src/lib/stream-view.tsx`

```text
1	import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
2	import { ActionPanel, Action, Detail } from "@raycast/api";
3	import { spawnTranslateStream } from "./translate";
4	
5	/** A pushed Detail that streams `translate … --stream` output token-by-token.
6	 *  Opt-in from the Translate view (⌘↵) — useful for long text and streaming
7	 *  engines; on a buffering provider it simply appears after first-token latency. */
8	export function StreamView({
9	  text,
10	  to,
11	  engine,
12	}: {
13	  text: string;
14	  to: string;
15	  engine?: string;
16	}) {
17	  const [md, setMd] = useState("");
18	  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
19	  const acc = useRef("");
20	
21	  useEffect(() => {
22	    acc.current = "";
23	    setMd("");
24	    setIsLoading(true);
25	    const cancel = spawnTranslateStream(
26	      text,
27	      { to, engine: engine || undefined },
28	      {
29	        onData: (chunk) => {
30	          acc.current += chunk;
31	          setMd(acc.current);
32	        },
33	        onDone: () => setIsLoading(false),
34	        onError: (err) => {
35	          acc.current += `\n\n> error: ${err.message}`;
36	          setMd(acc.current);
37	          setIsLoading(false);
38	        },
39	      },
40	    );
41	    return cancel;
42	  }, [text, to, engine]);
43	
44	  return (
45	    <Detail
46	      isLoading={isLoading}
47	      markdown={md || "…"}
48	      navigationTitle={`Translate → ${to}`}
49	      actions={
50	        <ActionPanel>
51	          <Action.CopyToClipboard title="Copy Translation" content={md} />
52	          <Action.Paste title="Paste Translation" content={md} />
53	        </ActionPanel>
54	      }
55	    />
56	  );
57	}
58
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/version.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"runtime/debug"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// version is injected at build time via
9	//
10	//	-ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.1"
11	//
12	// by packaged builds (the Homebrew formula, goreleaser, etc.). It overrides the
13	// build-info value below, which reports "(devel)" for a binary built from an
14	// extracted source tarball — no module version and no VCS metadata to read.
15	var version string
16	
17	// buildVersion reports a version string derived from the Go build info, so
18	// `translate --version` distinguishes a freshly-built dev binary from a stale
19	// installed one. For `go install <path>@<ver>` it shows the module version; for
20	// a local `go build`/`go install .` off a working tree it shows the VCS revision,
21	// a "+dirty" marker when the tree had uncommitted changes, and the build time.
22	// A build-time-injected version (above) takes precedence over both.
23	func buildVersion() string {
24		if version != "" {
25			return version
26		}
27		info, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()
28		if !ok {
29			return "(unknown; no build info)"
30		}
31		ver := info.Main.Version // e.g. "v0.1.0" or "(devel)"
32		var rev, when string
33		dirty := false
34		for _, s := range info.Settings {
35			switch s.Key {
36			case "vcs.revision":
37				rev = s.Value
38			case "vcs.time":
39				when = s.Value
40			case "vcs.modified":
41				dirty = s.Value == "true"
42			}
43		}
44		var b strings.Builder
45		b.WriteString(ver)
46		if rev != "" {
47			if len(rev) > 12 {
48				rev = rev[:12]
49			}
50			b.WriteString(" (" + rev)
51			if dirty {
52				b.WriteString("+dirty")
53			}
54			b.WriteString(")")
55		}
56		if when != "" {
57			b.WriteString(" commit " + when)
58		}
59		return b.String()
60	}
61	
62	// shortVersion is just the leading version token of buildVersion() (e.g.
63	// "v0.4.0" or "(devel)"), for stamping config.version and the outdated reminder.
64	func shortVersion() string {
65		v := buildVersion()
66		if i := strings.IndexByte(v, ' '); i > 0 {
67			return v[:i]
68		}
69		return v
70	}
71
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"os"
6	
7		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
11	)
12	
13	func newConfigCmd() *cobra.Command {
14		c := &cobra.Command{
15			Use:   "config",
16			Short: "Show or locate the configuration",
17		}
18		c.AddCommand(
19			&cobra.Command{
20				Use:   "path",
21				Short: "Print the config file path",
22				RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
23					fmt.Println(config.Path())
24					return nil
25				},
26			},
27			&cobra.Command{
28				Use:   "show",
29				Short: "Print the effective configuration as TOML",
30				RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
31					cfg, _, err := config.Load()
32					if err != nil {
33						return err
34					}
35					b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
36					if err != nil {
37						return err
38					}
39					_, err = os.Stdout.Write(b)
40					return err
41				},
42			},
43		)
44		return c
45	}
46
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
11	func newLangCmd() *cobra.Command {

13			Use:   "lang",
14			Short: "Language-code utilities",
15		}
16		c.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
17			Use:   "resolve <query>",
18			Short: "Fuzzy-resolve a language name/code (e.g. chinees -> zh)",
19			Args:  cobra.ExactArgs(1),
20			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
21				m, alts := lang.Resolve(args[0])
22				kind := "fuzzy"
23				if m.Exact {
24					kind = "exact"
25				}
26				fmt.Printf("%s -> %s (%s) [%s, score %.2f]\n", args[0], m.Code, m.Name, kind, m.Score)
27				for _, a := range alts {
28					fmt.Printf("  ~ %s (%s) %.2f\n", a.Code, a.Name, a.Score)
29				}
30				return nil
31			},
32		})
33		return c
34	}
35
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/speak.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tts"
16	)
17	
18	// speakOptions maps the [tts] config into tts.Options.
19	func speakOptions(cfg *config.Config) tts.Options {
20		t := cfg.TTS
21		return tts.Options{
22			Order:     t.Order,
23			Rate:      t.Rate,
24			Voices:    t.Voices,
25			GoogleURL: t.GoogleTTSURL,
26			UserAgent: t.UserAgent,
27			Timeout:   time.Duration(t.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
28			CacheDir:  t.CacheDir,
29			Player:    t.Player,
30		}
31	}
32	
33	// newSpeaker builds a Speaker from config.
34	func newSpeaker(cfg *config.Config) tts.Speaker { return tts.New(speakOptions(cfg)) }
35	
36	// tuiSpeaker returns a Speaker for the TUI, or nil when TTS is disabled (so the
37	// TUI can show a "tts disabled" hint instead of silently doing nothing).
38	func tuiSpeaker(cfg *config.Config) tts.Speaker {
39		if !cfg.TTS.Enabled {
40			return nil
41		}
42		return newSpeaker(cfg)
43	}
44	
45	// speakForeign resolves the preferred "副"/foreign language: the explicit config
46	// value, else the pair-mode "away" language (empty => Select derives it).
47	func speakForeign(cfg *config.Config, pairWith string) string {
48		if cfg.TTS.Foreign != "" {
49			return cfg.TTS.Foreign
50		}
51		return pairWith
52	}
53	
54	// shouldSpeak reports whether the one-shot CLI should speak this result.
55	func shouldSpeak(cfg *config.Config) bool {
56		return cfg.TTS.Enabled && (flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak)
57	}
58	
59	// speakResult speaks the foreign side of a one-shot translation result. Failures
60	// are reported to stderr and never abort the command.
61	func speakResult(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, srcText, srcLang, resLang, foreign string) {
62		ch, ok := tts.Select(tts.SelectInput{
63			SourceText: srcText,
64			SourceLang: srcLang,
65			ResultText: res.Translation,
66			ResultLang: resLang,
67			Foreign:    foreign,
68		})
69		if !ok {
70			return
71		}
72		applySpeakLangOverride(&ch)
73		if err := newSpeaker(cfg).Speak(ctx, ch.Text, ch.Lang); err != nil {
74			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: speech unavailable (%v)\n", err)
75		}
76	}
77	
78	// speakDefine speaks the looked-up head word itself (a `define --speak`
79	// pronounces the entry regardless of the foreign preference).
80	func speakDefine(ctx context.Context, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult) {
81		word := ""
82		if res.Dictionary != nil {
83			word = res.Dictionary.Word
84		}
85		if strings.TrimSpace(word) == "" {
86			word = res.Translation
87		}
88		if strings.TrimSpace(word) == "" {
89			return
90		}
91		code := ""
92		if flagSpeakLang != "" {
93			if lm, _ := lang.Resolve(flagSpeakLang); lm.Code != "" {
94				code = lm.Code
95			}
96		}
97		if code == "" {
98			code = lang.Detect(word)
99		}
100		if err := newSpeaker(cfg).Speak(ctx, word, code); err != nil {
101			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: speech unavailable (%v)\n", err)
102		}
103	}
104	
105	// applySpeakLangOverride forces the spoken language from --speak-lang when set.
106	func applySpeakLangOverride(ch *tts.Choice) {
107		if flagSpeakLang == "" {
108			return
109		}
110		if lm, _ := lang.Resolve(flagSpeakLang); lm.Code != "" {
111			ch.Lang = lm.Code
112		}
113	}
114	
115	// newSpeakCmd is the standalone `translate speak <text...>` subcommand.
116	func newSpeakCmd() *cobra.Command {
117		var langFlag, backend, voice string
118		c := &cobra.Command{
119			Use:   "speak <text...>",
120			Short: "Speak text aloud (free TTS: native offline, then Google)",
121			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
122			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
123				cfg, _, err := config.Load()
124				if err != nil {
125					return err
126				}
127				text := strings.Join(args, " ")
128	
129				code := langFlag
130				if code == "" {
131					code = flagSpeakLang
132				}
133				if code != "" {
134					if lm, _ := lang.Resolve(code); lm.Code != "" {
135						code = lm.Code
136					}
137				} else {
138					code = lang.Detect(text)
139				}
140	
141				opts := speakOptions(cfg)
142				if backend != "" {
143					opts.Order = []string{backend}
144				}
145				if voice != "" {
146					if opts.Voices == nil {
147						opts.Voices = map[string]string{}
148					}
149					opts.Voices[strings.ToLower(code)] = voice
150				}
151				if err := tts.New(opts).Speak(cmd.Context(), text, code); err != nil {
152					return fmt.Errorf("speak: %w", err)
153				}
154				return nil
155			},
156		}
157		c.Flags().StringVar(&langFlag, "lang", "", "language of the text (e.g. en, zh-TW); default: detect")
158		c.Flags().StringVar(&backend, "backend", "", "force a backend: native|google")
159		c.Flags().StringVar(&voice, "voice", "", "native voice override for this language")
160		return c
161	}
162
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/init.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"net/http"
7		"os"
8		"strings"
9		"time"
10	
11		"charm.land/huh/v2"
12		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
13	
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
17	)
18	
19	func newInitCmd() *cobra.Command {
20		return &cobra.Command{
21			Use:   "init",
22			Short: "Interactive configuration wizard (probes available providers)",
23			RunE:  runInit,
24		}
25	}
26	
27	func runInit(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
28		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
29		if err != nil {
30			return err
31		}
32	
33		// Probe providers so the form can show what's reachable.
34		copilotUp := probeProvider(cfg, "copilot")
35		ollamaUp := probeProvider(cfg, "ollama")
36		openrouterKey := os.Getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY") != ""
37	
38		engineChoice := cfg.General.Engine
39		target := cfg.General.DefaultTarget
40		live := cfg.General.LiveTranslate
41		preset := cfg.General.Preset
42		if preset == "" {
43			preset = engine.PresetContextual
44		}
45		instructions := cfg.General.Instructions
46		pair := cfg.General.Pair
47		pairWith := cfg.General.PairWith
48		if pairWith == "" {
49			pairWith = "en"
50		}
51	
52		form := huh.NewForm(
53			huh.NewGroup(
54				huh.NewSelect[string]().
55					Title("Default engine").
56					Description("Smart auto looks single words up in the dictionary and translates phrases with the LLM.").
57					Options(
58						huh.NewOption("Smart auto — dictionary for words, LLM for phrases (recommended)", "smartauto"),
59						huh.NewOption("Auto fallback chain (LLM providers → Google)", "auto"),
60						huh.NewOption(probeLabel("copilot-proxy", copilotUp), "copilot"),
61						huh.NewOption(probeLabel("Ollama (offline)", ollamaUp), "ollama"),
62						huh.NewOption("Google (free, translate only)", "google"),
63					).
64					Value(&engineChoice),
65	
66				huh.NewSelect[string]().
67					Title("Default target language").
68					Description("Type to filter; auto-detect is always the source.").
69					Options(targetLangOptions()...).
70					Height(12).
71					Filtering(true).
72					Value(&target),
73	
74				huh.NewSelect[string]().
75					Title("Translation style").
76					Description("How the LLM formats translations.").
77					Options(
78						huh.NewOption("contextual — translations across common senses", engine.PresetContextual),
79						huh.NewOption("concise — terse direct translation", engine.PresetConcise),
80						huh.NewOption("dictionary — translation + example sentences", engine.PresetDictionary),
81					).
82					Value(&preset),
83	
84				huh.NewText().
85					Title("Custom instructions (optional)").
86					Description("Extra guidance for the LLM, e.g. domain focus: quantitative finance + computer science.").
87					Value(&instructions),
88	
89				huh.NewConfirm().
90					Title("Live translate?").
91					Description("Auto-translate as you type. Off by default to avoid spamming the API.").
92					Value(&live),
93	
94				huh.NewConfirm().
95					Title("Bidirectional pair mode?").
96					Description("When you write in your target language, translate it to another instead. e.g. others → zh-TW, but zh → en.").
97					Value(&pair),
98	
99				huh.NewSelect[string]().
100					Title("…pair with (used only if pair mode is on)").
101					Description("Where your target-language input goes.").
102					Options(targetLangOptions()...).
103					Height(10).
104					Filtering(true).
105					Value(&pairWith),
106			),
107		)
108		if err := form.Run(); err != nil {
109			return err
110		}
111	
112		// Pair mode is a no-op when the "away" language equals the target (the default
113		// config ships target=en, pair_with=en). Force a distinct away so pair mode
114		// actually flips direction.
115		if pair && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, target)) {
116			old := pairWith
117			if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(target), "en") {
118				pairWith = "zh-TW"
119			} else {
120				pairWith = "en"
121			}
122			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "note: pair-with (%q) must differ from the target (%q); set pair-with=%s\n", old, target, pairWith)
123		}
124	
125		cfg.General.Engine = engineChoice
126		cfg.General.DefaultTarget = target
127		cfg.General.LiveTranslate = live
128		cfg.General.Preset = preset
129		cfg.General.Instructions = strings.TrimSpace(instructions)
130		cfg.General.Pair = pair
131		cfg.General.PairWith = pairWith
132		// Refresh copilot model ids to the verified-working recommendations (repairs
133		// configs written before a model id changed / was un-served by the proxy).
134		if p := cfg.ProviderByName("copilot"); p != nil {
135			p.Model, p.ModelFast, p.ModelMax = config.ModelDefault, config.ModelFast, config.ModelMax
136		}
137		if err := config.Save(cfg); err != nil {
138			return err
139		}
140	
141		fmt.Printf("Saved %s\n  engine=%s  target=%s  live=%v\n", config.Path(), engineChoice, target, live)
142		if engineChoice == "copilot" && !copilotUp {
143			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: copilot-proxy is not reachable — start it with `copilot-proxy start`.")
144		}
145		if engineChoice == "ollama" && !ollamaUp {
146			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: Ollama is not reachable — start it and `ollama pull llama3.2:3b`.")
147		}
148		if openrouterKey {
149			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "note: OPENROUTER_API_KEY detected — the openrouter provider is available.")
150		}
151		return nil
152	}
153	
154	// targetLangOptions builds the language dropdown options (name + code).
155	func targetLangOptions() []huh.Option[string] {
156		ls := lang.List()
157		opts := make([]huh.Option[string], 0, len(ls))
158		for _, l := range ls {
159			opts = append(opts, huh.NewOption(fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", l.Name, l.Code), l.Code))
160		}
161		return opts
162	}
163	
164	func probeLabel(name string, up bool) string {
165		if up {
166			return name + "  ● up"
167		}
168		return name + "  ○ down"
169	}
170	
171	func probeProvider(cfg *config.Config, name string) bool {
172		p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
173		if p == nil {
174			return false
175		}
176		return probeURL(strings.TrimRight(p.BaseURL, "/")+"/models", 1500*time.Millisecond)
177	}
178	
179	func probeURL(url string, timeout time.Duration) bool {
180		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), timeout)
181		defer cancel()
182		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
183		if err != nil {
184			return false
185		}
186		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
187		if err != nil {
188			return false
189		}
190		defer resp.Body.Close()
191		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
192	}
193
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:03:04Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./backlog/api-server.md`

```text
1	# Local HTTP API server (`translate serve`) + OpenAPI/Swagger
2	
3	**Status**: P? — needs a spike
4	**Effort**: L
5	**Related**: `TODO.md` P? · `internal/engine/` · [raycast-extension.md](raycast-extension.md) · [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	2026-07, surfaced while building the Raycast extension. Today every client (the
10	Raycast extension, script commands) **spawns the `translate` binary per call**. A
11	persistent local HTTP server (`translate serve`) with an OpenAPI/Swagger page was
12	proposed as "more usable." This doc records whether it is, and how.
13	
14	A related idea — folding `just raycast-dev` into the binary as `translate raycast
15	dev` — is **rejected** here (option C): it couples the Go CLI to the Node/`ray`
16	toolchain and the extension's on-disk location. Dev-tooling orchestration stays in
17	the `Justfile`. The useful subcommand is `translate serve`, not a `ray develop` wrapper.
18	
19	## Investigation
20	
21	- **Latency win is smaller than it looks.** Per-call spawn cost = config load +
22	  sqlite dict open + engine init. But LLM latency (~3–4 s) dominates, so a warm
23	  server mainly speeds up the **local dict/define** path, *not* LLM translation.
24	- **Streaming is the real win.** An HTTP server controls its own flushing → SSE
25	  (`text/event-stream`); clients consume via `fetch`/EventSource. Cleaner than the
26	  TTY / `--stream` / spawn path. (Caveat: upstream copilot-proxy still buffers claude
27	  `/v1/messages`, so visible streaming stays limited there regardless of transport.)
28	- **Multi-client + docs.** One loopback service → Raycast, Alfred, a browser
29	  extension, editor plugins, `curl`, a web UI. OpenAPI/Swagger documents it for all.
30	- **Sidesteps launchd-PATH** for request-time — the extension hits
31	  `http://127.0.0.1:PORT` instead of resolving/spawning the binary. But the *first*
32	  "start the daemon" still needs a spawn/LaunchAgent (PATH problem just moves).
33	- **Cheap to build.** Reuses `internal/engine` (pure Go, context-aware, TUI-free) as
34	  a thin HTTP adapter. stdlib `net/http` suffices (no framework dep). OpenAPI can be a
35	  hand-written `/openapi.json` + `go:embed`-ded Swagger UI, or generated
36	  (`swaggo/swag`, `danielgtaylor/huma`). Endpoints: `POST /v1/translate`,
37	  `POST /v1/define`, `GET /v1/history[?q=]`, SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`.
38	
39	## Options considered
40	
41	| Option | What | Verdict |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| A. Keep spawn (current) | extension spawns `translate --json` / `--stream` per call | simplest, works, no daemon; per-call startup cost is small vs LLM latency |
44	| B. `translate serve` (HTTP + SSE + OpenAPI) | persistent loopback service; extension opt-in via a "server URL" pref, spawn fallback | best for streaming + multi-client + no-PATH; adds daemon lifecycle + security surface |
45	| C. `translate raycast dev` subcommand | binary shells out to npm/`ray` | rejected — couples Go CLI to Node toolchain + extension location; keep in Justfile |
46	
47	## Current blocker / open questions
48	
49	- **Daemon lifecycle**: how to keep it running — menu-bar app, a `LaunchAgent`, or the
50	  extension auto-starting it (which reintroduces the PATH problem for the first spawn)?
51	- **Security**: loopback-only bind is mandatory (history is personal data); consider a
52	  token for `/v1/history`.
53	- **Concurrent history writes**: server + CLI both appending `history.jsonl` — the
54	  store must be concurrency-safe (append + lock).
55	- **Port**: fixed default (collision risk) vs dynamic (needs discovery). Lean to a
56	  config'd default (e.g. `4155`) + preference.
57	- **Is it worth it vs spawn?** Only if we want a local translation *service* (streaming
58	  UX, multiple clients, web UI). The raw-latency argument is weak (LLM-dominated).
59	
60	## Decision (if any)
61	
62	`2026-07 deferred` — spawn mode works and ships. Revisit if we want a streaming-first
63	UX, multiple clients, or a web UI. If built: start minimal (stdlib `net/http`,
64	loopback, SSE, hand-written OpenAPI + embedded Swagger), extension **opt-in** with a
65	spawn fallback so non-server users are unaffected.
66	
67	## References
68	
69	- stdlib `net/http`; `go:embed` for the Swagger UI assets
70	- OpenAPI generators: https://github.com/swaggo/swag · https://github.com/danielgtaylor/huma
71	- Raycast integration + the streaming caveat: [../docs/raycast-extension.md](../docs/raycast-extension.md)
72
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:03:04Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/store.go`

```text
1	// Package store persists translation history. v1 uses an append-only JSONL file
2	// with in-memory fuzzy search behind the Store interface; a sqlite+FTS5 backend
3	// can drop in later without changing callers.
4	package store
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// Record is one history entry (also the JSONL line shape).
12	type Record struct {
13		ID           string    `json:"id"`
14		TS           time.Time `json:"ts"`
15		SourceLang   string    `json:"source_lang"`
16		TargetLang   string    `json:"target_lang"`
17		Engine       string    `json:"engine"`
18		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
19		Input        string    `json:"input"`
20		Output       string    `json:"output"`
21		Alternatives []string  `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
22		Notes        string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
23		Favorite     bool      `json:"favorite,omitempty"`
24	}
25	
26	// Store is the history persistence interface.
27	type Store interface {
28		// Add assigns an ID and timestamp, persists the record, and returns it.
29		Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error)
30		// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
31		Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error)
32		// Search returns records fuzzy-matching query (over input+output), best first.
33		Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error)
34		// Close releases any resources.
35		Close() error
36	}
37
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"os"
6		"regexp"
7		"sort"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	)
11	
12	// cedictLine matches: Traditional Simplified [pin1 yin1] /def1/def2/
13	var cedictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`^(\S+)\s+(\S+)\s+\[([^\]]*)\]\s+/(.*)/\s*$`)
14	
15	type cedictEntry struct {
16		Trad, Simp, Pinyin string
17		Defs               []string
18	}
19	
20	// cedictIndex is an in-memory CC-CEDICT (Chinese → English), keyed on both the
21	// traditional and simplified forms. Loaded lazily on first lookup.
22	type cedictIndex struct {
23		path  string
24		once  sync.Once
25		err   error
26		byKey map[string][]*cedictEntry
27		keys  []string // sorted, for prefix suggestions
28	}
29	
30	func newCedictIndex(path string) *cedictIndex { return &cedictIndex{path: path} }
31	
32	func (ci *cedictIndex) load() error {
33		ci.once.Do(func() {
34			f, err := os.Open(ci.path)
35			if err != nil {
36				ci.err = err
37				return
38			}
39			defer f.Close()
40			ci.byKey = map[string][]*cedictEntry{}
41			sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
42			sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
43			for sc.Scan() {
44				line := sc.Text()
45				if strings.HasPrefix(line, "#") {
46					continue
47				}
48				m := cedictLine.FindStringSubmatch(line)
49				if m == nil {
50					continue
51				}
52				e := &cedictEntry{Trad: m[1], Simp: m[2], Pinyin: m[3]}
53				for _, d := range strings.Split(m[4], "/") {
54					if d = strings.TrimSpace(d); d != "" {
55						e.Defs = append(e.Defs, d)
56					}
57				}
58				ci.byKey[e.Simp] = append(ci.byKey[e.Simp], e)
59				if e.Trad != e.Simp {
60					ci.byKey[e.Trad] = append(ci.byKey[e.Trad], e)
61				}
62			}
63			ci.err = sc.Err()
64			ci.keys = make([]string, 0, len(ci.byKey))
65			for k := range ci.byKey {
66				ci.keys = append(ci.keys, k)
67			}
68			sort.Strings(ci.keys)
69		})
70		return ci.err
71	}
72	
73	func (ci *cedictIndex) lookup(word string) []*cedictEntry {
74		if ci.load() != nil {
75			return nil
76		}
77		return ci.byKey[word]
78	}
79	
80	// prefixSuggest returns up to n headwords beginning with word (excluding it).
81	// Chinese uses prefix, not edit distance (CJK edit distance is noisy).
82	func (ci *cedictIndex) prefixSuggest(word string, n int) []string {
83		if ci.load() != nil {
84			return nil
85		}
86		i := sort.SearchStrings(ci.keys, word)
87		var out []string
88		for ; i < len(ci.keys) && len(out) < n; i++ {
89			k := ci.keys[i]
90			if !strings.HasPrefix(k, word) {
91				break
92			}
93			if k != word {
94				out = append(out, k)
95			}
96		}
97		return out
98	}
99
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"compress/gzip"
5		"context"
6		"database/sql"
7		"encoding/csv"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strconv"
14		"strings"
15	
16		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
17	
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
19	)
20	
21	// DictDir returns the directory holding local dictionary data.
22	func DictDir(dir string) string {
23		if dir != "" {
24			return dir
25		}
26		return filepath.Join(xdgpath.DataDir(), "dict")
27	}
28	
29	// CedictPath / EcdictDBPath are the on-disk locations of the two data sources.
30	func CedictPath(dir string) string   { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "cedict_ts.u8") }
31	func EcdictDBPath(dir string) string { return filepath.Join(DictDir(dir), "ecdict.db") }
32	
33	const ecdictSchema = `CREATE TABLE entries(
34	  word TEXT, word_lc TEXT, phonetic TEXT, translation TEXT,
35	  definition TEXT, pos TEXT, frq INTEGER, exchange TEXT);`
36	
37	// DownloadCedict fetches the gzipped CC-CEDICT, decompresses it, and writes the
38	// plain text file atomically (~9.4 MB).
39	func DownloadCedict(ctx context.Context, url, dst string, prog func(string)) error {
40		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dst), 0o700); err != nil {
41			return err
42		}
43		if prog != nil {
44			prog("downloading CC-CEDICT…")
45		}
46		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, url, nil)
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
51		if err != nil {
52			return err
53		}
54		defer resp.Body.Close()
55		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
56			return fmt.Errorf("cedict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
57		}
58		gz, err := gzip.NewReader(resp.Body)
59		if err != nil {
60			return err
61		}
62		defer gz.Close()
63	
64		tmp := dst + ".tmp"
65		f, err := os.Create(tmp)
66		if err != nil {
67			return err
68		}
69		if _, err := io.Copy(f, gz); err != nil {
70			f.Close()
71			os.Remove(tmp)
72			return err
73		}
74		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
75			os.Remove(tmp)
76			return err
77		}
78		return os.Rename(tmp, dst)
79	}
80	
81	// BuildEcdictDB downloads the ECDICT CSV (~63 MB, MIT) and builds a queryable
82	// SQLite database (~80-100 MB) from it, atomically. Streams the CSV so it never
83	// holds the whole file in memory.
84	func BuildEcdictDB(ctx context.Context, csvURL, dbPath string, prog func(string)) (err error) {
85		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(dbPath), 0o700); err != nil {
86			return err
87		}
88		if prog != nil {
89			prog("downloading ECDICT CSV (~63 MB)…")
90		}
91		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, csvURL, nil)
92		if err != nil {
93			return err
94		}
95		resp, err := http.DefaultClient.Do(req)
96		if err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		defer resp.Body.Close()
100		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
101			return fmt.Errorf("ecdict download: http %d", resp.StatusCode)
102		}
103	
104		tmp := dbPath + ".tmp"
105		_ = os.Remove(tmp)
106		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+tmp+"?_pragma=journal_mode(off)&_pragma=synchronous(off)")
107		if err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110		// On any error, close and remove the partial db.
111		defer func() {
112			if err != nil {
113				db.Close()
114				os.Remove(tmp)
115			}
116		}()
117	
118		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, ecdictSchema); err != nil {
119			return err
120		}
121		if prog != nil {
122			prog("importing entries…")
123		}
124		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
125		if err != nil {
126			return err
127		}
128		stmt, err := tx.PrepareContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO entries(word,word_lc,phonetic,translation,definition,pos,frq,exchange) VALUES(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)`)
129		if err != nil {
130			return err
131		}
132	
133		r := csv.NewReader(resp.Body)
134		r.FieldsPerRecord = -1 // tolerate ragged rows
135		r.LazyQuotes = true
136		if _, err = r.Read(); err != nil { // header row
137			return err
138		}
139		n := 0
140		for {
141			if err = ctx.Err(); err != nil {
142				return err
143			}
144			rec, rerr := r.Read()
145			if rerr == io.EOF {
146				break
147			}
148			if rerr != nil || len(rec) < 4 {
149				continue
150			}
151			frq := 0
152			if len(rec) > 9 {
153				frq, _ = strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(rec[9]))
154			}
155			pos, exchange := "", ""
156			if len(rec) > 4 {
157				pos = rec[4]
158			}
159			if len(rec) > 10 {
160				exchange = rec[10]
161			}
162			if _, err = stmt.ExecContext(ctx, rec[0], strings.ToLower(rec[0]), rec[1], rec[3], rec[2], pos, frq, exchange); err != nil {
163				return err
164			}
165			n++
166		}
167		if err = tx.Commit(); err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err = db.ExecContext(ctx, `CREATE INDEX idx_word_lc ON entries(word_lc)`); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if err = db.Close(); err != nil {
174			return err
175		}
176		if prog != nil {
177			prog(fmt.Sprintf("built %d entries", n))
178		}
179		return os.Rename(tmp, dbPath)
180	}
181
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
267	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
268	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
269		if pair && away != "" {
270			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
271			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
272			return t
273		}
274		return home
275	}

277	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
278	func clip(s string, n int) string {
279		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
280		r := []rune(s)
281		if len(r) > n {
282			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
283		}
284		return s
285	}

287	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
288	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).

290		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
291			return nil
292		}
293		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
294			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
295		if err != nil {
296			return nil
297		}
298		return f
299	}

306		}
307		// Pair (and learn) mode anchors BOTH languages from config: Target is the home

312		// always auto-detected in pair mode too, so skip the whole override.
```

 `./cmd/root.go`

```text
460	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
461	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
462	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
463		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
464		// Stream when the config asks for it on a TTY, or when --stream forces it (a
465		// piped consumer like the Raycast extension). --json/--learn are structured
466		// and never stream.
467		stream := (flagStream || (streamPref && stdoutTTY)) && !flagJSON && !learn
468	
469		req := engine.Request{
470			Text:     text,
471			Source:   source,
472			Target:   target,
473			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
474			Stream:   stream,
475			Preset:   preset,
476			Extra:    instructions,
477			Pair:     pair,
478			PairHome: pairHome,
479			PairAway: pairAway,
480			Learn:    learn,
481		}
482		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
483		if err != nil {
484			return nil, err
485		}
486	
487		// streamed tracks whether any visible token actually reached stdout. We may
488		// request streaming (stream == true) yet the engine that answers ignores it and
489		// returns a single terminal result with zero tokens — every non-streaming engine
490		// does (the dictionary/smart-dict single-word path, google, a provider that fell
491		// back to a non-streaming completion). In that case nothing was printed during
492		// draining, so the final result must still be emitted below.
493		var onTok func(string)
494		streamed := false
495		if stream {
496			onTok = func(t string) {
497				if t != "" {
498					streamed = true
499				}
500				fmt.Print(t)
501			}
502		}
503		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
504		if err != nil {
505			if streamed {
506				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial streamed line before the error surfaces
507			}
508			return nil, err
509		}
510	
511		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
512		// failed and why, and how to switch.
513		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
514			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
515		}
516		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
517			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
518		}
519
```

Looking at the dependencies, there's no HTTP framework, JSON-schema library, or OpenAPI tooling—just the standard library's net/http used as a client for external services, along with cobra for CLI, bubbletea/huh/lipgloss for the TUI, sqlite for storage, and go-toml for configuration. The Raycast extension spawns the binary via execFile/spawn and probes for it in standard directories, with no HTTP server URL logic anywhere. The only HTTP references in the codebase are npm registry URLs in package-lock.json. I've also noted that the copilot-proxy buffers responses from Claude's API when streaming to the extension.

The engine exposes a public Translate entry point that returns channels of Chunk objects, which is streaming-native and relevant for SSE implementation. I have the directory structure mapped out and enough context to move forward.

---

# Exploration Report: `translate serve` API server + MCP feasibility

## 1. MCP-related code, dependencies, or mentions — NONE in the actual project

There is **no MCP code, no MCP dependency, and no MCP mention in any source, doc, backlog, TODO, README, or manifest** in this repo. I searched case-insensitively for `mcp`, `modelcontextprotocol`, and `model context protocol` across `*.go`, `*.mod`, `*.md`, `*.ts`, `*.tsx`, `*.json`, `*.yaml`, `*.yml`.

Every hit falls into one of two non-project buckets:

- **Agent chat transcripts** under `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/.specstory/history/*.md` — SpecStory-recorded coding sessions, not compiled or shipped. The only substantive one is your own kickoff note in `2026-07-22_10-01-17Z-at-backlog-api-server.md:10`: `(然後看看是不是順便把MCP server的功能也夾帶進去)` ("also see whether to smuggle in MCP server functionality too"), plus that transcript restating this very exploration task. Others are incidental: a `marimo[recommended,mcp]` Python tool-install table from an unrelated dotfiles session, and boilerplate WebFetch/WebSearch strings mentioning "an MCP-provided fetch tool" or Raycast's "Model Context Protocol" manual page.
- **Agent plan artifacts** under `.claude/plans/` — same category, not code.

`grep` restricted to code/docs/backlog/README/go.mod (excluding `.specstory/` and `.claude/plans/`) returns **zero matches**. Confirmed: no MCP surface exists today. Any MCP server would be greenfield.

## 2. Go dependencies (`go.mod`)

Module `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, `go 1.26.4`. File: `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/go.mod`.

**Direct requires:**
- CLI: `github.com/spf13/cobra v1.10.2` (+ `spf13/pflag` indirect)
- TUI: `charm.land/bubbles/v2`, `charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `charm.land/huh/v2`, `charm.land/lipgloss/v2`, `github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi`
- Config: `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2 v2.4.3`
- Storage: `modernc.org/sqlite v1.53.0` (pure-Go, cgo-free)
- Lang/fuzzy: `github.com/abadojack/whatlanggo`, `github.com/agnivade/levenshtein`, `github.com/sahilm/fuzzy`
- Misc: `github.com/atotto/clipboard`, `golang.org/x/term`

**Relevant to your question — what is NOT present:**
- **No HTTP framework** (no gin/echo/chi/fiber/gorilla). Only the **stdlib `net/http`**, and only as an outbound *client* — in `internal/engine/{google,dict,dictdata,llm}.go`, `internal/tts/google.go`, and `cmd/init.go`. There is **no `http.Server`, no `ListenAndServe`, no server-side routing anywhere** (verified by grep — the only `text/event-stream` hits are the LLM client setting an `Accept` header and a test server).
- **No JSON-schema library**, no OpenAPI/Swagger generator (no `swaggo/swag`, no `danielgtaylor/huma`), no SSE library.

So both an HTTP API server and an MCP server would start from stdlib. The `api-server.md` backlog explicitly plans to stay on stdlib `net/http`.

## 3. Documented decisions & open questions for the API server

### `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/backlog/api-server.md` — "Local HTTP API server (`translate serve`) + OpenAPI/Swagger"
**Status: P? — deferred (2026-07), Effort L.** Key documented positions:

- **Decision (deferred):** spawn mode ships and works; revisit only if you want a streaming-first UX, multiple clients, or a web UI. *If built:* start minimal — stdlib `net/http`, loopback-only bind, SSE, hand-written `/openapi.json` + `go:embed`-ded Swagger UI — and make the extension **opt-in with a spawn fallback** so non-server users are unaffected.
- **Proposed endpoints:** `POST /v1/translate`, `POST /v1/define`, `GET /v1/history[?q=]`, SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`.
- **Rationale:** latency win is weak (LLM ~3–4 s dominates; warm server only speeds the local dict/define path). The **real wins are streaming (server controls its own flushing → clean SSE), multi-client reuse, and OpenAPI docs.** Reuses `internal/engine` (pure Go, context-aware, TUI-free) as a thin HTTP adapter — "cheap to build."
- **Explicitly rejected (option C):** a `translate raycast dev` subcommand that shells to npm/`ray` — keeps the Go CLI decoupled from the Node toolchain; dev orchestration stays in the `Justfile`.
- **Open questions / blockers:** (a) **daemon lifecycle** — menu-bar app vs `LaunchAgent` vs extension auto-start (auto-start reintroduces the launchd-PATH problem for the *first* spawn); (b) **security** — loopback-only bind mandatory since history is personal data; consider a token for `/v1/history`; (c) **concurrent history writes** — server + CLI both appending `history.jsonl` needs a concurrency-safe append+lock; (d) **port** — leans to a config'd default (e.g. `4155`) + preference over dynamic discovery.
- **Streaming caveat carried through:** upstream copilot-proxy still buffers claude `/v1/messages`, so visible streaming stays limited there regardless of transport.

### `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/TODO.md` (P? section, line 46)
One-line index entry mirroring the above: `[?/L] Local HTTP API server (translate serve) + OpenAPI/Swagger` — persistent loopback service giving cleaner streaming, multi-client reuse, no launchd-PATH resolution; trade-off = daemon lifecycle + loopback security + concurrent history writes; latency win small (LLM-dominated); reuses `internal/engine`.

### `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/backlog/raycast-extension.md`
"Publish the Raycast extension to the store" (P? — local dev shipped, publish deferred). Not the API server, but relevant: notes the extension currently depends on the user-installed binary (a store-review headwind), and defers public publication.

## 4. How the Raycast extension invokes the binary — pure `spawn`/`execFile`, NO HTTP

Extension lives at `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/raycast/extension/`. The single wrapper is `src/lib/translate.ts`.

- **Binary resolution** (`resolveBinary()`, lines 89–103): returns an **absolute** path. Preference `binaryPath` first, else probes `PROBE_DIRS = [~/.local/bin, /opt/homebrew/bin, /usr/local/bin, ~/go/bin]` (lines 75–80), caches the hit, throws with install instructions on miss. This exists specifically because Raycast runs under launchd with a restricted PATH.
- **Invocation:** `runTranslate` (119–140) builds `[text, "--to", target, "--json", ...]` (optional `--from`, `--engine`, `--tier`, `--no-history`) and calls promisified `execFile` with `timeout: 60_000`, `maxBuffer: 16 MiB`, `env: baseEnv()`, and an `AbortSignal`. It `JSON.parse`s stdout into `TranslateResult` (interface at 42–54, explicitly "Mirrors internal/engine/engine.go TranslateResult"). `baseEnv()` (105–108) passes `HOME` through so the CLI finds `~/.config/translate/config.toml`.
- **Other calls:** `runDefine` → `["define", word, "--json"]`; `runHistory` → `["history","--json","--limit",N]` or `["history","search",q,"--json"]`; `speak` → fire-and-forget `[text,"--to",to,"--speak"]`.
- **Streaming:** `spawnTranslateStream` (207–226) uses `node:child_process` `spawn` with `[text,"--to",to,"--stream","--no-history",...]`, sets stdout to utf8, appends `data` chunks, returns a cancel fn that SIGTERMs the child. Consumed by `src/lib/stream-view.tsx` (`StreamView`, opt-in `⌘↵`), which accumulates chunks into `Detail.markdown`.

**Server-URL / HTTP status: NONE.** No `fetch()`, `EventSource`, `serverUrl`/`server_url` preference, `127.0.0.1`, or `localhost` anywhere in the extension source. The only `http(s)://` hits are npm registry URLs (`registry.npmmirror.com`) inside `package-lock.json`. Preferences declared in `package.json` (72–118) are exactly `binaryPath`, `defaultTarget`, `engine`, `tier`, `liveDebounceMs` (plus a per-command `prefill` dropdown) — **no server/URL/port preference exists yet.** This confirms the backlog's "extension opt-in via a 'server URL' pref" is entirely unbuilt.

Commands (`package.json` 11–71): `translate` (view), `translate-selection` (no-view), `define` (view), `history` (view). Deps: `@raycast/api ^1.103.6`, `@raycast/utils ^2.2.2`.

## 5. `docs/raycast-extension.md` — the copilot-proxy streaming caveat

File: `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/docs/raycast-extension.md`. This is present-tense reference reading. The streaming caveat (in "How Raycast extensions work," lines 39–51) states:

- The CLI's **`--stream` flag** forces token streaming even when stdout is piped (Raycast always pipes; without it the CLI treats non-TTY as buffered). A streaming `Detail` view can spawn `translate … --stream` and append stdout chunks into React state bound to `Detail.markdown`.
- **The caveat:** "visible progressive output depends on the provider — ollama streams; **copilot-proxy currently buffers its claude `/v1/messages` responses**, so the result appears after first-token latency." The default live view therefore uses `--json` (buffered, structured), which returns fast enough.
- It also documents *why* `execFile` is used over Raycast's `useExec` hook: `useExec` buffers with a 10 s default timeout that LLM engines exceed (and `timeout: 0` is coerced back to 10000).

This same caveat is repeated in `backlog/api-server.md:26-27` (SSE won't fix it — it's upstream) and in the TODO "Done" entry for `--stream` (line 50). **Takeaway for your design: SSE/MCP streaming transport will not make copilot-proxy claude responses stream visibly — the buffering is upstream in the proxy, independent of your transport.**

## 6. Repository layout (top level of `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui`)

**Go source:**
- `main.go` — entry point (thin; calls `cmd`).
- `cmd/` — Cobra commands: `root.go` (27k, the main translate command + flags incl. `--stream`, `--json`), `define.go`, `history.go`, `speak.go`, `init.go`, `config.go`, `lang.go`, `dictupdate.go`, `build.go`, `version.go`. **No `serve.go` exists** — that is where a `translate serve` command would be added.
- `internal/` — packages: `engine/` (translation engines + chain/failover; **streaming-native**, exposes `Chain.Translate(ctx, Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)` and `Drain`, plus `TranslateResult`/`DictEntry` types — the natural core for both an HTTP adapter and an MCP tool), `store/` (sqlite history), `config/`, `lang/`, `bitext/` (bilingual mode), `tts/`, `tui/`, `state/`, `debug/`, `xdgpath/`.

**Raycast integration:**
- `raycast/extension/` — the TypeScript `@raycast/api` extension (source in `src/`, `src/lib/`).
- `raycast/script-commands/` — three bash Script Commands (`translate-text.sh`, `translate-and-copy.sh`, `define-word.sh`).

**Maintainer-facing docs & metadata:**
- `backlog/` — long-form research notes (`api-server.md`, `raycast-extension.md`, `bilingual-immersive-mode.md`, `homebrew-distribution.md`, `dict-bundling.md`, `release-binaries.md`, `chezmoi-go-tool-integration.md`, `inbox.md`, `README.md` index). Never shipped to users.
- `docs/` — user-facing reference; currently just `raycast-extension.md`.
- `pitfalls/` — symptom-searchable troubleshooting knowledge base.
- `TODO.md` — single backlog index (P1/P2/P3/P?/Done). `AGENTS.md` — agent workflow. `README.md` (11k), `Justfile` (build/dev recipes incl. `raycast-dev`, `raycast-scripts`), `LICENSE` (MIT), `go.mod`/`go.sum`, `skills-lock.json`.

**Agent/tooling (not shipped):** `.agents/`, `.claude/`, `.specstory/`, `.gitignore`. A built `translate` binary (22 MB) sits at the root.

The README has no `serve`/`swagger`/`openapi`/`api server` content (the only `serve` hit is prose about claude models being "served only via the proxy").

---

### Bottom line for your design question
- MCP is **100% greenfield** — nothing to reuse or conflict with.
- The HTTP API server is **documented and deferred (P?/L)** with endpoints, security, lifecycle, and concurrency questions already scoped in `backlog/api-server.md`; no server code exists yet (`net/http` is client-only).
- The extension is **spawn-only** with no HTTP/server-URL surface — adding a server means adding a new opt-in preference and a fetch/EventSource path, exactly as the backlog anticipates.
- `internal/engine` is already **streaming-native and TUI-free** (channel-of-`Chunk` API), so it can back an HTTP handler *and* an MCP tool from the same binary with minimal glue.
- The copilot-proxy buffering caveat is transport-independent — factor it into any streaming promises for both HTTP/SSE and MCP.

# `translate` CLI architecture — findings for adding `translate serve`

## 0. Orientation
- **Module path**: `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate` (from `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/go.mod`)
- **Go version**: `go 1.26.4`
- **There is a pre-existing backlog design doc** for exactly this task: `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/backlog/api-server.md`. It proposes stdlib `net/http`, loopback-only bind, SSE endpoint, hand-written OpenAPI + `go:embed` Swagger UI, endpoints `POST /v1/translate`, `POST /v1/define`, `GET /v1/history[?q=]`, `GET /v1/translate/stream` (SSE), default port `4155`, and flags concurrent history writes as an open concern. Read it first — it's the spec skeleton.

---

## 1. CLI framework and root command

**Framework: `github.com/spf13/cobra` v1.10.2** (with `spf13/pflag`). Confirmed in `go.mod` and every `cmd/*.go`.

- **Entry point**: `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/main.go` — sets up a `signal.NotifyContext` (Ctrl-C/SIGTERM cancels in-flight LLM work) and calls `cmd.Execute(ctx)`.
- **Root command + subcommand registration**: `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/cmd/root.go`.
  - `NewRootCmd()` builds the root `*cobra.Command` (`Use: "translate [text...]"`, `Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs` so bare text falls through to translation while exact subcommands still route).
  - Subcommands are registered on line 95:
    ```go
    root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
    ```
    **To add `serve`, add `newServeCmd()` to this call** and create a new `cmd/serve.go` following the `newXxxCmd() *cobra.Command` factory pattern every other file uses.
  - `Execute(ctx)` (line 100) stamps the version then runs `NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)` — so `cmd.Context()` inside any RunE carries the cancellable context.

The root command itself (no subcommand) dispatches in `runRoot` (root.go:137): args → one-shot CLI; piped stdin (non-TTY) → one-shot; TTY + no args → Bubble Tea TUI.

---

## 2. Existing subcommands and their flags

Persistent flags (root.go:73–92) apply to **all** commands. Relevant globals are package-level vars in `cmd/root.go` (`flagJSON`, `flagStream`, etc.):

| Flag | Var | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--to,-t` `--from,-f` | `flagTo/flagFrom` | languages |
| `--model` `--provider` `--engine` `--tier` `--preset` `--instructions` | resp. | engine selection |
| `--pair` `--pair-with` `--learn` `--bilingual,-2` `--bilingual-mode` | resp. | modes |
| **`--json`** | `flagJSON` | "emit the full result as JSON" |
| **`--stream`** | `flagStream` | "force token streaming to stdout even when it is not a TTY (for piped consumers, e.g. the Raycast extension)" |
| `--no-history` | `flagNoHistory` | suppress history write |
| `--debug` `--speak,-s` `--speak-lang` | resp. | |

Subcommands (each in its own `cmd/*.go`):

- **`config`** (`cmd/config.go`): `config path`, `config show` (marshals effective config to TOML).
- **`lang`** (`cmd/lang.go`): `lang resolve <query>`.
- **`define <word>`** (`cmd/define.go`): flags `--plain`, `--smart`; honors `--json`. Dictionary lookup.
- **`history`** (`cmd/history.go`): `history` and `history search <query>`; flags `--limit` (default 20), `--tsv`; honors `--json`.
- **`init`** (`cmd/init.go`): interactive `huh` wizard.
- **`dict update [cedict|ecdict|all]`** (`cmd/dictupdate.go`): downloads/builds dictionaries.
- **`speak <text...>`** (`cmd/speak.go`): flags `--lang`, `--backend`, `--voice`.

### `--json` implementation
JSON output is `encoding/json` with `enc.SetIndent("", "  ")` encoding an `*engine.TranslateResult` (translate/define) or `[]store.Record` (history). Three call sites, all identical pattern:
- `cmd/root.go:521` (oneShot): `enc.Encode(res)` where `res *engine.TranslateResult`.
- `cmd/define.go:53`.
- `cmd/history.go:72` (printRecords).

The JSON shape for translate/define is `engine.TranslateResult` — defined in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/engine.go:72`. Key serialized fields: `translation`, `detected_source`, `target`, `alternatives`, `notes`, `confidence`, `warnings`, `engine`, `model`, `dictionary` (nested `DictEntry`→`meanings`→`definitions`), `learn` (nested `LearnResult`), `suggestions`. Note several transient fields are `json:"-"` (`Truncated`, `Bilingual`, `SuggestDistance`). **This same struct is a ready-made HTTP JSON response body.** The history JSON shape is `store.Record` (store.go:12).

### `--stream` implementation
Streaming is entirely in `oneShot` (`cmd/root.go:462`). Logic (root.go:467):
```go
stream := (flagStream || (streamPref && stdoutTTY)) && !flagJSON && !learn
```
i.e. stream when `--stream` forces it OR (config `stream=true` AND stdout is a TTY); never when `--json` or learn/bilingual (those are structured). It sets `Request.Stream`, then drains via `engine.Drain(ch, onTok)` where `onTok` does `fmt.Print(t)` per token. The engine emits `Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken}` values on a channel; `ChunkDone` carries the final `*TranslateResult`. **For SSE you would reuse this exact channel/`Drain` mechanism** — see §7.

---

## 3. Configuration loading

Package `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/config.go`.

- **Format**: TOML via `github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2`.
- **Location**: `config.Path()` returns `$TRANSLATE_CONFIG` if set, else `xdgpath.ConfigFile()` = `~/.config/translate/config.toml` (XDG-honoring; see `internal/xdgpath/paths.go`).
- **Loader**: `config.Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error)` — on first run writes `Default()` and returns `created=true`. Starts from `Default()` then unmarshals over it so omitted keys keep defaults. `config.Save(cfg)` writes atomically (temp+rename).
- **Top-level struct** `Config` (config.go:17): `Version`, `Schema`, `General`, `CLI/TUI *Overlay`, `Chain`, `Providers []Provider`, `Google`, `Dict`, `SmartDict`, `TTS`, `History`.
- **Resolution**: `cfg.Resolve(overrides, mode) config.Resolved` in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/config/resolve.go`. Precedence: **flag > env (`TRANSLATE_*`) > `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay > `[general]` > default**. `Resolved` (resolve.go:34) carries the merged view + the chosen `*Provider` + `*Config`. This is the value that `buildEngine` consumes.

`config.Load()` + `cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)` is the standard preamble for a subcommand. **`serve` will likely call these once at startup** (warm) rather than per request — the backlog doc's whole rationale.

Note: there is **no existing `[server]`/`[serve]` config table** — you'd add one to `Config` (e.g. a `Server struct { Port int; Bind string; Token string }` with `toml:"server"`) and to `Default()`, bumping `SchemaVersion` (currently `1`, config.go:43) since it's an additive field the auto-upgrade path (root.go:146–158) handles.

---

## 4. History store

Package `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/store/`.

- **Interface** `store.Store` (`store/store.go:27`): `Add(ctx, Record) (Record, error)`, `Recent(ctx, limit) ([]Record, error)`, `Search(ctx, query, limit) ([]Record, error)`, `Close() error`. `Record` (store.go:12) is the JSONL line shape (also the history JSON API shape).
- **Implementation**: `jsonlStore` in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/store/jsonl.go`. `OpenJSONL(path)` creates parent dirs and returns the store.
  - **`Add`** (jsonl.go:35): assigns id + timestamp, marshals one JSON line, then under `s.mu.Lock()` opens the file `O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY` (0600) and writes `line + '\n'`.
  - **`readAll`** / `Recent` / `Search` (jsonl.go:61–125): read whole file under the same mutex, split on newlines, unmarshal each; `Search` uses `github.com/sahilm/fuzzy` over `input + " " + output`.
- **Concurrency/locking**: **in-process only** — a single `sync.Mutex` on the `jsonlStore` (jsonl.go:21). There is **no cross-process file lock (no flock)**. `Add` relies on a single `O_APPEND` write, which is atomic for small writes on local FS, but `readAll` is not coordinated with other processes. ⚠️ This is exactly the backlog's flagged concern ("server + CLI both appending `history.jsonl` — the store must be concurrency-safe"). For `serve`, one long-lived `Store` instance shared across handler goroutines is safe *within the server* (the mutex covers it); the CLI-running-concurrently case is not fully protected.
- **File location**: default `xdgpath.HistoryFile()` = `~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl` (paths.go:51), overridable via `config.History.Path`. Opened by `openStore(cfg)` (root.go:333), which returns `nil` when history is disabled or `--no-history`.
- The config declares a `sqlite` backend option (`History.Backend`, config.go:153) but **only the `jsonl` backend is implemented** — `openStore` always calls `store.OpenJSONL`.

---

## 5. The SQLite dictionary (ECDICT)

The "sqlite dictionary" is **ECDICT (English→Chinese)**. (CC-CEDICT, the Chinese→English side, is a plain-text in-memory index in `internal/engine/cedict.go`, not SQLite.)

- **Driver**: `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go, CGO-free), imported blank in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/dictdata.go:16` and `internal/engine/ecdict.go`.
- **File location**: `engine.EcdictDBPath(dir)` = `<dir>/ecdict.db`, where `dir` defaults to `xdgpath.DataDir()/dict` = `~/.local/share/translate/dict/ecdict.db` (dictdata.go:22–31). CC-CEDICT lives at `.../dict/cedict_ts.u8`.
- **Build/open**:
  - `BuildEcdictDB` (dictdata.go:84) downloads the ECDICT CSV and streams it into a fresh sqlite DB (`journal_mode(off)&synchronous(off)` for the bulk import), table `entries(word, word_lc, phonetic, translation, definition, pos, frq, exchange)` + index `idx_word_lc`.
  - **Open (read path)**: `ecdictDB` in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/ecdict.go`. Opened **lazily via `sync.Once`** as **read-only**: `sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")` (ecdict.go:37). Long-lived `*sql.DB`.
- **Query interface**: `(*ecdictDB).lookup(ctx, word) (*ecdictEntry, error)` (ecdict.go:48) — a single `QueryRowContext` `SELECT word, phonetic, translation, definition FROM entries WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1` (case-insensitive exact match). Returns `nil, nil` on `sql.ErrNoRows`. `*sql.DB` is safe for concurrent use, so this is fine under an HTTP server.
- Not accessed directly by commands — the wrapper is `engine.LocalDictEngine` (`internal/engine/localdict.go`), built by `dictFromConfig(cfg)` (cmd/build.go:96). It routes Chinese→CC-CEDICT, English→ECDICT, with optional `dictionaryapi.dev` API fallback. `translate define` drives it through the standard `Engine.Translate(ctx, Request{Mode: ModeDict})` interface.

---

## 6. Build and run

`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/Justfile` (note both `Justfile` and `justfile` exist on disk — same content):
- `just build` → `go build -o translate .`
- `just run *ARGS` → `go run . {{ARGS}}`; `just tui` → `go run .`
- `just check` → `fmt vet build`; `just fmt` → `gofmt -w cmd internal main.go`; `just vet` → `go vet . ./cmd/... ./internal/...`; `just test` → `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/...`
- `just install DIR="~/.local/bin"` → builds then `install -m 0755 translate {{DIR}}/translate`
- Raycast recipes (`raycast-dev`, `raycast-build`, `raycast-lint`) shell out to npm — the extension currently **spawns the binary per call** (the thing `serve` would replace).

Version injection: `cmd/version.go` supports `-ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=..."`. **If you add a `serve` recipe/target, `gofmt`/`vet` already glob `cmd` and `internal`, so a new `cmd/serve.go` + `internal/server/` package are covered automatically.**

---

## 7. Existing HTTP client code (LLM / copilot-proxy) — and SSE support

**All existing `net/http` usage is client-side.** Confirmed no server anywhere (`grep` for `ListenAndServe`/`http.Server`/`NewServeMux`/`HandleFunc` → zero hits). `net/http` importers: `internal/engine/{llm.go,dict.go,dictdata.go,google.go}`, `internal/tts/google.go`, `cmd/init.go`.

**The LLM client is `internal/engine/llm.go`** (`LLMEngine`, built by `engine.NewLLM(LLMConfig)` via `llmFromProvider` in cmd/build.go:72). It talks to any OpenAI-compatible endpoint; default provider "copilot" → `http://localhost:4141/v1` (config.go:196). It uses a plain `&http.Client{Timeout: ...}` (default 60s).

**It already fully supports SSE streaming from upstream**, in two dialects:
- **OpenAI `/chat/completions`** (`translateOpenAI`, llm.go:417): sets `Accept: text/event-stream` when streaming; `readSSE` (llm.go:583) parses `data:` lines with a `bufio.Scanner` (1 MB buffer), emits a `Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken}` per `delta.content`, handles `[DONE]` and `finish_reason` (detects `length` truncation).
- **Anthropic `/v1/messages`** for `claude-*` models (`translateAnthropic`, llm.go:492; routed by `isAnthropicModel`): `readAnthropicSSE` (llm.go:628) parses `content_block_delta`/`text_delta` events, `message_stop`, and `stop_reason == max_tokens`.

Both run the HTTP request in a goroutine and return a `<-chan Chunk` (buffered 32) that closes after exactly one terminal `ChunkDone`/`ChunkError` — the uniform **Engine contract** (`internal/engine/engine.go:201`). `engine.Drain(ch, onToken)` (engine.go:225) consumes it.

**Implication for `serve`**: an SSE HTTP handler is a near-perfect impedance match. You'd build the engine once with the existing `buildEngine(res)` (cmd/build.go:16) — but note those builders (`buildEngine`, `llmFromProvider`, `dictFromConfig`, etc.) live in package `cmd`, not `internal/engine`, so a new `internal/server` package can't import them (would be an import cycle / wrong layer). Options: (a) put the server in package `cmd` alongside `build.go`, or (b) lift the small builder helpers into a reusable package. Then per request: call `eng.Translate(ctx, engine.Request{..., Stream: true})`, and in the handler `for chunk := range ch` write `data: <token>\n\n` and `w.(http.Flusher).Flush()` per `ChunkToken`, emitting the final `TranslateResult` JSON on `ChunkDone`. Pass `r.Context()` as the ctx so client disconnects cancel the upstream LLM call (mirrors the existing Ctrl-C wiring). The backlog doc's caveat still holds: copilot-proxy buffers Claude `/v1/messages`, so visible streaming is limited there regardless.

**Caveat worth surfacing**: the pitfall `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md` documents that a stream ending without a terminal marker must be treated as `Truncated` (already handled via `markTruncated`, llm.go:410) — preserve that signal in any SSE response you emit.

---

## Quick-reference: files most relevant to `serve`
- `backlog/api-server.md` — the design spec
- `cmd/root.go` — root cmd, subcommand registration (line 95), `oneShot` streaming/JSON logic, `openStore`, `overrides()`
- `cmd/build.go` — `buildEngine(res)` and all engine constructors (in package `cmd`)
- `internal/engine/engine.go` — `Engine` interface, `Request`, `TranslateResult` (JSON shape), `Chunk`, `Drain`
- `internal/engine/llm.go` — HTTP LLM client + SSE parsers (the streaming source)
- `internal/config/{config.go,resolve.go}` — config load + `Resolve`; add a `[server]` table here
- `internal/store/{store.go,jsonl.go}` — history `Store` (mutex-only concurrency; no cross-process lock)
- `internal/engine/{ecdict.go,dictdata.go,localdict.go,cedict.go}` — dictionary (ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT text)
- `internal/xdgpath/paths.go` — all file locations

This is the value that `buildEngine` consumes.

`config.Load()` + `cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)` is the standard preamble for a subcommand. **`serve` will likely call these once at startup** (warm) rather than per request — the backlog doc's whole rationale.

Note: there is **no existing `[server]`/`[serve]` config table** — you'd add one to `Config` (e.g. a `Server struct { Port int; Bind string; Token string }` with `toml:"server"`) and to `Default()`, bumping `SchemaVersion` (currently `1`, config.go:43) since it's an additive field the auto-upgrade path (root.go:146–158) handles.

---

## 4. History store

Package `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/store/`.

- **Interface** `store.Store` (`store/store.go:27`): `Add(ctx, Record) (Record, error)`, `Recent(ctx, limit) ([]Record, error)`, `Search(ctx, query, limit) ([]Record, error)`, `Close() error`. `Record` (store.go:12) is the JSONL line shape (also the history JSON API shape).
- **Implementation**: `jsonlStore` in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/store/jsonl.go`. `OpenJSONL(path)` creates parent dirs and returns the store.
  - **`Add`** (jsonl.go:35): assigns id + timestamp, marshals one JSON line, then under `s.mu.Lock()` opens the file `O_APPEND|O_CREATE|O_WRONLY` (0600) and writes `line + '\n'`.
  - **`readAll`** / `Recent` / `Search` (jsonl.go:61–125): read whole file under the same mutex, split on newlines, unmarshal each; `Search` uses `github.com/sahilm/fuzzy` over `input + " " + output`.
- **Concurrency/locking**: **in-process only** — a single `sync.Mutex` on the `jsonlStore` (jsonl.go:21). There is **no cross-process file lock (no flock)**. `Add` relies on a single `O_APPEND` write, which is atomic for small writes on local FS, but `readAll` is not coordinated with other processes. ⚠️ This is exactly the backlog's flagged concern ("server + CLI both appending `history.jsonl` — the store must be concurrency-safe"). For `serve`, one long-lived `Store` instance shared across handler goroutines is safe *within the server* (the mutex covers it); the CLI-running-concurrently case is not fully protected.
- **File location**: default `xdgpath.HistoryFile()` = `~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl` (paths.go:51), overridable via `config.History.Path`. Opened by `openStore(cfg)` (root.go:333), which returns `nil` when history is disabled or `--no-history`.
- The config declares a `sqlite` backend option (`History.Backend`, config.go:153) but **only the `jsonl` backend is implemented** — `openStore` always calls `store.OpenJSONL`.

---

## 5. The SQLite dictionary (ECDICT)

The "sqlite dictionary" is **ECDICT (English→Chinese)**. (CC-CEDICT, the Chinese→English side, is a plain-text in-memory index in `internal/engine/cedict.go`, not SQLite.)

- **Driver**: `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go, CGO-free), imported blank in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/dictdata.go:16` and `internal/engine/ecdict.go`.
- **File location**: `engine.EcdictDBPath(dir)` = `&lt;dir&gt;/ecdict.db`, where `dir` defaults to `xdgpath.DataDir()/dict` = `~/.local/share/translate/dict/ecdict.db` (dictdata.go:22–31). CC-CEDICT lives at `.../dict/cedict_ts.u8`.
- **Build/open**:
  - `BuildEcdictDB` (dictdata.go:84) downloads the ECDICT CSV and streams it into a fresh sqlite DB (`journal_mode(off)&amp;synchronous(off)` for the bulk import), table `entries(word, word_lc, phonetic, translation, definition, pos, frq, exchange)` + index `idx_word_lc`.
  - **Open (read path)**: `ecdictDB` in `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/internal/engine/ecdict.go`. Opened **lazily via `sync.Once`** as **read-only**: `sql.Open("sqlite", "file:"+path+"?_pragma=query_only(true)")` (ecdict.go:37). Long-lived `*sql.DB`.
- **Query interface**: `(*ecdictDB).lookup(ctx, word) (*ecdictEntry, error)` (ecdict.go:48) — a single `QueryRowContext` `SELECT word, phonetic, translation, definition FROM entries WHERE word_lc = ? LIMIT 1` (case-insensitive exact match). Returns `nil, nil` on `sql.ErrNoRows`. `*sql.DB` is safe for concurrent use, so this is fine under an HTTP server.
- Not accessed directly by commands — the wrapper is `engine.LocalDictEngine` (`internal/engine/localdict.go`), built by `dictFromConfig(cfg)` (cmd/build.go:96). It routes Chinese→CC-CEDICT, English→ECDICT, with optional `dictionaryapi.dev` API fallback. `translate define` drives it through the standard `Engine.Translate(ctx, Request{Mode: ModeDict})` interface.

---

## 6. Build and run

`/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/Justfile` (note both `Justfile` and `justfile` exist on disk — same content):
- `just build` → `go build -o translate .`
- `just run *ARGS` → `go run . {{ARGS}}`; `just tui` → `go run .`
- `just check` → `fmt vet build`; `just fmt` → `gofmt -w cmd internal main.go`; `just vet` → `go vet . ./cmd/... ./internal/...`; `just test` → `go test ./cmd/... ./internal/...`
- `just install DIR="~/.local/bin"` → builds then `install -m 0755 translate {{DIR}}/translate`
- Raycast recipes (`raycast-dev`, `raycast-build`, `raycast-lint`) shell out to npm — the extension currently **spawns the binary per call** (the thing `serve` would replace).

Version injection: `cmd/version.go` supports `-ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=..."`. **If you add a `serve` recipe/target, `gofmt`/`vet` already glob `cmd` and `internal`, so a new `cmd/serve.go` + `internal/server/` package are covered automatically.**

---

## 7. Existing HTTP client code (LLM / copilot-proxy) — and SSE support

**All existing `net/http` usage is client-side.** Confirmed no server anywhere (`grep` for `ListenAndServe`/`http.Server`/`NewServeMux`/`HandleFunc` → zero hits). `net/http` importers: `internal/engine/{llm.go,dict.go,dictdata.go,google.go}`, `internal/tts/google.go`, `cmd/init.go`.

**The LLM client is `internal/engine/llm.go`** (`LLMEngine`, built by `engine.NewLLM(LLMConfig)` via `llmFromProvider` in cmd/build.go:72). It talks to any OpenAI-compatible endpoint; default provider "copilot" → `http://localhost:4141/v1` (config.go:196). It uses a plain `&amp;http.Client{Timeout: ...}` (default 60s).

**It already fully supports SSE streaming from upstream**, in two dialects:
- **OpenAI `/chat/completions`** (`translateOpenAI`, llm.go:417): sets `Accept: text/event-stream` when streaming; `readSSE` (llm.go:583) parses `data:` lines with a `bufio.Scanner` (1 MB buffer), emits a `Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken}` per `delta.content`, handles `[DONE]` and `finish_reason` (detects `length` truncation).
- **Anthropic `/v1/messages`** for `claude-*` models (`translateAnthropic`, llm.go:492; routed by `isAnthropicModel`): `readAnthropicSSE` (llm.go:628) parses `content_block_delta`/`text_delta` events, `message_stop`, and `stop_reason == max_tokens`.

Both run the HTTP request in a goroutine and return a `&lt;-chan Chunk` (buffered 32) that closes after exactly one terminal `ChunkDone`/`ChunkError` — the uniform **Engine contract** (`internal/engine/engine.go:201`). `engine.Drain(ch, onToken)` (engine.go:225) consumes it.

**Implication for `serve`**: an SSE HTTP handler is a near-perfect impedance match. You'd build the engine once with the existing `buildEngine(res)` (cmd/build.go:16) — but note those builders (`buildEngine`, `llmFromProvider`, `dictFromConfig`, etc.) live in package `cmd`, not `internal/engine`, so a new `internal/server` package can't import them (would be an import cycle / wrong layer). Options: (a) put the server in package `cmd` alongside `build.go`, or (b) lift the small builder helpers into a reusable package. Then per request: call `eng.Translate(ctx, engine.Request{..., Stream: true})`, and in the handler `for chunk := range ch` write `data: &lt;token&gt;\n\n` and `w.(http.Flusher).Flush()` per `ChunkToken`, emitting the final `TranslateResult` JSON on `ChunkDone`. Pass `r.Context()` as the ctx so client disconnects cancel the upstream LLM call (mirrors the existing Ctrl-C wiring). The backlog doc's caveat still holds: copilot-proxy buffers Claude `/v1/messages`, so visible streaming is limited there regardless.

**Caveat worth surfacing**: the pitfall `/Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md` documents that a stream ending without a terminal marker must be treated as `Truncated` (already handled via `markTruncated`, llm.go:410) — preserve that signal in any SSE response you emit.

---

## Quick-reference: files most relevant to `serve`
- `backlog/api-server.md` — the design spec
- `cmd/root.go` — root cmd, subcommand registration (line 95), `oneShot` streaming/JSON logic, `openStore`, `overrides()`
- `cmd/build.go` — `buildEngine(res)` and all engine constructors (in package `cmd`)
- `internal/engine/engine.go` — `Engine` interface, `Request`, `TranslateResult` (JSON shape), `Chunk`, `Drain`
- `internal/engine/llm.go` — HTTP LLM client + SSE parsers (the streaming source)
- `internal/config/{config.go,resolve.go}` — config load + `Resolve`; add a `[server]` table here
- `internal/store/{store.go,jsonl.go}` — history `Store` (mutex-only concurrency; no cross-process lock)
- `internal/engine/{ecdict.go,dictdata.go,localdict.go,cedict.go}` — dictionary (ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT text)
- `internal/xdgpath/paths.go` — all file locations</result>
