 `./internal/engine/engine.go`

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	
46		// Learn marks a language-learning request. It is bidirectional (implies Pair,
47		// with PairHome=native and PairAway=foreign) and adaptive: native-language input
48		// is translated-and-taught (glosses + examples), foreign-language input is
49		// grammar-corrected-and-explained. The LLM engine answers with a strict JSON
50		// object (parsed into TranslateResult.Learn) and, because the output is
51		// structured, always runs NON-STREAMING regardless of Stream.
52		Learn bool
53	
54		// Bilingual marks a whole-document bilingual ("doc mode") request: Segments
55		// carries the full ordered document (prose to translate + code shown only as
56		// context) and the LLM returns a JSON map of prose-segment number → translation.
57		// Context-aware and single-call. LLM engines only (google/dict ignore it), and
58		// like Learn it always runs NON-STREAMING.
59		Bilingual bool
60		Segments  []Segment
61	}
62	
63	// Segment is one unit of a Bilingual document request: a prose block to translate,
64	// or a command/code block included only as surrounding context (Code == true).
65	type Segment struct {
66		Text string
67		Code bool
68	}
69	
70	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
71	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
72	type TranslateResult struct {
73		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
74		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
75		Target         string   `json:"target"`
76		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
77		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
78		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
79	
80		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
81		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
82		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
83	
84		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
85		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
86		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
87		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
88		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
89	
90		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
91		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
92		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
93	
94		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
95		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
96		// Learn payload (Request.Learn); nil outside learn mode. Translation still holds
97		// the main foreign-language sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation)
98		// so copy/history/speak keep working unchanged; the structured extras ride here.
99		Learn *LearnResult `json:"learn,omitempty"`
100		// Bilingual is the doc-mode payload: prose-segment number (1-based, as numbered
101		// in the prompt) → translation. nil outside bilingual doc mode. Transient.
102		Bilingual map[int]string `json:"-"`
103		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
104		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
105		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
106		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
107	
108		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
109		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
110		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
111		// Transient: not serialized.
112		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
113	}
114	
115	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
116	type DictEntry struct {
117		Word      string    `json:"word"`
118		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
119		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
120		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
121	}
122	
123	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
124	type Meaning struct {
125		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
126		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
127		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
128		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
129	}
130	
131	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
132	type Definition struct {
133		Text    string `json:"definition"`
134		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
135	}
136	
137	// LearnResult is the structured payload for learn/tutor mode (Request.Learn). The
138	// model fills only the fields relevant to the auto-detected direction; all glosses,
139	// explanations, and notes are written in the native (home) language.
140	type LearnResult struct {
141		Direction   string         `json:"direction"`           // "teach" (native→foreign) | "correct" (foreign→native)
142		Original    string         `json:"original"`            // the user's input as received
143		Corrected   string         `json:"corrected,omitempty"` // correct-direction: the fixed foreign sentence
144		Translation string         `json:"translation"`         // teach: foreign translation; correct: native translation of intent
145		Notes       string         `json:"notes,omitempty"`     // short tip/encouragement, in the NATIVE language
146		Issues      []LearnIssue   `json:"issues,omitempty"`    // correct-direction grammar/usage fixes
147		Vocab       []LearnGloss   `json:"vocab,omitempty"`     // teach-direction glosses
148		Examples    []LearnExample `json:"examples,omitempty"`  // teach-direction usage examples
149	}
150	
151	// LearnIssue is one grammar/usage correction (correct-direction).
152	type LearnIssue struct {
153		Span        string `json:"span"`        // the problematic fragment of the input
154		Fix         string `json:"fix"`         // the corrected fragment
155		Explanation string `json:"explanation"` // why — in the NATIVE language
156	}
157	
158	// LearnGloss is one vocabulary explanation (teach-direction).
159	type LearnGloss struct {
160		Term     string `json:"term"`               // foreign word/term
161		Pos      string `json:"pos,omitempty"`      // part of speech
162		Phonetic string `json:"phonetic,omitempty"` // KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese
163		Meaning  string `json:"meaning"`            // meaning in the NATIVE language
164	}
165	
166	// LearnExample is one usage example (teach-direction).
167	type LearnExample struct {
168		Foreign string `json:"foreign"` // example sentence in the foreign language
169		Native  string `json:"native"`  // its native-language translation
170	}
171	
172	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
173	type ChunkKind int
174	
175	const (
176		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
177		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
178		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
179		ChunkDone
180		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
181		ChunkError
182	)
183	
184	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
185	type Chunk struct {
186		Kind   ChunkKind
187		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
188		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
189		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
190	}
191	
192	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
193	//
194	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
195	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
196	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
197	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
198	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
199	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
200	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
201	type Engine interface {
202		Name() string
203		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
204		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
205		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
206		Supports(m Mode) bool
207	}
208	
209	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
210	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
211	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
212		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
213		if err != nil {
214			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
215		} else {
216			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
217		}
218		close(ch)
219		return ch
220	}
221	
222	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
223	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
224	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
225	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
226		for c := range ch {
227			switch c.Kind {
228			case ChunkToken:
229				if onToken != nil {
230					onToken(c.Text)
231				}
232			case ChunkDone:
233				return c.Result, nil
234			case ChunkError:
235				return nil, c.Err
236			}
237		}
238		return nil, ErrNoResult
239	}
240
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/config/config.go`

```text
1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
14	)
15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		// Version is the app version (git tag) that last wrote this file, and Schema is
19		// the config schema generation (see SchemaVersion). Both are stamped by Save;
20		// Version is informational, Schema drives the "your config is outdated" reminder.
21		// Declared first so they serialize above the [general] table.
22		Version string `toml:"version,omitempty"`
23		Schema  int    `toml:"schema"`
24	
25		General   General    `toml:"general"`
26		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
27		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
28		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
29		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
30		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
31		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
32		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
33		TTS       TTS        `toml:"tts"`
34		History   History    `toml:"history"`
35	}
36	
37	// SchemaVersion is the current config schema generation. Bump it whenever a new
38	// field or default is added that `translate init` would materialize, so an older
39	// on-disk config (schema < this) can be detected and the user reminded to re-init.
40	// It is NOT the app version (which is the git tag; see cmd/version.go). History:
41	//
42	//	1 — smart-auto default, pair-mode home anchoring, debug, TTS/learn fields.
43	const SchemaVersion = 1
44	
45	// Generator is the app version string (git tag) stamped into config.version on
46	// Save. The cmd layer sets it at startup; it stays "" for library/test callers,
47	// in which case the version key is simply omitted.
48	var Generator string
49	
50	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
51	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
52	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
53	type Overlay struct {
54		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
55		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
56		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
57		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
58		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
59		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
60		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
61		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
62		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
63		Learn         *bool   `toml:"learn,omitempty"`
64		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
65		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
66		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
67		Debug         *bool   `toml:"debug,omitempty"`
68	}
69	
70	// General holds behavior settings.
```
</details></tool-use>

268	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {

282			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
283		}

Design validated against source. The one genuine fork is the MCP piece — the user's explicit "順便夾帶MCP" question. Both axes (how clients reach it, and SDK vs hand-roll) meaningfully change the package structure, dependencies, and security surface, so I'll confirm those before finalizing.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:36:22Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**How should the MCP server be exposed to clients? (This is the '順便夾帶 MCP' part.) Both HTTP/REST+Swagger and MCP are thin adapters over the same shared engine core, so any of these is cheap once that core exists.**

Options:
- **stdio subcommand (translate mcp)** - Best for Claude Desktop/Code/Cursor, which spawn the binary and speak JSON-RPC over stdio. No open port, no token — sidesteps the whole HTTP security surface; history never crosses a network boundary. Can ship right after the shared Service core, independent of the HTTP work.
- **HTTP /mcp on the serve port** - One daemon serves REST + Swagger + MCP (Streamable HTTP) on 127.0.0.1:4155. Matches 'bundle it into the server' literally, but ties MCP to the daemon lifecycle + token surface, and most desktop MCP clients still expect a stdio command.
- **Both (stdio now, /mcp later)** - Ship the stdio subcommand first; add /mcp on the serve port as a later gated add reusing the same tool handlers. Most flexible, most total work.
- **Don't include MCP now** - Design serve + Swagger/OpenAPI only; keep MCP as a separate backlog item to revisit.

**If we build MCP, which implementation? (The official Go SDK just reached stable v1.x, which changes the earlier zero-dep calculus.)**

Options:
- **Official Go SDK (v1.6.1)** - Stable v1.x, maintained with Google. Handles protocol-version negotiation, capabilities, content typing, and future spec churn. Clean API (mcp.NewServer/AddTool, schema from struct tags). Adds one dependency to a repo that already uses cobra/bubbletea/sqlite.
- **Hand-rolled JSON-RPC (zero-dep)** - ~250 lines over stdio for 3 tools; keeps go.mod lean, matching the hand-written-OpenAPI stance. Trade-off: you own MCP spec compliance and drift as the protocol evolves. Isolated in one file so it can be swapped for the SDK later.


**Answer:** stdio subcommand (translate mcp), Official Go SDK (v1.6.1)
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:38:03Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# `translate serve` (HTTP + OpenAPI/Swagger) + `translate mcp` (MCP over stdio)

## Context

Every client today (Raycast extension, script commands) **spawns the `translate`
binary per call** and parses `--json`/`--stream` stdout. `backlog/api-server.md`
(P?/L, deferred 2026-07) scoped a persistent loopback HTTP service to unlock
streaming-first UX, multi-client reuse (Raycast/Alfred/curl/web UI), and OpenAPI
docs. While building the Raycast extension the follow-on idea surfaced: *also expose
an MCP server* so LLM hosts (Claude Desktop/Code, Cursor) can translate through this
tool as a first-class tool call.

This plan delivers both as **thin adapters over the existing `internal/engine`
core**, which is already TUI-free, context-aware, concurrency-safe, and streaming-
native (`Engine.Translate(ctx, Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)`; `TranslateResult` is
already fully JSON-tagged). The one structural obstacle — the engine *builders* live
in package `cmd`, which a server package cannot import — is resolved once, up front,
by lifting them into a new shared `internal/appcore` package. After that, REST, SSE,
Swagger, and MCP are each small additive layers.

**Decisions (confirmed with the user):** MCP ships as a separate **`translate mcp`
stdio subcommand** (no port/token; the transport is a private stdio pipe the host
already trusts), built on the **official Go MCP SDK v1.6.1** (`github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk`,
stable v1.x, maintained with Google — it owns protocol-version negotiation and future
spec churn; the 3 tools map 1:1 to the shared service).

## Architecture — package layout & the layering fix

Import direction (arrows = "imports"):

\```
internal/engine  internal/config  internal/store  internal/lang     (unchanged core)
                       ▲ ▲ ▲ ▲
              internal/appcore            NEW: engine builders + Service (warm engine + store)
                    ▲        ▲
        internal/server   internal/mcpserver   NEW transports (each imports only appcore)
                    ▲        ▲
                       cmd                  registers serve/mcp; keeps buildEngineSet (imports tui)
\```

`internal/appcore` imports only `config`/`engine`/`store`/`lang`/`debug` — **never
`tui` or `cmd`** — so `cmd` and both server packages import it with no cycle.

New files:
- `internal/appcore/build.go` — the 8 builders lifted from `cmd/build.go`, exported.
- `internal/appcore/service.go` — the `Service` type (warm engines + store + glue).
- `internal/appcore/record.go` — `ToRecord` + `effectiveTarget` (pair routing).
- `internal/server/{server.go,handlers.go,sse.go,openapi.go,errors.go}` + `internal/server/swagger/` (embedded UI).
- `internal/mcpserver/{mcp.go,tools.go}`.
- `cmd/serve.go`, `cmd/mcp.go` — thin cobra wrappers.
- `_test.go` alongside each new `internal/` package (picked up by the existing `./internal/...` globs).

---

## Phase 0 — Refactor to `internal/appcore` + `Service` (no behavior change)

Ships alone; existing CLI stays byte-identical. Gated by `just check` + `just test`.

1. **Move builders** `cmd/build.go` → `internal/appcore/build.go`, exporting each (they
   already take only `config.Resolved`/`*config.Config`, so no signature changes):
   `buildEngine→BuildEngine`, `buildAutoChain→BuildAutoChain`, `llmFromProvider→LLMFromProvider`,
   `googleFromConfig→GoogleFromConfig`, `dictFromConfig→DictFromConfig`,
   `smartDictFromConfig→SmartDictFromConfig`, `smartAutoFromConfig→SmartAutoFromConfig`,
   `learnEngineFromConfig→LearnEngineFromConfig`. **Leave `buildEngineSet` in `cmd`**
   (it imports `internal/tui`) and rewrite its body to call the `appcore.*` builders.
2. **Move glue**: `toRecord` (`cmd/root.go:364`) → `appcore.ToRecord`; the body of
   `effectiveTarget` (`cmd/root.go:268`, calls `lang.PairTarget`) → unexported
   `appcore.effectiveTarget`. Add `appcore.DefineEngine(res, plain, smart bool) engine.Engine`
   capturing `defineEngine` (`cmd/define.go`) so CLI and Service agree on the exact define engine.
3. **Rewire call sites** (the only churn): `cmd/root.go` (`buildEngine`→`appcore.BuildEngine`
   at ~:191; `learnEngineFromConfig`/`llmFromProvider` refs; `recordAndRemember`→`appcore.ToRecord`),
   `cmd/build.go` `buildEngineSet`, and `cmd/define.go` `defineEngine` (becomes a one-liner).
4. Verify `just check` (`go vet . ./cmd/... ./internal/...` + build) stays green.

### `internal/appcore.Service` (built once, warm, shared across goroutines)

\```go
type Service struct {
    cfg    *config.Config
    res    config.Resolved   // resolved once at startup, empty flag overrides
    trans  engine.Engine     // warm translate engine (BuildEngine)
    define engine.Engine     // warm define engine (DefineEngine)
    store  store.Store        // shared history; nil when disabled / --no-history
    home, away string; pair bool   // pair-mode routing, resolved once
}
type Options struct{ NoHistory bool }
func NewService(cfg *config.Config, opt Options) (*Service, error) // warms engines, opens store
func newService(trans, define engine.Engine, st store.Store, ...) *Service // unexported, for tests (no network)

type Params struct { Text, Source, Target, Preset, Instructions, Model string; MaxAlts int; Pair *bool }

func (s *Service) Translate(ctx, Params) (*engine.TranslateResult, error)
func (s *Service) TranslateStream(ctx, Params, onToken func(string)) (*engine.TranslateResult, error)
func (s *Service) Define(ctx, word string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error)
func (s *Service) HistoryRecent(ctx, limit int) ([]store.Record, error)
func (s *Service) HistorySearch(ctx, query string, limit int) ([]store.Record, error)
func (s *Service) Close() error
\```

Internals reuse existing paths, not new logic: a `buildRequest(p)` helper mirrors
`oneShot` (`cmd/root.go:462`) + `effectiveTarget`; consume the channel with the
existing `engine.Drain(ch, onToken)` (`internal/engine/engine.go:225`); on success
**and `!res.Truncated`**, persist via `s.store.Add(ctx, appcore.ToRecord(...))`.
Do **not** touch `state.json`/`saveLastPair` — the server must never fight the CLI's
remembered pair. `Define` = `s.define.Translate(ctx, Request{Text: word, Mode: ModeDict})` + `Drain`.

---

## Phase 1 — `translate serve`: REST JSON + config + security + shutdown

Router: stdlib `net/http.ServeMux` with Go 1.22+ method+path patterns
(`mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/translate", …)`) — no framework, matching the lean `go.mod`.

`cmd/serve.go` → `newServeCmd()`, registered in the `root.AddCommand(...)` call at
`cmd/root.go:95`. Flags override `[server]` config: `--port`, `--bind`, `--token`,
`--no-history`. `RunE`: `config.Load()` → resolve `[server]` (flags > config >
default) → `appcore.NewService` → `server.New(svc, sc)` → run with graceful shutdown.

Endpoints (response bodies reuse `engine.TranslateResult` / `store.Record` verbatim):

| Method + path | Request | Response |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /v1/translate` | `{text, source?, target?, preset?, instructions?, model?, max_alts?, pair?}` | `TranslateResult` |
| `POST /v1/define` | `{word}` | `TranslateResult` (dictionary/suggestions) |
| `GET /v1/history?q=&limit=` | — | `[]store.Record` (token-guarded) |
| `GET /healthz` | — | `{"status":"ok"}` |

Handlers: `json.Decode` → validate (empty text/word → 422) → `Service` → `json.Encode`.
Error envelope `{"error":{"code","message"}}` via `writeError(w,status,code,msg)`;
status map: 400 malformed JSON · 422 empty input (`engine.ErrEmptyInput`) · 401 bad
token · 404/405 routing · 500 engine error. Graceful shutdown: the serve command wires
its own `signal.NotifyContext(ctx, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)`; on `ctx.Done()` →
`srv.Shutdown(5s)` then `svc.Close()`. Log the bound address on start.

### Config `[server]` table (`internal/config/config.go`)

\```go
Server Server `toml:"server"`
type Server struct {
    Port     int    `toml:"port"`                 // default 4155
    Bind     string `toml:"bind"`                 // default "127.0.0.1"
    Token    string `toml:"token,omitempty"`      // optional inline bearer token
    TokenEnv string `toml:"token_env,omitempty"`  // env var holding the token (preferred)
}
\```
Add to `Default()`; **bump `SchemaVersion` 1→2** (`config.go:43`) with a history comment.
No migration code needed — the additive auto-upgrade re-save at `cmd/root.go:146-158`
materializes it. Add a small `Config.ResolveServer(flags)` helper.

### Security
- **Loopback fail-closed**: default `Bind:"127.0.0.1"`; refuse to start on a non-loopback
  bind (`net.ParseIP(...).IsLoopback()` in `server.New`) unless a token is set.
- **Bearer token**: resolve `--token` > `[server].token` > `os.Getenv(token_env)`
  (recommend `token_env` so the secret stays out of `config.toml`); require
  `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on `/v1/history` (personal data), compared with
  `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`. `/healthz`, `/openapi.json`, `/docs` stay open on loopback.

---

## Phase 2 — SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`

GET + query params (browser `EventSource` is GET-only). `internal/server/sse.go`:
set `Content-Type: text/event-stream`, get `http.Flusher` (503 if absent), pass
`r.Context()` (already cancels the upstream LLM call end-to-end via the engine's
`case <-ctx.Done()` on client disconnect — no extra plumbing). `onToken` writes
`event: token` + `flusher.Flush()`; terminal `event: done` carries the final
`TranslateResult` JSON. Because `TranslateResult.Truncated` is `json:"-"`
(`engine.go:88`), emit it explicitly as an `event: warning` before `done`.

**Honor the caveat, don't over-promise**: copilot-proxy buffers Claude `/v1/messages`,
so *visible* token cadence stays coarse for Claude models regardless of SSE (upstream,
transport-independent). SSE's real win is dict/google/OpenAI-style providers and
multi-client fan-out. Document this on `/docs`.

---

## Phase 3 — OpenAPI + Swagger UI

**Hand-written spec + embedded UI** (matches the backlog's stated preference and the
zero-framework philosophy; a static OpenAPI 3.1 doc never drifts at runtime, unlike
codegen/framework alternatives):
- `internal/server/openapi.json` (OpenAPI 3.1), `go:embed`-ded, served at
  `GET /openapi.json`. A `TestOpenAPISchemaMatchesStruct` reflection test guards the
  top-level `components.schemas` field names against `TranslateResult`/`DictEntry`/`Record`.
- Vendor a pinned `swagger-ui-dist` (`swagger-ui.css`, `swagger-ui-bundle.js`, and an
  `index.html` calling `SwaggerUIBundle({url:'/openapi.json'})`) under
  `internal/server/swagger/`, `go:embed`-ded and served at `GET /docs` via
  `http.FileServerFS`. No CDN (offline/loopback-friendly). Adds ~1.5 MB to the binary —
  accepted; gate behind a `swaggerui` build tag only if binary size becomes an issue.

---

## Phase 4 — `translate mcp`: MCP over stdio (official Go SDK)

Depends only on Phase 0 (the `Service`), **not** on Phases 1–3 — can ship in parallel
with or before the HTTP work. It's the cheapest high-value deliverable.

Dependency: `go get github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.6.1` (Go 1.26.4 satisfies it).

`internal/mcpserver/mcp.go` — `Serve(ctx, svc *appcore.Service) error`:
\```go
srv := mcp.NewServer(&mcp.Implementation{Name: "translate", Version: version}, nil)
mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{Name: "translate", Description: "…"}, translateHandler(svc))
mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{Name: "define",    Description: "…"}, defineHandler(svc))
mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{Name: "history",   Description: "…"}, historyHandler(svc))
return srv.Run(ctx, &mcp.StdioTransport{})   // stdin/stdout JSON-RPC
\```
Handler signature (SDK derives the input JSON Schema from struct tags):
`func(ctx, *mcp.CallToolRequest, In) (*mcp.CallToolResult, Out, error)`.

| Tool | Input struct (json+jsonschema tags) | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| `translate` | `{Text string; Target, Source string}` | `CallToolResult` text = translation (+ alternatives/notes); `StructuredContent` = `TranslateResult` |
| `define` | `{Word string}` | text = formatted definition; structured = `TranslateResult` |
| `history` | `{Query string; Limit int}` | text = compact record list; structured = `[]store.Record` |

`cmd/mcp.go` → `newMcpCmd()` (registered at `cmd/root.go:95`): builds
`appcore.NewService(cfg, {})` then calls `mcpserver.Serve(cmd.Context(), svc)`.
**Critical**: stdout is the JSON-RPC channel — route all diagnostics/logs to stderr.

---

## Decided design points (no further input needed)
- **Per-request engine/provider switching is out of scope for v1.** The Service warms
  one engine; per-request overrides are limited to what `engine.Request` already honors
  (source/target/preset/instructions/`model`/max_alts). An engine cache keyed by
  `{engine,provider,model}` is a later add if needed.
- **History cross-process safety**: harden `jsonlStore.Add` (`internal/store/jsonl.go`)
  with an advisory `syscall.Flock(fd, LOCK_EX)` on the append fd (unix build tag; Windows
  no-op) so a running server and a concurrent CLI can't interleave >4 KB appends. Small,
  contained; do it in Phase 1.
- **Bilingual/learn** get no API/MCP surface in v1 (structured, non-streaming, CLI-shaped).
- **Daemon lifecycle** (LaunchAgent/menu-bar/auto-start) stays out of scope — `serve`
  only offers the process; keeping it running is a separate backlog item.
- **Raycast extension** stays spawn-based; a "server URL" preference with spawn fallback
  is a separate, opt-in follow-up so non-server users are unaffected.

## Risks / open questions
- `history.jsonl` `Recent`/`Search` re-read the whole file per call (`readAll`,
  `jsonl.go:61`) — fine at personal scale, O(file) per request under a server.
- Port `4155` is a fixed default (collision risk; no discovery in v1) — config'd, per the backlog.
- MCP SDK is a new external dependency; isolated in `internal/mcpserver` so it stays contained.

## Verification
- **Per phase**: `just check` (`fmt` + `vet` + `build`) and `just test`
  (`go test ./cmd/... ./internal/...`) — the new `internal/*` packages are already in scope.
- **Service** (`appcore/service_test.go`): table tests with a stub `engine.Engine`
  emitting canned `Chunk`s; assert pair-target routing (`lang.PairTarget`), history
  recording, and that `Truncated` results are **not** persisted. Real store round-trip
  via `store.OpenJSONL(t.TempDir()+"/h.jsonl")`.
- **HTTP** (`internal/server/*_test.go`): follow the existing `httptest` pattern in
  `internal/engine/llm_test.go`; `httptest.NewRecorder` + mux for JSON endpoints (assert
  status + decoded body; 401 without token, 200 with). SSE: a recorder implements
  `http.Flusher` — assert `event: token`×N + terminal `event: done`; one `httptest.NewServer`
  test reads the body line-by-line and cancels the client to assert ctx propagation.
- **MCP** (`internal/mcpserver/mcp_test.go`): use the SDK's in-memory transport (or an
  `exec` of `translate mcp`) to drive `initialize` → `tools/list` → `tools/call`; assert
  3 tools advertised and a stubbed translation returned.
- **End-to-end manual**:
  - `go run . serve --port 4155` then
    `curl -s localhost:4155/v1/translate -d '{"text":"hello","target":"zh-TW"}'`,
    `curl -N 'localhost:4155/v1/translate/stream?text=hello&target=zh-TW'`, open `localhost:4155/docs`.
  - `just build` then register `{"command":"…/translate","args":["mcp"]}` in an MCP host
    (Claude Desktop/Code) and confirm the `translate`/`define`/`history` tools resolve and run.

## Critical files
- `cmd/build.go` — builders to lift into `internal/appcore` (only `buildEngineSet` keeps `tui`).
- `cmd/root.go` — register `serve`/`mcp` at `:95`; rewire `buildEngine`/`effectiveTarget`(:268)/`toRecord`(:364); `oneShot`(:462) is the template for `Service.buildRequest`; schema auto-upgrade at :146-158.
- `internal/engine/engine.go` — `TranslateResult`(:72) as the JSON/response body; `Engine`/`Chunk`/`Drain`(:225) contract the Service consumes.
- `internal/config/config.go` — add `[server]` table, bump `SchemaVersion`(:43), extend `Default()`.
- `internal/store/{store.go,jsonl.go}` — shared `Store`; add flock to `jsonlStore.Add`.
- `cmd/define.go` — `defineEngine` → `appcore.DefineEngine`.

## References
- `backlog/api-server.md` — original scope, endpoints, open questions.
- Official Go MCP SDK (v1.6.1, stable): https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk · docs https://go.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/
- MCP transports (stdio vs streamable HTTP): https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/transports

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:38:17Z)**_

I've written the plan. Here's the shape of it:

**Core idea:** REST+Swagger and MCP are both thin adapters over the existing `internal/engine` core. The one structural obstacle — engine *builders* trapped in package `cmd` — is fixed once by lifting them into a new `internal/appcore` package that also holds a warm `Service` (built engines + history store + pair-routing glue). Everything else layers additively on top.

**Phasing** (each independently shippable):
- **Phase 0** — refactor to `internal/appcore` + `Service`, zero behavior change.
- **Phase 1** — `translate serve` REST JSON (`/v1/translate`, `/v1/define`, `/v1/history`, `/healthz`), `[server]` config table, loopback fail-closed + bearer token, graceful shutdown, history flock hardening.
- **Phase 2** — SSE `/v1/translate/stream` (honoring the copilot-proxy buffering caveat).
- **Phase 3** — hand-written `/openapi.json` + `go:embed` Swagger UI at `/docs`.
- **Phase 4** — `translate mcp` stdio subcommand on the official Go SDK v1.6.1, exposing `translate`/`define`/`history` tools. Depends only on Phase 0, so it can land before or beside the HTTP work.

Responses reuse the already-JSON-tagged `engine.TranslateResult` verbatim; the SSE handler reuses `engine.Drain` and the engine's existing `ctx`-cancellation-on-disconnect. MCP-over-stdio deliberately sidesteps the entire port/token security surface.

Sources for the MCP recommendation: [official Go MCP SDK](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk) (v1.6.1, stable), [MCP transports spec](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/transports).

User has approved your plan. You can now start coding. Start with updating your todo list if applicable

Your plan has been saved to: /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui/.claude/plans/backlog-api-server-md-api-server-peppy-twilight.md
You can refer back to it if needed during implementation.

## Approved Plan:
# `translate serve` (HTTP + OpenAPI/Swagger) + `translate mcp` (MCP over stdio)

## Context

Every client today (Raycast extension, script commands) **spawns the `translate`
binary per call** and parses `--json`/`--stream` stdout. `backlog/api-server.md`
(P?/L, deferred 2026-07) scoped a persistent loopback HTTP service to unlock
streaming-first UX, multi-client reuse (Raycast/Alfred/curl/web UI), and OpenAPI
docs. While building the Raycast extension the follow-on idea surfaced: *also expose
an MCP server* so LLM hosts (Claude Desktop/Code, Cursor) can translate through this
tool as a first-class tool call.

This plan delivers both as **thin adapters over the existing `internal/engine`
core**, which is already TUI-free, context-aware, concurrency-safe, and streaming-
native (`Engine.Translate(ctx, Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)`; `TranslateResult` is
already fully JSON-tagged). The one structural obstacle — the engine *builders* live
in package `cmd`, which a server package cannot import — is resolved once, up front,
by lifting them into a new shared `internal/appcore` package. After that, REST, SSE,
Swagger, and MCP are each small additive layers.

**Decisions (confirmed with the user):** MCP ships as a separate **`translate mcp`
stdio subcommand** (no port/token; the transport is a private stdio pipe the host
already trusts), built on the **official Go MCP SDK v1.6.1** (`github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk`,
stable v1.x, maintained with Google — it owns protocol-version negotiation and future
spec churn; the 3 tools map 1:1 to the shared service).

## Architecture — package layout & the layering fix

Import direction (arrows = "imports"):

```
internal/engine  internal/config  internal/store  internal/lang     (unchanged core)
                       ▲ ▲ ▲ ▲
              internal/appcore            NEW: engine builders + Service (warm engine + store)
                    ▲        ▲
        internal/server   internal/mcpserver   NEW transports (each imports only appcore)
                    ▲        ▲
                       cmd                  registers serve/mcp; keeps buildEngineSet (imports tui)
```

`internal/appcore` imports only `config`/`engine`/`store`/`lang`/`debug` — **never
`tui` or `cmd`** — so `cmd` and both server packages import it with no cycle.

New files:
- `internal/appcore/build.go` — the 8 builders lifted from `cmd/build.go`, exported.
- `internal/appcore/service.go` — the `Service` type (warm engines + store + glue).
- `internal/appcore/record.go` — `ToRecord` + `effectiveTarget` (pair routing).
- `internal/server/{server.go,handlers.go,sse.go,openapi.go,errors.go}` + `internal/server/swagger/` (embedded UI).
- `internal/mcpserver/{mcp.go,tools.go}`.
- `cmd/serve.go`, `cmd/mcp.go` — thin cobra wrappers.
- `_test.go` alongside each new `internal/` package (picked up by the existing `./internal/...` globs).

---

## Phase 0 — Refactor to `internal/appcore` + `Service` (no behavior change)

Ships alone; existing CLI stays byte-identical. Gated by `just check` + `just test`.

1. **Move builders** `cmd/build.go` → `internal/appcore/build.go`, exporting each (they
   already take only `config.Resolved`/`*config.Config`, so no signature changes):
   `buildEngine→BuildEngine`, `buildAutoChain→BuildAutoChain`, `llmFromProvider→LLMFromProvider`,
   `googleFromConfig→GoogleFromConfig`, `dictFromConfig→DictFromConfig`,
   `smartDictFromConfig→SmartDictFromConfig`, `smartAutoFromConfig→SmartAutoFromConfig`,
   `learnEngineFromConfig→LearnEngineFromConfig`. **Leave `buildEngineSet` in `cmd`**
   (it imports `internal/tui`) and rewrite its body to call the `appcore.*` builders.
2. **Move glue**: `toRecord` (`cmd/root.go:364`) → `appcore.ToRecord`; the body of
   `effectiveTarget` (`cmd/root.go:268`, calls `lang.PairTarget`) → unexported
   `appcore.effectiveTarget`. Add `appcore.DefineEngine(res, plain, smart bool) engine.Engine`
   capturing `defineEngine` (`cmd/define.go`) so CLI and Service agree on the exact define engine.
3. **Rewire call sites** (the only churn): `cmd/root.go` (`buildEngine`→`appcore.BuildEngine`
   at ~:191; `learnEngineFromConfig`/`llmFromProvider` refs; `recordAndRemember`→`appcore.ToRecord`),
   `cmd/build.go` `buildEngineSet`, and `cmd/define.go` `defineEngine` (becomes a one-liner).
4. Verify `just check` (`go vet . ./cmd/... ./internal/...` + build) stays green.

### `internal/appcore.Service` (built once, warm, shared across goroutines)

```go
type Service struct {
    cfg    *config.Config
    res    config.Resolved   // resolved once at startup, empty flag overrides
    trans  engine.Engine     // warm translate engine (BuildEngine)
    define engine.Engine     // warm define engine (DefineEngine)
    store  store.Store        // shared history; nil when disabled / --no-history
    home, away string; pair bool   // pair-mode routing, resolved once
}
type Options struct{ NoHistory bool }
func NewService(cfg *config.Config, opt Options) (*Service, error) // warms engines, opens store
func newService(trans, define engine.Engine, st store.Store, ...) *Service // unexported, for tests (no network)

type Params struct { Text, Source, Target, Preset, Instructions, Model string; MaxAlts int; Pair *bool }

func (s *Service) Translate(ctx, Params) (*engine.TranslateResult, error)
func (s *Service) TranslateStream(ctx, Params, onToken func(string)) (*engine.TranslateResult, error)
func (s *Service) Define(ctx, word string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error)
func (s *Service) HistoryRecent(ctx, limit int) ([]store.Record, error)
func (s *Service) HistorySearch(ctx, query string, limit int) ([]store.Record, error)
func (s *Service) Close() error
```

Internals reuse existing paths, not new logic: a `buildRequest(p)` helper mirrors
`oneShot` (`cmd/root.go:462`) + `effectiveTarget`; consume the channel with the
existing `engine.Drain(ch, onToken)` (`internal/engine/engine.go:225`); on success
**and `!res.Truncated`**, persist via `s.store.Add(ctx, appcore.ToRecord(...))`.
Do **not** touch `state.json`/`saveLastPair` — the server must never fight the CLI's
remembered pair. `Define` = `s.define.Translate(ctx, Request{Text: word, Mode: ModeDict})` + `Drain`.

---

## Phase 1 — `translate serve`: REST JSON + config + security + shutdown

Router: stdlib `net/http.ServeMux` with Go 1.22+ method+path patterns
(`mux.HandleFunc("POST /v1/translate", …)`) — no framework, matching the lean `go.mod`.

`cmd/serve.go` → `newServeCmd()`, registered in the `root.AddCommand(...)` call at
`cmd/root.go:95`. Flags override `[server]` config: `--port`, `--bind`, `--token`,
`--no-history`. `RunE`: `config.Load()` → resolve `[server]` (flags > config >
default) → `appcore.NewService` → `server.New(svc, sc)` → run with graceful shutdown.

Endpoints (response bodies reuse `engine.TranslateResult` / `store.Record` verbatim):

| Method + path | Request | Response |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /v1/translate` | `{text, source?, target?, preset?, instructions?, model?, max_alts?, pair?}` | `TranslateResult` |
| `POST /v1/define` | `{word}` | `TranslateResult` (dictionary/suggestions) |
| `GET /v1/history?q=&limit=` | — | `[]store.Record` (token-guarded) |
| `GET /healthz` | — | `{"status":"ok"}` |

Handlers: `json.Decode` → validate (empty text/word → 422) → `Service` → `json.Encode`.
Error envelope `{"error":{"code","message"}}` via `writeError(w,status,code,msg)`;
status map: 400 malformed JSON · 422 empty input (`engine.ErrEmptyInput`) · 401 bad
token · 404/405 routing · 500 engine error. Graceful shutdown: the serve command wires
its own `signal.NotifyContext(ctx, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)`; on `ctx.Done()` →
`srv.Shutdown(5s)` then `svc.Close()`. Log the bound address on start.

### Config `[server]` table (`internal/config/config.go`)

```go
Server Server `toml:"server"`
type Server struct {
    Port     int    `toml:"port"`                 // default 4155
    Bind     string `toml:"bind"`                 // default "127.0.0.1"
    Token    string `toml:"token,omitempty"`      // optional inline bearer token
    TokenEnv string `toml:"token_env,omitempty"`  // env var holding the token (preferred)
}
```
Add to `Default()`; **bump `SchemaVersion` 1→2** (`config.go:43`) with a history comment.
No migration code needed — the additive auto-upgrade re-save at `cmd/root.go:146-158`
materializes it. Add a small `Config.ResolveServer(flags)` helper.

### Security
- **Loopback fail-closed**: default `Bind:"127.0.0.1"`; refuse to start on a non-loopback
  bind (`net.ParseIP(...).IsLoopback()` in `server.New`) unless a token is set.
- **Bearer token**: resolve `--token` > `[server].token` > `os.Getenv(token_env)`
  (recommend `token_env` so the secret stays out of `config.toml`); require
  `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on `/v1/history` (personal data), compared with
  `crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare`. `/healthz`, `/openapi.json`, `/docs` stay open on loopback.

---

## Phase 2 — SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`

GET + query params (browser `EventSource` is GET-only). `internal/server/sse.go`:
set `Content-Type: text/event-stream`, get `http.Flusher` (503 if absent), pass
`r.Context()` (already cancels the upstream LLM call end-to-end via the engine's
`case <-ctx.Done()` on client disconnect — no extra plumbing). `onToken` writes
`event: token` + `flusher.Flush()`; terminal `event: done` carries the final
`TranslateResult` JSON. Because `TranslateResult.Truncated` is `json:"-"`
(`engine.go:88`), emit it explicitly as an `event: warning` before `done`.

**Honor the caveat, don't over-promise**: copilot-proxy buffers Claude `/v1/messages`,
so *visible* token cadence stays coarse for Claude models regardless of SSE (upstream,
transport-independent). SSE's real win is dict/google/OpenAI-style providers and
multi-client fan-out. Document this on `/docs`.

---

## Phase 3 — OpenAPI + Swagger UI

**Hand-written spec + embedded UI** (matches the backlog's stated preference and the
zero-framework philosophy; a static OpenAPI 3.1 doc never drifts at runtime, unlike
codegen/framework alternatives):
- `internal/server/openapi.json` (OpenAPI 3.1), `go:embed`-ded, served at
  `GET /openapi.json`. A `TestOpenAPISchemaMatchesStruct` reflection test guards the
  top-level `components.schemas` field names against `TranslateResult`/`DictEntry`/`Record`.
- Vendor a pinned `swagger-ui-dist` (`swagger-ui.css`, `swagger-ui-bundle.js`, and an
  `index.html` calling `SwaggerUIBundle({url:'/openapi.json'})`) under
  `internal/server/swagger/`, `go:embed`-ded and served at `GET /docs` via
  `http.FileServerFS`. No CDN (offline/loopback-friendly). Adds ~1.5 MB to the binary —
  accepted; gate behind a `swaggerui` build tag only if binary size becomes an issue.

---

## Phase 4 — `translate mcp`: MCP over stdio (official Go SDK)

Depends only on Phase 0 (the `Service`), **not** on Phases 1–3 — can ship in parallel
with or before the HTTP work. It's the cheapest high-value deliverable.

Dependency: `go get github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.6.1` (Go 1.26.4 satisfies it).

`internal/mcpserver/mcp.go` — `Serve(ctx, svc *appcore.Service) error`:
```go
srv := mcp.NewServer(&mcp.Implementation{Name: "translate", Version: version}, nil)
mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{Name: "translate", Description: "…"}, translateHandler(svc))
mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{Name: "define",    Description: "…"}, defineHandler(svc))
mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{Name: "history",   Description: "…"}, historyHandler(svc))
return srv.Run(ctx, &mcp.StdioTransport{})   // stdin/stdout JSON-RPC
```
Handler signature (SDK derives the input JSON Schema from struct tags):
`func(ctx, *mcp.CallToolRequest, In) (*mcp.CallToolResult, Out, error)`.

| Tool | Input struct (json+jsonschema tags) | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| `translate` | `{Text string; Target, Source string}` | `CallToolResult` text = translation (+ alternatives/notes); `StructuredContent` = `TranslateResult` |
| `define` | `{Word string}` | text = formatted definition; structured = `TranslateResult` |
| `history` | `{Query string; Limit int}` | text = compact record list; structured = `[]store.Record` |

`cmd/mcp.go` → `newMcpCmd()` (registered at `cmd/root.go:95`): builds
`appcore.NewService(cfg, {})` then calls `mcpserver.Serve(cmd.Context(), svc)`.
**Critical**: stdout is the JSON-RPC channel — route all diagnostics/logs to stderr.

---

## Decided design points (no further input needed)
- **Per-request engine/provider switching is out of scope for v1.** The Service warms
  one engine; per-request overrides are limited to what `engine.Request` already honors
  (source/target/preset/instructions/`model`/max_alts). An engine cache keyed by
  `{engine,provider,model}` is a later add if needed.
- **History cross-process safety**: harden `jsonlStore.Add` (`internal/store/jsonl.go`)
  with an advisory `syscall.Flock(fd, LOCK_EX)` on the append fd (unix build tag; Windows
  no-op) so a running server and a concurrent CLI can't interleave >4 KB appends. Small,
  contained; do it in Phase 1.
- **Bilingual/learn** get no API/MCP surface in v1 (structured, non-streaming, CLI-shaped).
- **Daemon lifecycle** (LaunchAgent/menu-bar/auto-start) stays out of scope — `serve`
  only offers the process; keeping it running is a separate backlog item.
- **Raycast extension** stays spawn-based; a "server URL" preference with spawn fallback
  is a separate, opt-in follow-up so non-server users are unaffected.

## Risks / open questions
- `history.jsonl` `Recent`/`Search` re-read the whole file per call (`readAll`,
  `jsonl.go:61`) — fine at personal scale, O(file) per request under a server.
- Port `4155` is a fixed default (collision risk; no discovery in v1) — config'd, per the backlog.
- MCP SDK is a new external dependency; isolated in `internal/mcpserver` so it stays contained.

## Verification
- **Per phase**: `just check` (`fmt` + `vet` + `build`) and `just test`
  (`go test ./cmd/... ./internal/...`) — the new `internal/*` packages are already in scope.
- **Service** (`appcore/service_test.go`): table tests with a stub `engine.Engine`
  emitting canned `Chunk`s; assert pair-target routing (`lang.PairTarget`), history
  recording, and that `Truncated` results are **not** persisted. Real store round-trip
  via `store.OpenJSONL(t.TempDir()+"/h.jsonl")`.
- **HTTP** (`internal/server/*_test.go`): follow the existing `httptest` pattern in
  `internal/engine/llm_test.go`; `httptest.NewRecorder` + mux for JSON endpoints (assert
  status + decoded body; 401 without token, 200 with). SSE: a recorder implements
  `http.Flusher` — assert `event: token`×N + terminal `event: done`; one `httptest.NewServer`
  test reads the body line-by-line and cancels the client to assert ctx propagation.
- **MCP** (`internal/mcpserver/mcp_test.go`): use the SDK's in-memory transport (or an
  `exec` of `translate mcp`) to drive `initialize` → `tools/list` → `tools/call`; assert
  3 tools advertised and a stubbed translation returned.
- **End-to-end manual**:
  - `go run . serve --port 4155` then
    `curl -s localhost:4155/v1/translate -d '{"text":"hello","target":"zh-TW"}'`,
    `curl -N 'localhost:4155/v1/translate/stream?text=hello&target=zh-TW'`, open `localhost:4155/docs`.
  - `just build` then register `{"command":"…/translate","args":["mcp"]}` in an MCP host
    (Claude Desktop/Code) and confirm the `translate`/`define`/`history` tools resolve and run.

## Critical files
- `cmd/build.go` — builders to lift into `internal/appcore` (only `buildEngineSet` keeps `tui`).
- `cmd/root.go` — register `serve`/`mcp` at `:95`; rewire `buildEngine`/`effectiveTarget`(:268)/`toRecord`(:364); `oneShot`(:462) is the template for `Service.buildRequest`; schema auto-upgrade at :146-158.
- `internal/engine/engine.go` — `TranslateResult`(:72) as the JSON/response body; `Engine`/`Chunk`/`Drain`(:225) contract the Service consumes.
- `internal/config/config.go` — add `[server]` table, bump `SchemaVersion`(:43), extend `Default()`.
- `internal/store/{store.go,jsonl.go}` — shared `Store`; add flock to `jsonlStore.Add`.
- `cmd/define.go` — `defineEngine` → `appcore.DefineEngine`.

## References
- `backlog/api-server.md` — original scope, endpoints, open questions.
- Official Go MCP SDK (v1.6.1, stable): https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk · docs https://go.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/
- MCP transports (stdio vs streamable HTTP): https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-06-18/basic/transports
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strings"
14		"sync"
