```text
16		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
17		"golang.org/x/term"
18	
19		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
20		lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
21	
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/bitext"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
26		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
27		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
28		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
29		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
30		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
31	)
32	
33	var (
34		flagTo            string
35		flagFrom          string
36		flagModel         string
37		flagProvider      string
38		flagEngine        string
39		flagTier          string
40		flagPreset        string
41		flagInstructions  string
42		flagPair          bool
43		flagPairWith      string
44		flagLearn         bool
45		flagBilingual     bool
46		flagBilingualMode string
47		flagJSON          bool
48		flagStream        bool
49		flagNoHistory     bool
50		flagDebug         bool
51		flagSpeak         bool
52		flagSpeakLang     string
53	)
54	
55	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
56	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
57		root := &cobra.Command{
58			Use:   "translate [text...]",
59			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
60			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
61				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
62				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
63				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
64			Version:       buildVersion(),
65			SilenceUsage:  true,
66			SilenceErrors: true,
67			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
68			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
69			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
70			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
71			RunE: runRoot,
72		}
73		f := root.PersistentFlags()
74		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
75		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
76		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
77		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
78		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
79		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
80		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
81		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
82		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
83		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
84		f.BoolVar(&flagLearn, "learn", false, "learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)")
85		f.BoolVarP(&flagBilingual, "bilingual", "2", false, "bilingual pipe mode: keep original (with color) + translation beneath (stdin only)")
86		f.StringVar(&flagBilingualMode, "bilingual-mode", "doc", "bilingual strategy: doc (context-aware, one LLM call) | blocks (per-block)")
87		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
88		f.BoolVar(&flagStream, "stream", false, "force token streaming to stdout even when it is not a TTY (for piped consumers, e.g. the Raycast extension)")
89		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
90		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
91		f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud (free TTS)")
92		f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
93	
94		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
95		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
96		return root
97	}
98	
99	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
100	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
101		// Stamp the writing app version into any config Save this run makes.
102		config.Generator = shortVersion()
103		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
104	}
105	
106	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
107	func overrides() config.Overrides {
108		return config.Overrides{
109			Source:       flagFrom,
110			Target:       flagTo,
111			Engine:       flagEngine,
112			Provider:     flagProvider,
113			Model:        flagModel,
114			Tier:         flagTier,
115			Preset:       flagPreset,
116			Instructions: flagInstructions,
117			Pair:         flagPair,
118			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
119			Learn:        flagLearn,
120			Debug:        flagDebug,
121		}
122	}
123	
124	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
125	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
126	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
127	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
128		if len(args) > 0 {
129			return config.ModeCLI
130		}
131		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
132			return config.ModeCLI
133		}
134		return config.ModeTUI
135	}
136	
137	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
138		ctx := cmd.Context()
139	
140		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
141		if err != nil {
142			return err
143		}
144		if created {
145			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
146		} else if cfg.Outdated() {
147			// Schema bumps so far are additive: Load already filled any new fields with
148			// their Default() values in memory, so re-saving materializes them on disk
149			// (every existing value preserved) and re-stamps the schema — no wizard
150			// needed. A genuinely breaking change would instead need an explicit
151			// migration here before the re-save.
152			from := cfg.Schema
153			if err := config.Save(cfg); err != nil {
154				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: config is outdated but auto-upgrade failed (%v) — run `translate init`\n", err)
155			} else {
156				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: upgraded config schema v%d→v%d (new settings use defaults; `translate init` to review)\n", from, config.SchemaVersion)
157			}
158		}
159	
160		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
161		if res.Debug {
162			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
163			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
164			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
165				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
166			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
167				debug.Enable(w)
168			}
169		}
170		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
171		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
172		pairWith := ""
173		if res.Pair {
174			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
175			pairWith = pwm.Code
176		}
177		// Learn mode is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language so
178		// direction routing has two sides to choose between.
179		if res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
180			pairWith = defaultAway(tgt)
181		}
182		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s learn=%v",
183			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith, res.Learn)
184		if res.Pair && !res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
185			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
186		}
187	
188		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
189			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
190		}
191		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
192		if err != nil {
193			return err
194		}
195	
196		// The one-shot CLI path routes a learn request through a bare LLM engine
197		// (bypassing smart-auto/dictionary); the TUI keeps its normal primary engine and
198		// toggles learn at runtime against Params.LearnEngine.
199		learnCLI := res.Learn && invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI
200		if learnCLI && res.Provider == nil {
201			return fmt.Errorf("learn mode requires an LLM provider; check %s", config.Path())
202		}
203		oneShotEng := eng
204		if learnCLI {
205			oneShotEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
206		}
207	
208		st := openStore(cfg)
209		if st != nil {
210			defer st.Close()
211		}
212	
213		switch {
214		case len(args) > 0:
215			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
216			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
217			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
218			if err != nil {
219				return err
220			}
221			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
222			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
223				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
224			}
225			return nil
226	
227		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
228			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
229			if err != nil {
230				return err
231			}
232			// Strip ANSI/SGR escapes so colored piped input (e.g. `tldr rg | translate`)
233			// never pollutes the prompt. The raw bytes are retained only for --bilingual,
234			// which needs the original styling for display.
235			text := strings.TrimSpace(bitext.Strip(string(b)))
236			if text == "" {
237				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
238			}
239			// Bilingual is a multi-block reading view; --json/--learn keep their own
240			// structured output and take precedence.
241			if flagBilingual && !flagJSON && !res.Learn {
242				// doc mode needs an LLM (structured JSON, empty Text confuses
243				// smartauto/chain routing) — build a bare LLM like learn mode; nil when
244				// no provider, in which case runBilingual falls back to per-block.
245				var docEng engine.Engine
246				if res.Provider != nil {
247					docEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
248				}
249				return runBilingual(ctx, oneShotEng, docEng, string(b), src, tgt, res.Instructions, flagBilingualMode)
250			}
251			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
252			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
253			if err != nil {
254				return err
255			}
256			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
257			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
258				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
259			}
260			return nil
261	
262		default:
263			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
264		}
265	}
266	
267	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
268	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
269		if pair && away != "" {
270			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
271			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
272			return t
273		}
274		return home
275	}
276	
277	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
278	func clip(s string, n int) string {
279		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
280		r := []rune(s)
281		if len(r) > n {
282			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
283		}
284		return s
285	}
286	
287	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
288	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
289	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
290		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
291			return nil
292		}
293		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
294			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
295		if err != nil {
296			return nil
297		}
298		return f
299	}
300	
301	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
302	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
303	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
304		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
305			return
306		}
307		// Pair (and learn) mode anchors BOTH languages from config: Target is the home
308		// language, PairWith is the away language, and routing is decided per input. A
309		// remembered target must not override the home here — if the last session
310		// happened to translate into the away language, restoring it as the home makes
311		// home == away and pair mode collapses to a no-op (the "en⇄en" bug). Source is
312		// always auto-detected in pair mode too, so skip the whole override.
313		if res.Pair {
314			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: skipped (pair mode anchors languages from config)")
315			return
316		}
317		stt, err := state.Load()
318		if err != nil || stt == nil {
319			return
320		}
321		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
322			res.Source = stt.Source
323			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
324		}
325		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
326			res.Target = stt.Target
327			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
328		}
329	}
330	
331	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
332	// suppressed with --no-history.
333	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
334		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
335			return nil
336		}
337		path := cfg.History.Path
338		if path == "" {
339			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
340		}
341		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
342		if err != nil {
343			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
344			return nil
345		}
346		return st
347	}
348	
349	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
350	//
351	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
352	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
353	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
354	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
355		if st != nil {
356			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
357		}
358		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
359			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
360		}
361	}
362	
363	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
364	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
365		src := source
366		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
367			src = res.DetectedSource
368		}
369		return store.Record{
370			SourceLang:   src,
371			TargetLang:   target,
372			Engine:       res.Engine,
373			Model:        res.Model,
374			Input:        input,
375			Output:       res.Translation,
376			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
377			Notes:        res.Notes,
378		}
379	}
380	
381	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
382	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
383		mode := "fixed"
384		if source == "auto" {
385			mode = "auto"
386		}
387		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
388			Source:     source,
389			Target:     target,
390			SourceMode: mode,
391			Engine:     engineName,
392		})
393	}
394	
395	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
396	// pair on exit.
397	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
398		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
399		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
400		modelProvider := ""
401		if res.Provider != nil {
402			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
403			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
404		}
405		// Learn mode (^n) runs against a bare LLM engine; nil when no provider is
406		// configured, in which case the toggle refuses.
407		var learnEng engine.Engine
408		if res.Provider != nil {
409			learnEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
410		}
411		p := tui.Params{
412			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
413			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
414			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
415			Store:         st,
416			Source:        source,
417			Target:        target,
418			Pair:          res.Pair,
419			PairWith:      pairWith,
420			Learn:         res.Learn,
421			LearnEngine:   learnEng,
422			Model:         res.Model,
423			Preset:        res.Preset,
424			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
425			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
426			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
427			Speaker:       tuiSpeaker(res.Cfg),
428			Foreign:       speakForeign(res.Cfg, pairWith),
429		}
430		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
431		if err != nil {
432			return err
433		}
434		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
435			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
436				s, t := fm.Pair()
437				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
438			}
439		}
440		return nil
441	}
442	
443	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
444	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
445	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
446		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
447		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
448		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
449			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
450		}
451		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
452			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
453		}
454		return sm.Code, tm.Code
455	}
456	
457	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
458	//
459	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
460	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
461	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
462	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
463		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
464		// Stream when the config asks for it on a TTY, or when --stream forces it (a
465		// piped consumer like the Raycast extension). --json/--learn are structured
466		// and never stream.
467		stream := (flagStream || (streamPref && stdoutTTY)) && !flagJSON && !learn
468	
469		req := engine.Request{
470			Text:     text,
471			Source:   source,
472			Target:   target,
473			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
474			Stream:   stream,
475			Preset:   preset,
476			Extra:    instructions,
477			Pair:     pair,
478			PairHome: pairHome,
479			PairAway: pairAway,
480			Learn:    learn,
481		}
482		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
483		if err != nil {
484			return nil, err
485		}
486	
487		// streamed tracks whether any visible token actually reached stdout. We may
488		// request streaming (stream == true) yet the engine that answers ignores it and
489		// returns a single terminal result with zero tokens — every non-streaming engine
490		// does (the dictionary/smart-dict single-word path, google, a provider that fell
491		// back to a non-streaming completion). In that case nothing was printed during
492		// draining, so the final result must still be emitted below.
493		var onTok func(string)
494		streamed := false
495		if stream {
496			onTok = func(t string) {
497				if t != "" {
498					streamed = true
499				}
500				fmt.Print(t)
501			}
502		}
503		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
504		if err != nil {
505			if streamed {
506				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial streamed line before the error surfaces
507			}
508			return nil, err
509		}
510	
511		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
512		// failed and why, and how to switch.
513		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
514			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
515		}
516		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
517			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
518		}
519	
520		switch {
521		case flagJSON:
522			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
523			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
524			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
525				return nil, err
526			}
527		case res.Learn != nil:
528			fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
529		case streamed:
530			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
531		default:
532			// Piped output, or a non-streaming engine answered a stream request with no
533			// tokens (dictionary single-word, google, …): emit the full result now.
534			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
535		}
536		return res, nil
537	}
538	
539	// runBilingual renders the "immersive" bilingual view for piped input: every
540	// original block is printed verbatim (ANSI/color intact) and a translation is shown
541	// beneath each prose block; indented command/code blocks are echoed untranslated.
542	// mode "doc" (default) translates the whole document in one context-aware call via
543	// docEng (a bare LLM); "blocks" (or a doc-mode fallback) translates each prose block
544	// in isolation via blockEng. Pair and learn routing are out of scope for this view.
545	func runBilingual(ctx context.Context, blockEng, docEng engine.Engine, raw, source, target, instructions, mode string) error {
546		blocks := bitext.Split(raw)
547		if len(blocks) == 0 {
548			return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
549		}
550	
551		var translations map[int]string
552		switch mode {
553		case "blocks":
554			translations = bilingualBlocks(ctx, blockEng, blocks, source, target, instructions)
555		default: // "doc"
556			if docEng != nil {
557				if t, ok := bilingualDoc(ctx, docEng, blocks, source, target, instructions); ok {
558					translations = t
559				}
560			}
561			if translations == nil { // no LLM provider, or the doc call failed/was unparseable
562				fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "translate: bilingual: doc mode unavailable, using per-block")
563				translations = bilingualBlocks(ctx, blockEng, blocks, source, target, instructions)
564			}
565		}
566	
567		dim := dimFunc(term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd())))
568		fmt.Print(bitext.Render(blocks, translations, dim))
569		return nil
570	}
571	
572	// bilingualBlocks translates each prose block in isolation (concurrent, bounded).
573	// The original strategy: works with any engine, but has no cross-block context.
574	func bilingualBlocks(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, blocks []bitext.Block, source, target, instructions string) map[int]string {
575		translations := make(map[int]string)
576		errs := make(map[int]error)
577		var mu sync.Mutex
578		var wg sync.WaitGroup
579		sem := make(chan struct{}, 5) // bound concurrent LLM calls
580	
581		for i, blk := range blocks {
582			if blk.Kind != bitext.Prose {
583				continue
584			}
585			wg.Add(1)
586			go func(i int, plain string) {
587				defer wg.Done()
588				sem <- struct{}{}
589				defer func() { <-sem }()
590	
591				// Force concise (Stream:false, Preset:"") so each block maps to exactly
592				// one translation — contextual/dictionary presets would reshape output.
593				r, err := translateOnce(ctx, eng, engine.Request{
594					Text:   plain,
595					Source: source,
596					Target: target,
597					Mode:   engine.ModeTranslate,
598					Extra:  instructions,
599				})
600				mu.Lock()
601				defer mu.Unlock()
602				if err != nil {
603					errs[i] = err
604					return
605				}
606				tr := strings.TrimSpace(r.Translation)
607				// Drop echoes (proper nouns / already-target text): nothing to add beneath.
608				if tr != "" && !strings.EqualFold(tr, strings.TrimSpace(plain)) {
609					translations[i] = tr
610				}
611			}(i, blk.Plain)
612		}
613		wg.Wait()
614	
615		// Never fail silently: report untranslated blocks (originals still printed).
616		if len(errs) > 0 {
617			var sample error
618			for _, e := range errs {
619				sample = e
620				break
621			}
622			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %d block(s) not translated (%v)\n", len(errs), sample)
623		}
624		return translations
625	}
626	
627	// bilingualDoc translates the whole document in ONE context-aware structured call:
628	// the model sees every block (prose + code as context) and returns a JSON map of
629	// prose-segment number → translation. Returns ok=false when structured output isn't
630	// available (no LLM, error, or unparseable/empty reply) so the caller falls back.
631	func bilingualDoc(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, blocks []bitext.Block, source, target, instructions string) (map[int]string, bool) {
632		var segs []engine.Segment
633		proseBlock := make(map[int]int) // prose-segment number (1-based) → block index
634		n := 0
635		for i, blk := range blocks {
636			switch blk.Kind {
637			case bitext.Prose:
638				n++
639				proseBlock[n] = i
640				segs = append(segs, engine.Segment{Text: blk.Plain})
641			case bitext.Code:
642				segs = append(segs, engine.Segment{Text: blk.Plain, Code: true})
643			}
644		}
645		if n == 0 {
646			return map[int]string{}, true // nothing to translate
647		}
648	
649		r, err := translateOnce(ctx, eng, engine.Request{
650			Bilingual: true,
651			Segments:  segs,
652			Source:    source,
653			Target:    target,
654			Mode:      engine.ModeTranslate,
655			Extra:     instructions,
656		})
657		if err != nil {
658			debug.Logf("bilingual doc: LLM call failed: %v", err)
659			return nil, false
660		}
661		if r == nil || len(r.Bilingual) == 0 {
662			warn := ""
663			if r != nil && len(r.Warnings) > 0 {
664				warn = r.Warnings[0]
665			}
666			debug.Logf("bilingual doc: empty/unparseable structured result (warnings: %q)", warn)
667			return nil, false
668		}
669	
670		translations := make(map[int]string)
671		for num, tr := range r.Bilingual {
672			bi, ok := proseBlock[num]
673			if !ok {
674				continue
675			}
676			t := strings.TrimSpace(tr)
677			if t != "" && !strings.EqualFold(t, strings.TrimSpace(blocks[bi].Plain)) {
678				translations[bi] = t
679			}
680		}
681		return translations, true
682	}
683	
684	// translateOnce runs a single non-streaming translation and returns the result,
685	// draining the engine channel with no token callback. It is the printing-free core
686	// shared by bilingual mode.
687	func translateOnce(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, req engine.Request) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
688		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
689		if err != nil {
690			return nil, err
691		}
692		return engine.Drain(ch, nil)
693	}
694	
695	// dimFunc returns the styler for bilingual translation lines. It dims (grey) only
696	// when stdout is a TTY and NO_COLOR is unset, so piping the output onward stays
697	// clean; otherwise it is the identity function. The original block's own ANSI is
698	// never touched — only our added translation lines are styled.
699	func dimFunc(stdoutTTY bool) func(string) string {
700		if !stdoutTTY || os.Getenv("NO_COLOR") != "" {
701			return func(s string) string { return s }
702		}
703		style := lg.NewStyle().Foreground(lg.Color("#6C6C6C"))
704		return func(s string) string { return style.Render(s) }
705	}
706	
707	// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
708	// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
709	// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
710	func defaultAway(home string) string {
711		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
712			return "zh-TW"
713		}
714		return "en"
715	}
716	
717	// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
718	func renderLearnCLI(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
719		l := res.Learn
720		if l == nil {
721			return res.Translation + "\n"
722		}
723		var b strings.Builder
724		if l.Direction == "correct" {
725			corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
726			if corrected == "" {
727				corrected = res.Translation
728			}
729			b.WriteString("✔ " + corrected + "\n")
730			if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
731				b.WriteString("  ↳ " + s + "\n")
732			}
733			for _, is := range l.Issues {
734				frag := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(is.Span+" → "+is.Fix, " → "), " → "))
735				expl := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation)
736				line := expl
737				switch {
738				case frag != "" && expl != "":
739					line = frag + ": " + expl
740				case frag != "":
741					line = frag
742				}
743				if line != "" {
744					b.WriteString("  ✎ " + line + "\n")
745				}
746			}
747		} else {
748			tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
749			if tr == "" {
750				tr = res.Translation
751			}
752			b.WriteString(tr + "\n")
753			for _, v := range l.Vocab {
754				head := "  • " + v.Term
755				if v.Pos != "" {
756					head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
757				}
758				if v.Phonetic != "" {
759					head += " " + v.Phonetic
760				}
761				if v.Meaning != "" {
762					head += " — " + v.Meaning
763				}
764				b.WriteString(head + "\n")
765			}
766			for _, ex := range l.Examples {
767				b.WriteString("  ✎ " + ex.Foreign + "\n")
768				if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
769					b.WriteString("    ↳ " + s + "\n")
770				}
771			}
772		}
773		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
774			b.WriteString("  ⓘ " + s + "\n")
775		}
776		return b.String()
777	}
778
```

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		if flagSpeak && cfg.TTS.Enabled {
64			speakDefine(cmd.Context(), cfg, res2)
65		}
66		return nil
67	}
68	
69	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
70	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
71	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
72	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
73		cfg := res.Cfg
74		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
75			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
76		}
77		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
78			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
79		}
80		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
81	}
82	
83	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
84	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
85		d := res.Dictionary
86		if d == nil {
87			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
88				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
89			}
90			return res.Translation + "\n"
91		}
92		var b strings.Builder
93		head := d.Word
94		if d.Phonetic != "" {
95			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
96		}
97		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
98		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
99			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
100			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
101				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
102					break
103				}
104				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
105				if def.Example != "" {
106					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
107				}
108			}
109		}
110		return b.String()
111	}
112
```

```text
70	// General holds behavior settings.
71	type General struct {
72		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
73		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
74		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
75		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
76		Learn             bool   `toml:"learn"`               // learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)
77		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
78		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
79		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
80		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | smartauto | llm | google | <provider>
81		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
82		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
83		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
84		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
85		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
86		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
87		Debug             bool   `toml:"debug"` // verbose decision logging (also --debug / TRANSLATE_DEBUG)
88	}
89	
90	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
91	type Chain struct {
92		Order []string `toml:"order"`
93	}
94	
95	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
96	type Provider struct {
97		Name      string `toml:"name"`
98		Type      string `toml:"type"` // openai | ollama | openrouter | litellm | generic
99		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
100		Model     string `toml:"model"`
101		ModelFast string `toml:"model_fast,omitempty"`
102		ModelMax  string `toml:"model_max,omitempty"`
103		APIKeyEnv string `toml:"api_key_env,omitempty"` // "" => no Authorization header
104	}
105	
106	// Google configures the free translate_a/single endpoint.
107	type Google struct {
108		Enabled   bool     `toml:"enabled"`
109		Endpoint  string   `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
110		ExtraDT   []string `toml:"extra_dt,omitempty"`
111		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
112		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
113	}
114	
115	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.
116	type Dict struct {
117		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`
118		Source   string `toml:"source"` // local (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT) | api (dictionaryapi.dev)
119		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
120		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
121		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`
122		Wordlist string `toml:"wordlist,omitempty"` // "" => /usr/share/dict/words if present
123	
124		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
125		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
126		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
127		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
128		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
129		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
130	}
131	
132	// TTS configures free, cross-platform text-to-speech (pronunciation playback).
133	// Backends are tried in Order: "native" (macOS say / Linux espeak / Windows SAPI,
134	// offline) then "google" (translate_tts MP3 + a discovered player).
135	type TTS struct {
136		Enabled       bool              `toml:"enabled"`
137		AutoSpeak     bool              `toml:"auto_speak"`        // CLI: speak every result without --speak
138		Order         []string          `toml:"order,omitempty"`   // e.g. ["native","google"]
139		Foreign       string            `toml:"foreign,omitempty"` // the 副/away language to speak; "" => derive
140		PreferForeign bool              `toml:"prefer_foreign"`    // auto-side selection prefers the foreign side
141		Rate          int               `toml:"rate,omitempty"`    // native words/min; 0 => backend default
142		Voices        map[string]string `toml:"voices,omitempty"`  // lang -> native voice override
143		GoogleTTSURL  string            `toml:"google_tts_url,omitempty"`
144		UserAgent     string            `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
145		TimeoutMs     int               `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
146		CacheDir      string            `toml:"cache_dir,omitempty"`
147		Player        string            `toml:"player,omitempty"` // forced audio player binary
148	}
149	
150	// History configures translation history storage.
151	type History struct {
152		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
153		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
154		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
155	}
156	
157	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
158	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
159	type SmartDict struct {
160		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
161		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
162		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
163		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
164		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
165		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
166	}
167	
168	// Recommended copilot-proxy model ids per tier. Claude models are served via the
169	// Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages); the LLM engine routes them there
170	// automatically (they 400 on /chat/completions).
171	const (
172		ModelDefault = "claude-sonnet-5"  // balanced
173		ModelFast    = "claude-haiku-4-5" // snappy — the out-of-box default tier
174		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
175	)
176	
177	// Default returns a config with sensible defaults, written on first run.
178	func Default() *Config {
179		return &Config{
180			General: General{
181				DefaultTarget:     "en",
182				DefaultSource:     "auto",
183				Pair:              false,
184				PairWith:          "en", // used when pair mode is enabled
185				RememberLastPair:  true,
186				LiveTranslate:     false, // off by default to avoid spamming LLM/API while typing
187				DebounceMs:        700,   // when live is on, wait longer before firing
188				Engine:            "auto",
189				Tier:              "fast", // haiku by default — snappy for short, quick translations
190				Preset:            "contextual",
191				AlternativesCount: 3,
192				Stream:            true,
193				Color:             "auto",
194			},
195			Chain: Chain{Order: []string{"copilot", "ollama", "google", "dict"}},
196			Providers: []Provider{
197				{
198					Name:      "copilot",
199					Type:      "openai",
200					BaseURL:   "http://localhost:4141/v1",
201					Model:     ModelDefault,
202					ModelFast: ModelFast,
203					ModelMax:  ModelMax,
204				},
205				{
206					Name:    "ollama",
207					Type:    "ollama",
208					BaseURL: "http://localhost:11434/v1",
209					Model:   "llama3.2:3b",
210				},
211				{
212					Name:      "openrouter",
213					Type:      "openrouter",
214					BaseURL:   "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
215					Model:     "anthropic/claude-sonnet-5",
216					APIKeyEnv: "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
217				},
218			},
219			Google: Google{
220				Enabled:   true,
221				Endpoint:  "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single",
222				ExtraDT:   []string{"bd", "at"},
223				UserAgent: "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli",
224				TimeoutMs: 4000,
225			},
226			Dict: Dict{
227				Enabled:      true,
228				Source:       "local", // CC-CEDICT (zh→en) + ECDICT (en→zh); run `translate dict update`
229				Endpoint:     "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries",
230				Lang:         "en",
231				Fuzzy:        true,
232				CedictURL:    "https://www.mdbg.net/chinese/export/cedict/cedict_1_0_ts_utf-8_mdbg.txt.gz",
233				EcdictURL:    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv",
234				AutoDownload: false, // explicit `dict update` is the blessed path
235				APIFallback:  true,  // English lookups still work before ECDICT is installed
236			},
237			SmartDict: SmartDict{
238				CloseDistance: 1,            // distance-1 typos stay "did you mean"; farther → LLM
239				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences
240				DefineDefault: true,         // `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
241			},
242			TTS: TTS{
243				Enabled:       true,
244				AutoSpeak:     false, // opt-in per call via --speak / TUI ^s
245				Order:         []string{"native", "google"},
246				Foreign:       "", // "" => derive the non-Chinese side to speak
247				PreferForeign: true,
248				GoogleTTSURL:  "https://translate.google.com/translate_tts",
249				UserAgent:     "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli",
250				TimeoutMs:     5000,
251			},
252			History: History{
253				Enabled: true,
254				Backend: "jsonl",
255			},
256		}
257	}
258	
259	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
260	func Path() string {
261		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
262			return p
263		}
264		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
265	}
266	
267	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
268	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
269	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
270		p := Path()
271		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
272		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
273			cfg = Default()
274			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
275				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
276			}
277			return cfg, true, nil
278		}
279		if err != nil {
280			return nil, false, err
281		}
282		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
283		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
284			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
285		}
286		return cfg, false, nil
287	}
288	
289	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename). It stamps the current
290	// schema generation and (when known) the writing app version, so a later build
291	// can detect an outdated config.
292	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
293		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
294			return err
295		}
296		cfg.Schema = SchemaVersion
297		if Generator != "" {
298			cfg.Version = Generator
299		}
300		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
301		if err != nil {
302			return err
303		}
304		p := Path()
305		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
306		if err != nil {
307			return err
308		}
309		tmpName := tmp.Name()
310		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
311		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
312			tmp.Close()
313			return err
314		}
315		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
316			return err
317		}
318		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
319	}
320	
321	// Outdated reports whether the loaded config predates the current schema, i.e.
322	// it was written by an older build (or before schema tracking existed, in which
323	// case the key is absent and reads as 0). The caller uses it to nudge a re-init.
324	func (c *Config) Outdated() bool { return c.Schema < SchemaVersion }
325	
326	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
327	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
328		for i := range c.Providers {
329			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
330				return &c.Providers[i]
331			}
332		}
333		return nil
334	}
335	
336	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
337	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
338	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
339		switch tier {
340		case "fast":
341			if p.ModelFast != "" {
342				return p.ModelFast
343			}
344		case "max":
345			if p.ModelMax != "" {
346				return p.ModelMax
347			}
348		}
349		return p.Model
350	}
351
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Learn        bool // --learn forces learning mode on (implies pair)
21		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
22	}
23	
24	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
25	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
26	type Mode int
27	
28	const (
29		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
30		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
31	)
32	
33	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
34	type Resolved struct {
35		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
36		Target       string
37		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
38		Provider     *Provider
39		Model        string
40		Tier         string
41		Preset       string
42		Instructions string
43		Pair         bool
44		PairWith     string
45		Learn        bool
46		Stream       bool
47		Color        string
48		Debug        bool
49	
50		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
51		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
52		LiveTranslate bool
53		DebounceMs    int
54	
55		Cfg *Config
56	}
57	
58	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
59	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
60	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
61	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
62	
63	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
64	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
65		switch mode {
66		case ModeCLI:
67			return c.CLI
68		case ModeTUI:
69			return c.TUI
70		}
71		return nil
72	}
73	
74	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
75	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
76	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
77		if ov == nil {
78			return
79		}
80		if ov.Engine != nil {
81			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
82		}
83		if ov.Tier != nil {
84			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
85		}
86		if ov.Preset != nil {
87			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
88		}
89		if ov.Instructions != nil {
90			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
91		}
92		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
93			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
94		}
95		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
96			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
97		}
98		if ov.Pair != nil {
99			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
100		}
101		if ov.PairWith != nil {
102			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
103		}
104		if ov.Learn != nil {
105			g.Learn = *ov.Learn
106		}
107		if ov.Stream != nil {
108			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
109		}
110		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
111			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
112		}
113		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
114			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
115		}
116		if ov.Debug != nil {
117			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
122	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
123	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
124		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
125		// the on-disk config is untouched).
126		g := c.General
127		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
128		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
129	
130		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
131			if flag != "" {
132				return flag
133			}
134			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
135				return v
136			}
137			return cfgVal
138		}
139	
140		r := Resolved{
141			Cfg:           c,
142			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
143			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
144			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
145			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
146			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
147			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
148			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
149			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
150			Learn:         o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "",
151			Color:         g.Color,
152			Stream:        g.Stream,
153			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
154			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
155			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
156		}
157	
158		// Learning mode is inherently bidirectional; it reuses pair's home/away
159		// direction routing, so enabling learn enables pair.
160		if r.Learn {
161			r.Pair = true
162		}
163	
164		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
165		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
166		if provName == "" {
167			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
168				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
169			} else {
170				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
171			}
172		}
173		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
174	
175		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
176		// provider's tier model.
177		cfgModel := ""
178		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
179			cfgModel = *ov.Model
180		}
181		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
182		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
183			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
184		}
185		return r
186	}
187	
188	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
189	// else the first configured provider, else "".
190	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
191		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
192			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
193				return name
194			}
195		}
196		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
197			return c.Providers[0].Name
198		}
199		return ""
200	}
201
```

 `./internal/store/store.go`

```text
1	// Package store persists translation history. v1 uses an append-only JSONL file
2	// with in-memory fuzzy search behind the Store interface; a sqlite+FTS5 backend
3	// can drop in later without changing callers.
4	package store
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// Record is one history entry (also the JSONL line shape).
12	type Record struct {
13		ID           string    `json:"id"`
14		TS           time.Time `json:"ts"`
15		SourceLang   string    `json:"source_lang"`
16		TargetLang   string    `json:"target_lang"`
17		Engine       string    `json:"engine"`
18		Model        string    `json:"model,omitempty"`
19		Input        string    `json:"input"`
20		Output       string    `json:"output"`
21		Alternatives []string  `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
22		Notes        string    `json:"notes,omitempty"`
23		Favorite     bool      `json:"favorite,omitempty"`
24	}
25	
26	// Store is the history persistence interface.
27	type Store interface {
28		// Add assigns an ID and timestamp, persists the record, and returns it.
29		Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error)
30		// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
31		Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error)
32		// Search returns records fuzzy-matching query (over input+output), best first.
33		Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error)
34		// Close releases any resources.
35		Close() error
36	}
37
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:39:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/jsonl.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10		"strconv"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/sahilm/fuzzy"
16	)
17	
18	// jsonlStore is an append-only JSONL-backed Store with in-memory fuzzy search.
19	type jsonlStore struct {
20		path string
21		mu   sync.Mutex
22	}
23	
24	// OpenJSONL opens (creating parent dirs for) a JSONL history file.
25	func OpenJSONL(path string) (Store, error) {
26		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o700); err != nil {
27			return nil, err
28		}
29		return &jsonlStore{path: path}, nil
30	}
31	
32	func (s *jsonlStore) Close() error { return nil }
33	
34	// Add stamps the record with an id + timestamp and appends it as one JSON line.
35	func (s *jsonlStore) Add(ctx context.Context, r Record) (Record, error) {
36		if r.ID == "" {
37			r.ID = newID()
38		}
39		if r.TS.IsZero() {
40			r.TS = time.Now()
41		}
42		line, err := json.Marshal(r)
43		if err != nil {
44			return r, err
45		}
46	
47		s.mu.Lock()
48		defer s.mu.Unlock()
49		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o600)
50		if err != nil {
51			return r, err
52		}
53		defer f.Close()
54		if _, err := f.Write(append(line, '\n')); err != nil {
55			return r, err
56		}
57		return r, nil
58	}
59	
60	// readAll loads every record in chronological (file) order.
61	func (s *jsonlStore) readAll() ([]Record, error) {
62		s.mu.Lock()
63		defer s.mu.Unlock()
64		b, err := os.ReadFile(s.path)
65		if err != nil {
66			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
67				return nil, nil
68			}
69			return nil, err
70		}
71		var out []Record
72		for _, line := range strings.Split(string(b), "\n") {
73			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
74			if line == "" {
75				continue
76			}
77			var r Record
78			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &r) == nil {
79				out = append(out, r)
80			}
81		}
82		return out, nil
83	}
84	
85	// Recent returns up to limit records, newest first.
86	func (s *jsonlStore) Recent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
87		all, err := s.readAll()
88		if err != nil {
89			return nil, err
90		}
91		reverse(all)
92		if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
93			all = all[:limit]
94		}
95		return all, nil
96	}
97	
98	// Search fuzzy-ranks records by "input ⏎ output", best match first.
99	func (s *jsonlStore) Search(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Record, error) {
100		all, err := s.readAll()
101		if err != nil {
102			return nil, err
103		}
104		q := strings.TrimSpace(query)
105		if q == "" {
106			reverse(all)
107			if limit > 0 && len(all) > limit {
108				all = all[:limit]
109			}
110			return all, nil
111		}
112		hay := make([]string, len(all))
113		for i, r := range all {
114			hay[i] = r.Input + " " + r.Output
115		}
116		matches := fuzzy.Find(q, hay)
117		out := make([]Record, 0, len(matches))
118		for _, m := range matches {
119			out = append(out, all[m.Index])
120			if limit > 0 && len(out) >= limit {
121				break
122			}
123		}
124		return out, nil
125	}
126	
127	func reverse(rs []Record) {
128		for i, j := 0, len(rs)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
129			rs[i], rs[j] = rs[j], rs[i]
130		}
131	}
132	
133	// newID is a sortable-enough unique id: base36 nanos + 4 random bytes.
134	func newID() string {
135		var b [4]byte
136		_, _ = rand.Read(b[:])
137		return strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 36) + "-" + hex.EncodeToString(b[:])
138	}
139
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
// Package appcore holds the transport-agnostic core shared by every front-end:
// the engine builders (constructed from resolved config) and a Service that wraps
// a warm engine + history store behind Translate/Define/History methods. The
// one-shot CLI, the HTTP server (translate serve), and the MCP server (translate
// mcp) all drive this same core and diverge only at their transport edge.
//
// It imports only config/engine/store/lang/debug — never tui or cmd — so both cmd
// and the server packages can import it without an import cycle.
package appcore

import (
	"fmt"
	"time"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
)

// BuildEngine constructs the Engine for an invocation from resolved settings.
// engine="auto" builds a fallback Chain over the providers named in chain.order;
// engine="smartauto" builds the smart default (dictionary for words, LLM for
// phrases, bidirectional); a named provider builds a single LLM engine.
func BuildEngine(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
	cfg := res.Cfg

	switch res.Engine {
	case "auto":
		return BuildAutoChain(res)
	case "smartauto":
		// Needs an LLM provider for the word-miss fallback and phrase translation;
		// without one, behave like the plain auto chain (google/dict).
		if res.Provider == nil {
			return BuildAutoChain(res)
		}
		return SmartAutoFromConfig(res), nil
	case "google":
		return GoogleFromConfig(cfg), nil
	case "llm":
		// "llm" means "the resolved provider" (already chosen by Resolve).
		if res.Provider != nil {
			return LLMFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
		}
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
	default:
		// A named provider (e.g. "copilot", "ollama").
		if res.Provider != nil {
			return LLMFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), nil
		}
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown engine/provider %q (check %s)", res.Engine, config.Path())
	}
}

// BuildAutoChain builds the "auto" fallback Chain over the providers/google named
// in chain.order. (google/dict engines otherwise join only via smart-auto or the
// TUI ^e cycle.)
func BuildAutoChain(res config.Resolved) (engine.Engine, error) {
	cfg := res.Cfg
	var engines []engine.Engine
	for _, name := range cfg.Chain.Order {
		switch {
		case cfg.ProviderByName(name) != nil:
			p := cfg.ProviderByName(name)
			engines = append(engines, LLMFromProvider(p, p.ModelForTier(res.Tier)))
		case name == "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled:
			engines = append(engines, GoogleFromConfig(cfg))
		}
		// "dict" is wired in via smart-auto / the TUI engine cycle.
	}
	if len(engines) == 0 {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no engines available in chain.order (check %s)", config.Path())
	}
	if len(engines) == 1 {
		return engines[0], nil
	}
	return engine.NewChain(engines, 5*time.Second), nil
}

// LLMFromProvider builds an LLM engine for a provider using the given model id.
func LLMFromProvider(p *config.Provider, model string) *engine.LLMEngine {
	if model == "" {
		model = p.Model
	}
	return engine.NewLLM(engine.LLMConfig{
		Name:      p.Name,
		BaseURL:   p.BaseURL,
		Model:     model,
		APIKeyEnv: p.APIKeyEnv,
	})
}

// GoogleFromConfig builds the free Google engine from config.
func GoogleFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) *engine.GoogleEngine {
	return engine.NewGoogle(engine.GoogleConfig{
		Endpoint:  cfg.Google.Endpoint,
		ExtraDT:   cfg.Google.ExtraDT,
		UserAgent: cfg.Google.UserAgent,
		Timeout:   time.Duration(cfg.Google.TimeoutMs) * time.Millisecond,
	})
}

// DictFromConfig builds the dictionary engine from config: the offline bilingual
// engine (source=local, default) or the dictionaryapi.dev engine (source=api).
func DictFromConfig(cfg *config.Config) engine.Engine {
	if cfg.Dict.Source == "api" {
		return engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
			Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
			Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
			Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
			Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
		})
	}
	var apiFb *engine.DictEngine
	if cfg.Dict.APIFallback {
		apiFb = engine.NewDict(engine.DictConfig{
			Endpoint: cfg.Dict.Endpoint,
			Lang:     cfg.Dict.Lang,
			Fuzzy:    cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
			Wordlist: cfg.Dict.Wordlist,
		})
	}
	return engine.NewLocalDict(engine.LocalDictConfig{
		Dir:          cfg.Dict.Dir,
		CedictURL:    cfg.Dict.CedictURL,
		EcdictURL:    cfg.Dict.EcdictURL,
		AutoDownload: cfg.Dict.AutoDownload,
		Fuzzy:        cfg.Dict.Fuzzy,
		APIFallback:  apiFb,
	})
}

// SmartDictFromConfig builds the smart-dict engine: the plain dictionary plus an
// LLM fallback (resolved provider/model) for misses and too-weak fuzzy matches.
// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
func SmartDictFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
	cfg := res.Cfg
	return engine.NewSmartDict(DictFromConfig(cfg), LLMFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model), engine.SmartDictConfig{
		CloseDistance: cfg.SmartDict.CloseDistance,
		Preset:        cfg.SmartDict.Preset,
	})
}

// SmartAutoFromConfig builds the smart-auto default: a single word/term is a
// dictionary lookup (smart-dict, with an LLM fallback), and a phrase is an LLM
// translation via the auto chain (so phrases keep provider→google resilience).
// The caller must ensure res.Provider != nil.
func SmartAutoFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
	llm, err := BuildAutoChain(res)
	if err != nil {
		// No chain engines (unlikely once a provider exists): fall back to the bare
		// resolved provider so phrases can still translate.
		llm = LLMFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
	}
	return engine.NewSmartAuto(SmartDictFromConfig(res), llm)
}

// LearnEngineFromConfig builds the engine for learning mode: a bare LLM engine
// (the resolved provider/model), bypassing smart-auto/dictionary routing so a
// single word still gets the structured tutor treatment. Caller ensures Provider != nil.
func LearnEngineFromConfig(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
	return LLMFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
}

// DefineEngine picks the dictionary engine for a definition lookup: the plain
// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
// available and not overridden. plain forces plain; smart forces smart. It is the
// single source of truth shared by `translate define` and the Service.
func DefineEngine(res config.Resolved, plain, smart bool) engine.Engine {
	cfg := res.Cfg
	if plain || res.Provider == nil {
		return DictFromConfig(cfg)
	}
	if smart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
		return SmartDictFromConfig(res)
	}
	return DictFromConfig(cfg)
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```go
package appcore

import (
	"strings"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
)

// EffectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
// It is the single source of truth for pair routing, shared by the one-shot CLI
// and the Service.
func EffectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
	if pair && away != "" {
		t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
		debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
		return t
	}
	return home
}

// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
func clip(s string, n int) string {
	s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
	r := []rune(s)
	if len(r) > n {
		return string(r[:n]) + "…"
	}
	return s
}

// ToRecord builds a history Record from a translation result. When the source was
// "auto", the engine's detected language is substituted so history stays specific.
func ToRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
	src := source
	if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
		src = res.DetectedSource
	}
	return store.Record{
		SourceLang:   src,
		TargetLang:   target,
		Engine:       res.Engine,
		Model:        res.Model,
		Input:        input,
		Output:       res.Translation,
		Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
		Notes:        res.Notes,
	}
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:42:07Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```go
package appcore

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
)

// Service is the warm, transport-agnostic core: it builds the translate/define
// engines once at startup and holds the history store for the process lifetime.
// A single Service is safe to share across many goroutines — the engines are
// concurrency-safe and the store serializes its own writes.
//
// The Service deliberately never touches state.json (the CLI's remembered pair):
// a long-lived server must not fight the interactive CLI over that file.
type Service struct {
	cfg      *config.Config
	res      config.Resolved
	trans    engine.Engine // warm translate engine
	define   engine.Engine // warm define engine
	store    store.Store   // shared history; nil when disabled / NoHistory
	provider string        // resolved provider name (for per-request model overrides)

	source string // resolved default source (usually "auto")
	home   string // resolved home target
	away   string // resolved pair "away" language; "" when pair is off
	pair   bool
}

// Options tunes Service construction.
type Options struct {
	NoHistory bool // do not open/record history
}

// Params is one translation request. Empty fields fall back to the Service
// defaults resolved at startup; Pair is a tri-state (nil => use the default).
type Params struct {
	Text         string
	Source       string // "" => service default (usually "auto")
	Target       string // "" => service home target
	Preset       string // "" => service default
	Instructions string // "" => service default
	Model        string // "" => the warm engine's model
	MaxAlts      int    // 0 => engine default
	Pair         *bool  // nil => service default; non-nil overrides
}

// NewService resolves config (flags empty, env honored) once, warms both engines,
// and opens the history store. It fails fast on a misconfigured provider, mirroring
// the CLI's guard in runRoot.
func NewService(cfg *config.Config, opt Options) (*Service, error) {
	res := cfg.Resolve(config.Overrides{}, config.ModeCLI)
	if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
	}
	trans, err := BuildEngine(res)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	def := DefineEngine(res, false, false)
	st := openStore(cfg, opt.NoHistory)
	return newService(cfg, res, trans, def, st), nil
}

// newService assembles a Service from already-built parts. It is the unexported
// seam used by tests to inject stub engines and an in-memory store (no network).
func newService(cfg *config.Config, res config.Resolved, trans, def engine.Engine, st store.Store) *Service {
	source, home, away, pair := resolveRouting(res)
	provider := ""
	if res.Provider != nil {
		provider = res.Provider.Name
	}
	return &Service{
		cfg: cfg, res: res, trans: trans, define: def, store: st,
		provider: provider, source: source, home: home, away: away, pair: pair,
	}
}

// resolveRouting fuzzy-resolves the source/target/pair-with languages the same way
// the CLI does (resolvePair + lang.Resolve), so "chinese" → "zh" etc.
func resolveRouting(res config.Resolved) (source, home, away string, pair bool) {
	sm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.Source)
	tm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.Target)
	source, home = sm.Code, tm.Code
	pair = res.Pair
	if res.Pair {
		pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
		away = pwm.Code
	}
	return source, home, away, pair
}

// Translate performs a non-streaming translation and records it in history.
func (s *Service) Translate(ctx context.Context, p Params) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	return s.translate(ctx, p, false, nil)
}

// TranslateStream performs a streaming translation, delivering tokens to onToken
// as they arrive, and records the final result in history.
func (s *Service) TranslateStream(ctx context.Context, p Params, onToken func(string)) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	return s.translate(ctx, p, true, onToken)
}

func (s *Service) translate(ctx context.Context, p Params, stream bool, onToken func(string)) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	req := s.buildRequest(p, stream)
	ch, err := s.trans.Translate(ctx, req)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onToken)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	s.record(ctx, res, req.Text, req.Source, req.Target)
	return res, nil
}

// buildRequest maps Params onto an engine.Request, mirroring the CLI's oneShot +
// EffectiveTarget so the API and CLI route identically.
func (s *Service) buildRequest(p Params, stream bool) engine.Request {
	source := firstNonEmpty(p.Source, s.source)
	home := firstNonEmpty(p.Target, s.home)
	pair := s.pair
	if p.Pair != nil {
		pair = *p.Pair
	}
	target := EffectiveTarget(pair, home, s.away, p.Text)

	req := engine.Request{
		Text:     p.Text,
		Source:   source,
		Target:   target,
		Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
		Stream:   stream,
		MaxAlts:  p.MaxAlts,
		Preset:   firstNonEmpty(p.Preset, s.res.Preset),
		Extra:    firstNonEmpty(p.Instructions, s.res.Instructions),
		Pair:     pair,
		PairHome: home,
		PairAway: s.away,
	}
	if p.Model != "" {
		// Scope the override to the resolved provider so a copilot model id never
		// leaks into an Ollama fallback (see engine.Request.ModelProvider).
		req.Model = p.Model
		req.ModelProvider = s.provider
	}
	return req
}

// Define performs a dictionary/definition lookup (not recorded in history, matching
// the CLI's `translate define`).
func (s *Service) Define(ctx context.Context, word string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	ch, err := s.define.Translate(ctx, engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return engine.Drain(ch, nil)
}

// HistoryRecent returns up to limit history records, newest first (empty when
// history is disabled).
func (s *Service) HistoryRecent(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]store.Record, error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, nil
	}
	return s.store.Recent(ctx, limit)
}

// HistorySearch fuzzy-searches history (empty when history is disabled).
func (s *Service) HistorySearch(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]store.Record, error) {
	if s.store == nil {
		return nil, nil
	}
	return s.store.Search(ctx, query, limit)
}

// Close releases the history store.
func (s *Service) Close() error {
	if s.store != nil {
		return s.store.Close()
	}
	return nil
}

// record persists a successful, complete result. Truncated results (a stream cut
// short) are never recorded, so a partial translation is never cached as complete.
func (s *Service) record(ctx context.Context, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) {
	if s.store == nil || res == nil || res.Truncated {
		return
	}
	_, _ = s.store.Add(ctx, ToRecord(res, input, source, target))
}

// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
// suppressed. Unlike the CLI helper it stays silent on failure (the server logs
// separately); a nil store simply disables history for this process.
func openStore(cfg *config.Config, noHistory bool) store.Store {
	if !cfg.History.Enabled || noHistory {
		return nil
	}
	path := cfg.History.Path
	if path == "" {
		path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
	}
	st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
	if err != nil {
		return nil
	}
	return st
}

func firstNonEmpty(a, b string) string {
	if a != "" {
		return a
	}
	return b
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:42:38Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```go
package cmd

import (
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/appcore"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
)

// buildEngineSet builds the list of engines the TUI can cycle through with ^e:
// the resolved primary (translate), plus Google (fast, keyless), the dictionary
// (word lookup), and — when an LLM provider is available — the smart dictionary
// (dictionary with an LLM fallback), so the user can switch on the fly.
//
// The engine constructors themselves live in internal/appcore (shared with the CLI
// one-shot path and the serve/mcp servers); this wrapper stays in cmd because it
// depends on internal/tui, which appcore must not import.
func buildEngineSet(res config.Resolved, primary engine.Engine) []tui.NamedEngine {
	cfg := res.Cfg
	primaryName := primary.Name() // "auto", "smart-auto", "copilot", "google", …
	if primaryName == "" {
		primaryName = "auto"
	}
	set := []tui.NamedEngine{{Name: primaryName, Engine: primary, Mode: engine.ModeTranslate}}
	if primaryName != "google" && cfg.Google.Enabled {
		set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "google", Engine: appcore.GoogleFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeTranslate})
	}
	if cfg.Dict.Enabled {
		set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "dictionary", Engine: appcore.DictFromConfig(cfg), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
		if res.Provider != nil {
			set = append(set, tui.NamedEngine{Name: "smart-dict", Engine: appcore.SmartDictFromConfig(res), Mode: engine.ModeDict})
		}
	}
	return set
}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 10:42:44Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>

 `./internal/lang/detect.go`

```text
90	// language, my language → foreign" both work from one input box).
91	//
92	// When exactly one side of the pair is a CJK language, routing is decided by
93	// which script dominates the text — reliable and offline, and symmetric
94	// regardless of which side is "home". Dominance (not mere presence) is what lets
95	// short Latin input like "test" route to the CJK side, while a few CJK proper
96	// nouns inside a long Latin passage stay routed to the CJK side too (rather than
97	// flipping the whole thing to Latin). Same-script pairs (e.g. en⇄es) fall back to
98	// best-effort trigram detection.
99	func PairTarget(home, away, text string) string {
100		if away == "" || away == home {
101			return home
102		}
103		homeCJK, awayCJK := isCJKLang(home), isCJKLang(away)
104		if homeCJK != awayCJK {
105			cjk, latin := home, away
106			if awayCJK {
107				cjk, latin = away, home
108			}
109			if cjkDominant(text) {
110				return latin // predominantly CJK → the non-CJK language
111			}
112			return cjk // predominantly non-CJK → the CJK language
113		}
114		// Same-script pair (both or neither CJK): best-effort, symmetric detection.
115		inHome, inAway := inLang(text, home), inLang(text, away)
116		if inHome && !inAway {
117			return away
118		}
119		if inAway && !inHome {
120			return home
121		}
122		return home // inconclusive → default to home
123	}
124	
125	// inLang reports whether text is (best-effort) in the language of code. Chinese
126	// uses a reliable Han-script check; others fall back to offline detection.
127	func inLang(text, code string) bool {
128		base := Base(code)
129		switch base {
130		case "", "auto":
131			return false
132		case "zh":
133			return IsChinese(text)
134		default:
```
