<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package cmd

import (
	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/appcore"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/mcpserver"
)

func newMcpCmd() *cobra.Command {
	var noHistory bool
	c := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "mcp",
		Short: "Run an MCP server over stdio (for Claude Desktop/Code, Cursor, …)",
		Long: "mcp runs a Model Context Protocol server over stdin/stdout, exposing the\n" +
			"translate, define, and history tools to any MCP host that spawns this binary.\n" +
			"There is no port or token — the transport is a private stdio pipe.\n\n" +
			"Register it in an MCP host with:\n" +
			"  { \"command\": \"translate\", \"args\": [\"mcp\"] }\n\n" +
			"stdout carries JSON-RPC; do not pipe it anywhere but the host.",
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
			cfg, _, err := config.Load()
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			svc, err := appcore.NewService(cfg, appcore.Options{NoHistory: noHistory})
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			defer svc.Close()
			return mcpserver.Serve(cmd.Context(), svc, shortVersion())
		},
	}
	c.Flags().BoolVar(&noHistory, "no-history", false, "do not read or write history")
	return c
}

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 11:02:20Z)**_

```go
package mcpserver

import (
	"context"
	"sort"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/appcore"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
)

// fakeService implements Service with canned data and captured inputs.
type fakeService struct {
	result     *engine.TranslateResult
	defineRes  *engine.TranslateResult
	recent     []store.Record
	lastParams appcore.Params
	lastSearch string
}

func (f *fakeService) Translate(_ context.Context, p appcore.Params) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	f.lastParams = p
	return f.result, nil
}
func (f *fakeService) Define(_ context.Context, _ string) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	return f.defineRes, nil
}
func (f *fakeService) HistoryRecent(_ context.Context, _ int) ([]store.Record, error) {
	return f.recent, nil
}
func (f *fakeService) HistorySearch(_ context.Context, q string, _ int) ([]store.Record, error) {
	f.lastSearch = q
	return f.recent, nil
}

// connect starts the MCP server over an in-memory transport and returns a
// connected client session. Both are torn down when the test ends.
func connect(t *testing.T, svc Service) *mcp.ClientSession {
	t.Helper()
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	t.Cleanup(cancel)

	clientT, serverT := mcp.NewInMemoryTransports()
	srv := newServer(svc, "test")
	go func() { _ = srv.Run(ctx, serverT) }()

	client := mcp.NewClient(&mcp.Implementation{Name: "test-client", Version: "0"}, nil)
	session, err := client.Connect(ctx, clientT, nil)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("client connect: %v", err)
	}
	t.Cleanup(func() { _ = session.Close() })
	return session
}

func firstText(t *testing.T, res *mcp.CallToolResult) string {
	t.Helper()
	if len(res.Content) == 0 {
		t.Fatal("result has no content")
	}
	tc, ok := res.Content[0].(*mcp.TextContent)
	if !ok {
		t.Fatalf("content[0] is %T, want *mcp.TextContent", res.Content[0])
	}
	return tc.Text
}

func TestMCPToolsList(t *testing.T) {
	session := connect(t, &fakeService{})
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
	defer cancel()

	res, err := session.ListTools(ctx, nil)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("ListTools: %v", err)
	}
	var names []string
	for _, tool := range res.Tools {
		names = append(names, tool.Name)
	}
	sort.Strings(names)
	want := []string{"define", "history", "translate"}
	if len(names) != len(want) {
		t.Fatalf("tools = %v, want %v", names, want)
	}
	for i := range want {
		if names[i] != want[i] {
			t.Fatalf("tools = %v, want %v", names, want)
		}
	}
}

func TestMCPCallTranslate(t *testing.T) {
	fake := &fakeService{result: &engine.TranslateResult{Translation: "你好", Target: "zh", Engine: "stub"}}
	session := connect(t, fake)
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
	defer cancel()

	res, err := session.CallTool(ctx, &mcp.CallToolParams{
		Name:      "translate",
		Arguments: map[string]any{"text": "hello", "target": "zh"},
	})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("CallTool: %v", err)
	}
	if res.IsError {
		t.Fatalf("unexpected tool error: %s", firstText(t, res))
	}
	if got := firstText(t, res); got != "你好" {
		t.Fatalf("text content = %q, want 你好", got)
	}
	if fake.lastParams.Text != "hello" || fake.lastParams.Target != "zh" {
		t.Fatalf("params = %+v, want text=hello target=zh", fake.lastParams)
	}
	// Structured output carries the full result.
	sc, ok := res.StructuredContent.(map[string]any)
	if !ok {
		t.Fatalf("structuredContent is %T, want object", res.StructuredContent)
	}
	if sc["translation"] != "你好" {
		t.Fatalf("structuredContent.translation = %v, want 你好", sc["translation"])
	}
}

func TestMCPCallDefine(t *testing.T) {
	fake := &fakeService{defineRes: &engine.TranslateResult{
		Dictionary: &engine.DictEntry{Word: "hello", Phonetic: "/həˈloʊ/"},
	}}
	session := connect(t, fake)
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
	defer cancel()

	res, err := session.CallTool(ctx, &mcp.CallToolParams{
		Name:      "define",
		Arguments: map[string]any{"word": "hello"},
	})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("CallTool: %v", err)
	}
	if got := firstText(t, res); got == "" || !contains(got, "hello") {
		t.Fatalf("define text = %q, want it to mention hello", got)
	}
}

func TestMCPCallHistorySearch(t *testing.T) {
	fake := &fakeService{recent: []store.Record{{Input: "hi", Output: "你好", SourceLang: "en", TargetLang: "zh"}}}
	session := connect(t, fake)
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
	defer cancel()

	res, err := session.CallTool(ctx, &mcp.CallToolParams{
		Name:      "history",
		Arguments: map[string]any{"query": "hi", "limit": 5},
	})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("CallTool: %v", err)
	}
	if fake.lastSearch != "hi" {
		t.Fatalf("search query = %q, want hi", fake.lastSearch)
	}
	if got := firstText(t, res); !contains(got, "你好") {
		t.Fatalf("history text = %q, want it to include 你好", got)
	}
}

func contains(s, sub string) bool {
	return len(s) >= len(sub) && (s == sub || indexOf(s, sub) >= 0)
}

func indexOf(s, sub string) int {
	for i := 0; i+len(sub) <= len(s); i++ {
		if s[i:i+len(sub)] == sub {
			return i
		}
	}
	return -1
}

```

rm -f /tmp/tmcp /tmp/tmcp-cfg.toml

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The stdio transport might be closing the pipe before the server finishes processing and writing responses, or the responses are getting buffered and lost when the input stream reaches EOF. The in-memory transport works fine, so the issue is specific to how the stdio transport handles the read-write cycle when stdin closes immediately after the messages are sent.

I should try keeping stdin open longer by adding a sleep after the printf — that way the server has time to write its responses before the pipe closes and the process exits.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 11:05:17Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I see the issue now — the config upgrade only happens in the root command path through `runRoot`, but `translate lang resolve` is a subcommand that bypasses that logic entirely. Subcommands call `config.Load` directly without triggering the auto-upgrade mechanism, which is why the schema stayed at version 1 and no server table was created.

To properly test this, I need to invoke the root command instead of the subcommand — something like piping input through the main translate command with an engine flag. The upgrade happens early in `runRoot` before the engine is even built, so the config should get re-saved even if translation fails afterward. I could use a bogus provider to fail fast and avoid network hangs, since the re-save happens before the engine building step.

But actually, this might not be a real issue — the on-disk upgrade only happening via the root command is the existing behavior, not something my change introduced. The schema v1 was already designed to upgrade this way. My `[server]` addition is just additive, and `config.Load()` fills in defaults in memory anyway. The key question is whether `translate serve` works with an old schema-1 config even without the on-disk upgrade, and it should, since `Load()` starts from `Default()` and fills in the missing fields.

Let me test this directly: run `translate serve` with a schema-1 config (no `[server]` section) and verify it works because the in-memory defaults kick in. I should also confirm that running the root command does bump the schema to v2.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 11:05:17Z)**_

The upgrade re-save only runs on the root command path (existing design), not subcommands like `lang`. What actually matters is that `serve` works with an old schema-1 config — since `Load()` fills `Server` defaults in memory. Let me verify both:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 11:05:23Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui
go build -o /tmp/tup . 2>&1
export TRANSLATE_CONFIG=/tmp/tup-cfg.toml

echo "### A. serve works with an OLD schema-1 config (no [server] table) ###"
printf 'schema = 1\n\n[general]\ndefault_target = "en"\nengine = "auto"\n' > "$TRANSLATE_CONFIG"
/tmp/tup serve --port 4166 --no-history >/tmp/tup.log 2>&1 &
SRV=$!; sleep 2
curl -s -w " [%{http_code}]\n" http://127.0.0.1:4166/healthz | tr -d '\n'; echo
kill $SRV 2>/dev/null

echo "### B. root command triggers schema v1->v2 upgrade + materializes [server] ###"
printf 'schema = 1\n\n[general]\ndefault_target = "en"\nengine = "google"\n' > "$TRANSLATE_CONFIG"
printf 'x' | timeout 6 /tmp/tup --engine google >/dev/null 2>/tmp/tup.err || true
grep -i "schema" /tmp/tup.err | head -1
echo "after upgrade:" && grep -E '^schema|^\[server\]|^port|^bind' "$TRANSLATE_CONFIG"
rm -f /tmp/tup /tmp/tup-cfg.toml /tmp/tup.err /tmp/tup.log
```

```text
### A. serve works with an OLD schema-1 config (no [server] table) ###
{  "status": "ok"} [200]
### B. root command triggers schema v1->v2 upgrade + materializes [server] ###
translate: upgraded config schema v1→v2 (new settings use defaults; `translate init` to review)
after upgrade:
schema = 2
[server]
port = 4155
bind = '127.0.0.1'
```
</details></tool-use>

32	- [ ] **[S] Automate the Homebrew formula bump on release** — the tap formula (`daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap` `Formula/translate.rb`) is currently hand-bumped (url + sha256) per tag. Add an on-tag GitHub Action (e.g. `dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula`, needs a cross-repo PAT) so a new `vX.Y.Z` auto-updates the formula. Keeps build-from-source (no goreleaser/cask). See [backlog/homebrew-distribution.md](backlog/homebrew-distribution.md).
33	- [ ] **[M] Table/wrapped-prose-aware bilingual mode** — the `--bilingual`/`-2` reading view interleaves a translation per blank-line-delimited prose block, which breaks on tabular output (`ls -l`, `kubectl get`, `git status`) and on hard-wrapped prose split by blank lines. Add column/table detection (skip or column-align) and soft-wrap coalescing; consider per-span parsing via the `charmbracelet/x/ansi` tokenizer already imported. See [backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md](backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md).
34	- [ ] **[M] lingua-go detection upgrade behind the `Detector` seam** — whatlanggo is light/fast but weak on short and mixed-script text; lingua-go is more accurate but heavy (embedded n-gram models → bigger binary, slower init). Swap behind the existing `internal/lang` interface only if short-text detection proves insufficient. Detection is mostly a fallback (Google returns the source, LLM returns `DetectedSource`), so keeping it light is defensible.
35	- [ ] **[S] MyMemory fallback engine** — trivial flat JSON (`responseData.translatedText`, `matches[]`); wire as an easy secondary free path after Google. Limits: 5k chars/day anon (50k with `&de=email`), 500 B max per `q`, no source auto-detect.
36	- [ ] **[M] Azure Translator engine behind the engine seam** — the only supported Microsoft path (needs an Azure subscription key). Keyless Bing scraping (`ttranslatev3` + transient `IG`/`IID`/token) is too fragile/ToS-risky for v1. Leave the engine interface ready; add when a key is available.
37	- [ ] **[S] Drop the whole-request `http.Client{Timeout: 60s}` on the streaming path** — in `internal/engine/llm.go`, `NewLLM` sets a client-wide `Timeout` that Go applies to the *entire* request including reading the streamed body, so a long translation is cut at 60s mid-stream. Now surfaced as `⚠ truncated` (see [pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md](pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md)) instead of silently accepted, but the long output still can't finish. Fix by relying on `ctx` deadlines for the streaming `Do()` (keep the short timeout only for `Available`/`Models` probes).
38

Also unlocks shipping the dictionary DB as a release asset. → [research](backlog/release-binaries.md)
44	- [ ] **[?/M] Bundle or prebuild the dictionary vs the 67 MB runtime `dict update`** — evaluate embedding a trimmed DB via `go:embed`, or shipping the built ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT as release assets, so first run isn't a big download/build. Weigh binary-size blowup vs first-run friction. → [research](backlog/dict-bundling.md)
45	- [ ] **[?/L] Publish the Raycast extension to the store** — the local-dev extension (`raycast/extension`) works via `npm run dev`; publishing publicly hits the "avoid requiring manual installs" review guideline (it depends on the `translate` binary). Evaluate private org store vs public, icon/screenshots/CHANGELOG, and graceful binary-not-found onboarding. → [research](backlog/raycast-extension.md)
46	- [ ] **[?/L] Local HTTP API server (`translate serve`) + OpenAPI/Swagger** — a persistent loopback service (`POST /v1/translate`, `/v1/define`, `GET /v1/history`, SSE stream) would give the Raycast extension (and other clients: Alfred, browser, editors) cleaner streaming, multi-client reuse, and no launchd-PATH resolution. Trade-off: daemon lifecycle + loopback security + concurrent history writes; latency win is small (LLM-dominated). Reuses `internal/engine`. → [research](backlog/api-server.md)
47	
48	## Done
49	
50	- ✅ [2026-07-22] [P3/S] `--stream` flag to force token streaming on non-TTY — piped consumers (`foo | translate`, the Raycast extension) can now force streaming; `cmd/root.go` ORs `--stream` into the TTY gate. Visible progressive output still depends on the provider (copilot-proxy buffers its claude `/v1/messages` responses; ollama streams). Part of the Raycast integration ([backlog/raycast-extension.md](backlog/raycast-extension.md), [docs/raycast-extension.md](docs/raycast-extension.md)).
51	- ✅ [2026-07-22] [—/S] TUI result pane: collapse excess blank lines between paragraphs — multi-paragraph translations (esp. terminal-scrape input) showed 2–4 blank rows per paragraph break while `^y` copy stayed clean. Root cause was `lipgloss.Style.Render` block-padding a multi-line block's blank lines out to the widest line's width, which the `SoftWrap` viewport then re-wrapped into several blank rows. Fixed by styling per line (`styles.renderTranslation`) + Unicode-aware blank-run collapsing (`collapseBlankLines`, handles `U+3000`/NBSP/CRLF). End-to-end guard drives the real Model with long wrapping paragraphs. See [pitfalls/tui-lipgloss-block-padding-inflates-blank-lines-in-viewport.md](pitfalls/tui-lipgloss-block-padding-inflates-blank-lines-in-viewport.md).
52	- ✅ [2026-07-22] [P2/S] Align `just install` with `~/.local/bin` — the `install` recipe now installs to `~/.local/bin` (first on PATH, where `go install` lands), with a `DIR=` override, instead of `~/.dotfiles/bin`; both paths agree so a stale copy can't shadow. See [pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md](pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md).
53	- ✅ [2026-07-20] [P3/M] Homebrew tap distribution — `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate` is live (build-from-source personal tap). Landed the two prereqs in v0.3.1 (injectable `var version` in `cmd/version.go` + MIT `LICENSE`) and published `Formula/translate.rb` to `daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap`. Verified install/`brew test` on macos_intel; no Gatekeeper/quarantine (source build). → [backlog/homebrew-distribution.md](backlog/homebrew-distribution.md).
54	- ✅ [2026-07-20] [P2/M] Bilingual `--bilingual`/`-2` immersive pipe mode + ANSI-strip-on-input — piped colored input is now stripped (`bitext.Strip`) before reaching the LLM; `--bilingual` keeps each original block (color intact) and prints the translation beneath prose blocks, dimmed on a TTY, echoing indented command/code blocks untranslated. New pure `internal/bitext` pkg (Split/Render). Per-word recoloring (approaches A/B) rejected as over-engineering. → [backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md](backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md).
55	- ✅ [2026-07-09] [P?/L] Wire `translate` into chezmoi dotfiles as an auto-installed go tool — go_tools ansible role added to dotfiles (commit 306bfb0): go install → ~/.local/bin, mise-gated, + cat_go upgrade path. Pending chezmoi apply on normal cadence.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 11:07:28Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
158	## Dictionary mode
159	
160	`^e` cycles to the dictionary engine (or use `translate define <word>`). It is an
161	**offline bilingual** dictionary that routes by script — Chinese → CC-CEDICT
162	(zh→en), English → ECDICT (en→zh):
163	
164	```sh
165	translate dict update all      # one-time ~67 MB download/build into ~/.local/share/translate/dict
166	```
167	
168	Until then, English lookups fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (`[dict] api_fallback`),
169	and Chinese lookups prompt you to run the update. Misses show a ranked "did you
170	mean" list. Set `[dict] source = "api"` to use only dictionaryapi.dev.
171	
172	### Smart dictionary (`smart-dict`)
173	
174	A distinct engine that keeps the offline dictionary fast and deterministic, but on
175	a miss — no entry, or a fuzzy match too far off — falls back to the LLM for a gloss
176	plus example sentences (never silently: the result carries a `⚠ … defined via <provider> (LLM)`
177	warning). Close typos (edit distance ≤ `[smartdict] close_distance`, default 1) still
178	show "did you mean" without calling the LLM.
179	
180	```sh
181	translate define serendipity        # exact ECDICT entry
182	translate define zzzznotaword       # miss → LLM definition (⚠ warning on stderr)
183	translate define helllo             # distance-1 typo → "did you mean: hello, …"
184	translate define --plain <word>     # force the offline dictionary, no LLM fallback
185	```
186	
187	`translate define` uses smart-dict whenever an LLM provider is reachable
188	(`[smartdict] define_default`, `--smart`/`--plain` to force); in the TUI, `^e`
189	cycles to a `smart-dict` engine alongside the plain `dictionary`.
190	
191	```toml
192	[smartdict]
193	close_distance = 1            # en edit-distance ≤ this stays "did you mean"; beyond → LLM
194	preset         = "dictionary" # LLM output style for the fallback
195	define_default = true         # `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
196	```
197	
198	Data: **CC-CEDICT** (CC BY-SA 4.0, © Paul Andrew Denisowski / MDBG) and
199	**ECDICT** (MIT, © skywind3000).
200	
201	## Developer note — Charm v2 (`charm.land/*`)
202	
203	This project pins the **v2** Charm stack on the `charm.land/*` vanity module paths
204	(`charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `bubbles/v2`, `lipgloss/v2`, `huh/v2`) — **not**
205	`github.com/charmbracelet/*`. v1 code will not compile here. When editing the TUI:
206	`View()` returns `tea.View` (wrap strings with `tea.NewView`); keys arrive as
207	`tea.KeyPressMsg`; alt-screen is the declarative `View.AltScreen` field; window
208	size is requested via `tea.RequestWindowSize`.
209	
210	## Architecture
211	
212	The `internal/engine` and `internal/store` layers are pure Go (no TUI imports);
213	the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical engine values and
214	diverge only at presentation. A single monotonic `seq` counter in the TUI drives
215	debounce-collapse, in-flight cancellation, and stale-result dropping.
216	
217	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:readme-roadmap -->
218	<!-- Snippet for project's README.md, placed near other meta sections like
219	     "Customization" or "Contributing". -->
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 11:08:42Z)**_

All gates pass and both commands are live. Everything is implemented, tested, and documented. Here's the summary.

## What shipped

Starting from `backlog/api-server.md`, I designed *and* built the full thing across five verified phases. Both servers are thin adapters over one new shared core.

**New shared core — `internal/appcore`** (Phase 0)
- Lifted the engine builders out of package `cmd` into `appcore` (resolving the import-cycle that blocked any server package), and added `appcore.Service`: a warm engine + history store + pair-routing glue with `Translate` / `TranslateStream` / `Define` / `HistoryRecent` / `HistorySearch`. The CLI now drives this same core — byte-identical behavior, confirmed.

**`translate serve`** (Phases 1–3) — `internal/server`
- Stdlib `net/http` (Go 1.22 method routing, no framework): `POST /v1/translate`, `POST /v1/define`, `GET /v1/history`, SSE `GET /v1/translate/stream`, `GET /healthz`.
- Responses reuse `engine.TranslateResult` verbatim; JSON error envelope; graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown.
- **Security**: loopback-only bind (fail-closed unless a token is set), bearer-token guard on `/v1/history` (`crypto/subtle`), new `[server]` config table (schema bumped v1→v2, auto-upgrades existing configs).
- SSE threads `r.Context()` end-to-end (client disconnect aborts the upstream LLM call) and surfaces truncation as an explicit `warning` event.
- **OpenAPI 3.1 + Swagger UI at `/docs`**, fully offline (vendored `swagger-ui-dist` via `go:embed`); a reflection test guards schema↔struct drift.
- Hardened history appends with `flock` for concurrent server+CLI writes.

**`translate mcp`** (Phase 4) — `internal/mcpserver`
- MCP over **stdio** via the official Go SDK **v1.6.1** (your two choices), exposing `translate` / `define` / `history` tools with typed structured output inferred from the Go structs. No port, no token.

## Verification
- `just check` (gofmt/vet/build) and `just test` fully green; new unit tests for `appcore.Service`, all HTTP handlers, SSE (incl. a real client-cancellation test), the OpenAPI drift guard, and the MCP handshake.
- Real end-to-end smoke tests: `serve` (health, history, 422/400/404, loopback refusal, `/docs` + embedded assets, SSE `text/event-stream`); `mcp` (real stdio `initialize` → `tools/list` returning all three tools with schemas, clean stdout); and the config v1→v2 auto-upgrade.

## Notes
- One new dependency (`github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk`); `go.mod`/`go.sum` tidied. Binary grows ~1.6 MB from the embedded Swagger UI (accepted per the plan; gateable behind a build tag later if needed).
- Docs updated: README (`serve` + `mcp` usage, architecture note), `backlog/api-server.md` (marked shipped with outcome), `TODO.md` (moved to Done).
- The Raycast extension is untouched — it stays spawn-based; wiring it to the server is a separate opt-in follow-up.
- I have **not committed** anything — the tree is staged-free and ready for you to review. Want me to commit this (I'd branch off `main` first) or open a PR?

Two small things I noticed but left alone as out-of-scope: `TODO.md` has a pre-existing lint violation on the `[—/S]` Done entry (the `promote-todo.sh` validator already fails on it before my change), and per-request provider/model switching in the API is limited to what the warm engine honors (documented in the plan as a future add).

---

Now I need to modify root.go's AddCommand to call newServeCmd instead of both commands, then rewind server.go back to Phase 1 by removing the stream route and registerDocs call, and simplify server_test.go accordingly. I'm staging all the config, store, server, and cmd files for this commit. But I'm realizing Phase 1's server_test.go still needs the fakeService to implement TranslateStream since it's part of the Service interface—I need to figure out how to handle that. The streamTok field was already part of the Phase 1 fakeService struct, so removing the streamFn branch in Phase 1 just means stripping out that conditional logic while keeping the core structure intact. For Phase 2, I'm adding the streamFn field back and restoring the branch that uses it in the TranslateStream method, then adding the stream route to the server.

For Phase 3, I'm introducing the OpenAPI documentation layer by adding registerDocs and updating the log line in server.go, which requires staging the OpenAPI schema files and test files. Phase 4 brings in the MCP server integration by adding the newMcpCmd to the root command and wiring up the MCP-related modules.

For Phase 6, I'm documenting everything by staging the README, TODO, and backlog files. I notice there are some pre-existing modified files from earlier in the session—the specstory transcripts and plans—but those are unrelated to this work, so I'll leave them unstaged rather than including them in these commits.

I also need to verify that the `translate` binary created by the build is properly gitignored so I don't accidentally stage a large binary file.

For the Go dependencies, `go.mod` and `go.sum` were modified when I added the MCP SDK, which belongs in Phase 4 since that's when the SDK is first used. The earlier phases won't import the SDK, so keeping it out of their commits keeps them consistent and buildable on their own.

My working tree already has the SDK in `go.mod`, but I won't stage those files until Phase 4 — the Phase 1 commit will use the current HEAD's `go.mod` without the SDK, which is fine. After all commits are done, the working tree should be clean.

I need to be careful with rewinding and forwarding `server.go` and `server_test.go` across phases to avoid conflicts or losing changes. I think the rewind/forward approach is necessary to keep phases isolated, even though it means carefully managing edits to shared files like server.go and server_test.go. Let me define exactly what Phase 1's state of these files should be, starting with the newMux function and its route handlers.

Writing Phase 1 code... Adding the streaming endpoint and docs registration...

I need to track the phases: Phase 1 is the baseline without streaming or docs, Phase 2 adds the stream endpoint, and Phase 3 adds the docs registration and updates the log message. To revert to Phase 1, I'd remove both the stream line and the registerDocs call, then adjust the log output back to just mentioning the health check endpoint.

For the test file, I'm updating the fakeService struct to remove the streamFn field and instead add a streamTok slice that the TranslateStream method iterates through, calling the onToken callback for each token before returning the result.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 12:14:18Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui
git commit -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(serve): translate serve — loopback HTTP JSON API

Add `translate serve`, a persistent loopback HTTP service over appcore.Service
(stdlib net/http, Go 1.22 method routing — no framework):

- POST /v1/translate, POST /v1/define, GET /v1/history, GET /healthz.
  Responses reuse engine.TranslateResult / store.Record verbatim; uniform
  {"error":{code,message}} envelope (400/401/422/500).
- Security: binds 127.0.0.1 only (a non-loopback bind is refused unless a
  token is set); /v1/history requires a bearer token when one is configured
  (crypto/subtle compare). New [server] config table (port 4155, bind, token,
  token_env); schema bumped v1→v2 (additive auto-upgrade).
- Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM (http.Server.Shutdown).
- Harden history append with flock so a running server and a concurrent CLI
  can't interleave writes (unix; no-op elsewhere).

Handlers decouple from the concrete Service via a small interface, tested
with a fake + httptest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git log --oneline -1
```

```text
0c461c5 feat(serve): translate serve — loopback HTTP JSON API
```
</details></tool-use>

feat(serve): SSE streaming endpoint GET /v1/translate/stream

Stream a translation as Server-Sent Events (GET + query params, since browser
EventSource is GET-only). Events: token (JSON-encoded), warning, done (the
final TranslateResult), error — all JSON payloads so multi-line tokens survive
SSE line framing.

- Reuses appcore.Service.TranslateStream + http.Flusher.
- r.Context() is threaded to the engine, so a client disconnect aborts the
  upstream LLM request (covered by a real cancellation test).
- Truncated is transient (json:"-"), so it's surfaced as an explicit `warning`
  event before `done`.

Note: visible token cadence still depends on the provider — copilot-proxy
buffers Claude /v1/messages regardless of transport.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git log --oneline -1

feat(mcp): translate mcp — MCP server over stdio

Add `translate mcp`, exposing the engine as MCP tools to any host that spawns
the binary (Claude Desktop/Code, Cursor, …) — no port, no token; the transport
is a private stdio pipe and history never crosses a network boundary.

- Built on the official Go MCP SDK (github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk
  v1.6.1). Tools: translate, define, history — each returns readable text plus
  typed structured output inferred from the Go structs.
- Thin adapter over appcore.Service (same core as the CLI and HTTP server).
- stdout is the JSON-RPC channel; diagnostics go to stderr.
- Tested via the SDK's in-memory transport (initialize → tools/list →
  tools/call).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-22 12:16:30Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-translate-cli-tui
WT=/tmp/tr-verify-wt
git worktree add -q --detach "$WT" 2>&1
for c in $(git rev-list --reverse main..HEAD); do
  git -C "$WT" checkout -q "$c"
  # build tags: build ignores _test.go for `go build`; use `go vet` to also compile tests
  if (cd "$WT" && go build ./... >/tmp/b.log 2>&1); then B=ok; else B=FAIL; fi
  printf "%s  build:%-5s  %s\n" "$(git log -1 --format=%h "$c")" "$B" "$(git log -1 --format=%s "$c")"
  [ "$B" = FAIL ] && cat /tmp/b.log
done
git worktree remove --force "$WT" 2>&1
echo "worktree cleaned"
```
